usage.name: Abort cycle safely
usage.statement: Stop a running cycle and place the system in a safe state during an abnormal event.
usage.description: This matters when a fault, leak, or emergency requires halting the process without hazard.

usage.name: Adjust cycle parameters
usage.statement: Modify permitted process setpoints while a cleaning or sterilization cycle is actively running.
usage.description: Operator changes flow rate, dwell time, temperature, or other permitted variables mid-cycle to correct deviations or accommodate process conditions.

usage.name: Advance cycle phase
usage.statement: Manually advance the active cycle from one programmed phase to the next.
usage.description: Operator intervenes to progress the cycle when automatic phase-transition criteria cannot be met or when a non-standard sequence is required.

usage.name: Circulate cleaning solution
usage.statement: Circulate detergent and rinse solutions through the process circuit during a cycle.
usage.description: This matters because effective soil removal depends on sustained fluid flow across all wetted surfaces.

usage.name: Control cycle temperature
usage.statement: Maintain the solution temperature within the setpoint range throughout the cycle.
usage.description: This matters because temperature directly affects the chemical reaction rate and sterilization effectiveness.

usage.name: Document cycle results
usage.statement: Record cycle parameters and outcomes for each completed cleaning or sterilization event.
usage.description: Quality technician or operator captures time-stamped data — temperatures, flow rates, chemical concentrations, pass/fail status — for each cycle to support traceability and compliance.

usage.name: Dose cleaning chemicals
usage.statement: Deliver cleaning agents at target concentrations into the circulating solution during a cycle.
usage.description: System meters detergent, acid, alkali, or sanitizer into the circuit to achieve and hold programmed concentration setpoints.

usage.name: Initiate cleaning cycle
usage.statement: Start an in-place cleaning cycle on the target process circuit or vessel.
usage.description: Operator triggers a programmed CIP cycle to begin automated cleaning of connected process equipment.

usage.name: Manage return flow
usage.statement: Direct returning cycle media to the designated destination.
usage.description: This controls whether returning media follows the intended recirculation, recovery, treatment, or discharge path.

usage.name: Monitor cycle parameters
usage.statement: Track flow, temperature, conductivity, and pressure during an active cycle.
usage.description: This matters because real-time monitoring confirms the cleaning is proceeding within validated limits.

usage.name: Operate in manual mode
usage.statement: Run individual cleaning steps manually to handle a non-standard cleaning task.
usage.description: This matters during commissioning, testing, or unusual soiling that automated recipes do not cover.

usage.name: Pause active cycle
usage.statement: Pause the active cycle at an allowable operating point.
usage.description: This permits temporary intervention without immediately terminating the entire cycle.

usage.name: Recover reusable solutions
usage.statement: Capture and return spent detergent or rinse solutions for reuse in later cycles.
usage.description: This matters when solution recovery reduces chemical and water consumption per cleaning cycle.

usage.name: Respond to cycle alarms
usage.statement: Acknowledge and respond to alarms generated during an active cleaning or sterilization cycle.
usage.description: Operator or control room personnel assess alarm conditions, take corrective action, and clear alarms to allow the cycle to continue or abort safely.

usage.name: Resume interrupted cycle
usage.statement: Resume an interrupted cycle from an approved operating state.
usage.description: This allows operation to continue after the interruption has been assessed and cleared.

usage.name: Route cycle media
usage.statement: Direct cleaning or sterilization media through the selected in-place circuit via valve sequencing.
usage.description: Operator or control system positions valves and selects flow paths to route the active cycle medium through the intended process circuit.

usage.name: Select cleaning program
usage.statement: Select the appropriate cleaning or sterilization program for the target equipment before starting a cycle.
usage.description: Operator chooses from pre-validated programs stored in the system to match the soil type, product residue, and equipment to be cleaned.

usage.name: Sequence multiple circuits
usage.statement: Coordinate cleaning across multiple tanks, lines, or vessels in one operation.
usage.description: This matters when a shared skid must service several process circuits without cross-contamination.

usage.name: Sterilize equipment surfaces
usage.statement: Apply steam or heated solution to sterilize the process equipment in place.
usage.description: This matters when equipment must be rendered free of microbial contamination before production resumes.

usage.name: Troubleshoot cycle deviations
usage.statement: Diagnose and resolve alarms or out-of-spec conditions during a cycle.
usage.description: This matters when a cleaning cycle fails to reach parameters and production is delayed.

usage.name: Verify rinse completion
usage.statement: Confirm that the final rinse meets acceptance criteria before releasing equipment for production use.
usage.description: Quality technician or operator checks conductivity, turbidity, or other indicators at cycle end to validate rinse water quality and authorise equipment release.

disposal.name: Archive compliance records
disposal.statement: Archive validation, maintenance, and compliance records from the system according to retention requirements.
disposal.description: Compile and archive all regulatory compliance, validation, and maintenance documentation associated with the system for the required retention period.

disposal.name: Cancel service contracts
disposal.statement: Cancel service and supply contracts associated with the system.
disposal.description: Service, chemical supply, and remote monitoring contracts tied to the system must be ended to avoid ongoing charges.

disposal.name: Decommission the system
disposal.statement: Decommission the cleaning and sterilization in place system at end of service life.
disposal.description: Formally retire the system from operational use following established decommissioning procedures.

disposal.name: Decontaminate product-contact surfaces
disposal.statement: Decontaminate product-contact surfaces of the system before removal or transfer.
disposal.description: Apply decontamination procedures to all product-contact surfaces to neutralize biological or chemical hazards prior to disassembly, removal, or transfer of the system.

disposal.name: Disconnect utility connections
disposal.statement: Disconnect the system from utility and process connections.
disposal.description: Piping and vessels must be safely disconnected from steam, water, drain, and process utility connections during removal.

disposal.name: Dispose of hazardous chemicals
disposal.statement: Dispose of hazardous cleaning and sanitizing chemicals from the system through approved channels.
disposal.description: Remove and dispose of residual hazardous chemical agents remaining in the system at end of life in compliance with applicable regulations.

disposal.name: Document final condition
disposal.statement: Document the final physical and operational condition of the system at end of service.
disposal.description: Record the system's physical condition, outstanding defects, remaining component life, and operational status at the point of retirement to support disposal, transfer, or replacement decisions.

disposal.name: Drain residual chemicals
disposal.statement: Drain residual cleaning and sanitizing chemicals from the system.
disposal.description: Residual caustic, acid, and sanitizer chemicals in tanks and lines must be drained and neutralized before the unit is removed.

disposal.name: Export cycle records
disposal.statement: Export historical cycle and validation records from the system.
disposal.description: Batch records, cleaning validation logs, and cycle data stored in the controller must be exported before shutdown for compliance and audit purposes.

disposal.name: Migrate system configurations to replacement equipment
disposal.statement: Migrate compatible system configurations and parameters to the replacement equipment.
disposal.description: Identify, extract, and transfer applicable control configurations, cleaning cycle parameters, and validated settings from the retiring system to the replacement system.

disposal.name: Notify regulatory bodies of system retirement
disposal.statement: Notify applicable regulatory bodies of the system's retirement from regulated service.
disposal.description: Submit required notifications or deregistration filings to applicable regulatory authorities when retiring a system that was registered, validated, or permitted for use in a regulated environment.

disposal.name: Notify stakeholders
disposal.statement: Notify stakeholders of the system's removal from service.
disposal.description: Downstream process lines, utilities, and monitoring teams must be informed that the cleaning system is being taken out of service.

disposal.name: Purge control system data
disposal.statement: Remove stored recipes, parameters, and configuration data from the control system before release.
disposal.description: Erase proprietary process recipes, cleaning parameters, and configuration data stored in the control system to prevent unauthorized access after disposal.

disposal.name: Recycle recoverable materials
disposal.statement: Recycle recoverable materials from the retired system through appropriate recycling channels.
disposal.description: Sort and route recyclable materials — including stainless steel, metal components, and other recoverable materials — from the decommissioned system to appropriate recycling streams.

disposal.name: Remove system identification markings
disposal.statement: Remove or obliterate system identification markings, asset tags, and nameplates before transfer or scrapping.
disposal.description: Strip, deface, or remove facility-specific asset tags, identification markings, calibration labels, and proprietary nameplates from the system prior to transfer to a new owner or scrapping, to prevent misuse of facility identification.

disposal.name: Salvage reusable components
disposal.statement: Salvage serviceable components from the retired system.
disposal.description: This retains pumps, valves, instruments, controls, or other eligible parts for approved reuse.

disposal.name: Transfer system ownership
disposal.statement: Transfer ownership of the system to a new owner or operator.
disposal.description: Execute the legal and logistical transfer of system ownership, including documentation and handover, to a new owner or operator.

disposal.name: Transition operations to replacement system
disposal.statement: Transition operational processes to the replacement system at the end of the retired system's service life.
disposal.description: Plan and execute the operational handover from the decommissioned system to its replacement, ensuring continuity of cleaning and sterilization operations.

acquisition.name: Arrange site assessment
acquisition.statement: Arrange a pre-purchase site assessment for installation feasibility.
acquisition.description: Site surveys confirm space, utilities, and access needed to install the system.

acquisition.name: Assess process compatibility
acquisition.statement: Assess compatibility of the system with existing process equipment and piping.
acquisition.description: Evaluates whether candidate systems can be integrated with the facility's existing process lines, vessels, and piping topology.

acquisition.name: Compare system specifications
acquisition.statement: Compare technical specifications and performance ratings across candidate systems.
acquisition.description: Side-by-side evaluation of cleaning coverage, cycle times, flow rates, temperature/pressure ratings, and other technical parameters across shortlisted systems.

acquisition.name: Complete purchase transaction
acquisition.statement: Complete the purchase contract and finalize commercial terms.
acquisition.description: The purchase contract, warranty terms, and delivery schedule must be finalized.

acquisition.name: Coordinate system delivery
acquisition.statement: Coordinate delivery and rigging of the system to the facility.
acquisition.description: Manages logistics, rigging contractors, and site access to bring the capital system from supplier to the installation site.

acquisition.name: Define system requirements
acquisition.statement: Define the required performance and operational capabilities for the cleaning and sterilization in place system.
acquisition.description: Establishes the technical, regulatory, and operational requirements that candidate systems must satisfy, forming the basis for evaluation and vendor engagement.

acquisition.name: Evaluate total cost of ownership
acquisition.statement: Evaluate the total cost of ownership including capital, utilities, consumables, and maintenance.
acquisition.description: Calculates and compares lifecycle financial impact of candidate systems to support acquisition decision-making.

acquisition.name: Navigate procurement approval
acquisition.statement: Navigate the internal procurement and capital approval process.
acquisition.description: Capital equipment purchases require documented approval through procurement and finance.

acquisition.name: Procure initial spare parts kit
acquisition.statement: Procure an initial spare parts and consumables kit alongside the system acquisition.
acquisition.description: Sources and purchases recommended spare parts, seals, and consumables bundled with or alongside the capital equipment purchase to ensure operational readiness.

acquisition.name: Qualify system supplier
acquisition.statement: Qualify the supplier of the cleaning and sterilization in place system.
acquisition.description: This assesses the supplier's technical capability, manufacturing controls, delivery record, service coverage, and parts support.

acquisition.name: Register warranty and service coverage
acquisition.statement: Register the system and activate warranty and service coverage with the supplier.
acquisition.description: Completes ownership registration and activates contractual warranty, service agreements, and support entitlements.

acquisition.name: Request quotation and proposal
acquisition.statement: Request a detailed quotation and engineering proposal from candidate system suppliers.
acquisition.description: Formally solicits pricing, lead times, scope of supply, and engineering documentation from vendors under consideration.

acquisition.name: Research system options
acquisition.statement: Research available cleaning and sterilization in place system configurations and technologies.
acquisition.description: Identifies and surveys the landscape of CIP/SIP system types, configurations, and enabling technologies prior to formal evaluation.

acquisition.name: Seek vendor references
acquisition.statement: Seek references and case studies from comparable installations.
acquisition.description: Facilities seek references and case studies from similar operations to validate vendor claims.

acquisition.name: Track system shipment
acquisition.statement: Track the cleaning and sterilization in place system during shipment.
acquisition.description: This supports receiving coordination when delivery timing, shipment status, or transport exceptions could affect site readiness.

acquisition.name: Verify documentation package
acquisition.statement: Verify receipt of required engineering drawings, documentation, and validation protocols.
acquisition.description: Confirms that all contractually required technical documents, certificates, and validation materials are included with the delivered system.

acquisition.name: Verify regulatory compliance
acquisition.statement: Verify that candidate systems meet applicable sanitary design and regulatory standards.
acquisition.description: Confirms that shortlisted systems satisfy relevant sanitary, safety, and industry regulatory requirements for the intended application.

preparation.name: Assemble system components
preparation.statement: Assemble the cleaning and sterilization in place system components per installation drawings.
preparation.description: Mechanically assemble all sub-assemblies, manifolds, vessels, and structural components of the system according to the manufacturer's installation drawings and procedures.

preparation.name: Calibrate process sensors
preparation.statement: Calibrate the temperature, flow, conductivity, and pressure sensors on the system.
preparation.description: Sensors for temperature, conductivity, flow, and pressure must be calibrated to ensure cycle accuracy and validation.

preparation.name: Charge chemical dosing reservoirs
preparation.statement: Fill and prime the chemical dosing reservoirs of the system prior to operation.
preparation.description: Load detergent, sanitiser, and other process chemicals into the system's dosing reservoirs and prime the delivery lines to ensure correct chemical availability before cycle execution.

preparation.name: Commission and validate installed system
preparation.statement: Commission and qualify the installed system against installation qualification and operational qualification protocols.
preparation.description: Execute formal IQ/OQ validation protocols to demonstrate that the installed system meets design intent, regulatory requirements, and operational specifications before release for production use.

preparation.name: Configure cleaning cycle recipes
preparation.statement: Configure the cleaning and sterilization cycle recipes for each process circuit.
preparation.description: Cleaning and sterilization recipes define flow rates, temperatures, concentrations, and cycle times for each target circuit.

preparation.name: Configure safety interlocks and alarm limits
preparation.statement: Configure the safety interlocks, alarm limits, and pressure-relief settings of the system.
preparation.description: Set up all safety-critical parameters — interlocks, high/low alarm thresholds, and relief device setpoints — required before the system can operate safely.

preparation.name: Configure user access permissions
preparation.statement: Configure user accounts and access permissions on the system controller.
preparation.description: Create operator and administrator accounts, assign role-based access permissions, and enforce authentication policies on the system controller before first operational use.

preparation.name: Connect chemical agent supply lines
preparation.statement: Connect the system to its designated cleaning and sterilisation agent supply sources.
preparation.description: Install and verify connections between the system's chemical dosing inlets and the bulk chemical supply containers, totes, or distribution headers.

preparation.name: Connect process piping
preparation.statement: Connect the system supply and return piping to the equipment being cleaned or sterilized.
preparation.description: Supply and return lines must be routed and connected so cleaning solutions can circulate through the target equipment.

preparation.name: Connect utilities and services
preparation.statement: Connect the system to the required process utilities including water, steam, drain, chemical supply, and electrical services.
preparation.description: Make all utility tie-ins — water supply and drain, steam or hot-water heating, chemical dosing supply lines, compressed air, and electrical power — required for system operation.

preparation.name: Document system as-built configuration
preparation.statement: Record the as-built configuration of the installed system.
preparation.description: Compile and file as-built drawings, instrument calibration records, wiring diagrams, valve tag lists, and configuration reports that reflect the system as actually installed and commissioned.

preparation.name: Establish preventive maintenance baseline
preparation.statement: Establish the preventive maintenance schedule and baseline service intervals for the system.
preparation.description: Define initial PM task lists, service intervals, and spare-parts stocking levels based on manufacturer recommendations and site operating conditions, and enter them into the site's maintenance management system before first operation.

preparation.name: Flush and passivate system
preparation.statement: Flush and passivate the system to remove installation residues before first use.
preparation.description: Before the first production cycle the entire system is flushed and passivated to remove installation residues.

preparation.name: Integrate with plant automation network
preparation.statement: Integrate the system controller with the plant automation and data historian network.
preparation.description: Configure network interfaces, communication protocols, and data tags to connect the system controller to existing plant automation, SCADA, or data historian infrastructure.

preparation.name: Map and verify circuits
preparation.statement: Map and verify each cleaning circuit for coverage and drainability before use.
preparation.description: A new circuit must be verified for coverage, drainability, and spray ball reach before it is added to the cleaning program.

preparation.name: Position and mount system modules
preparation.statement: Position and mount the cleaning and sterilization in place system modules in their designated locations.
preparation.description: Move, align, level, and mechanically anchor all system modules and skids to their designated installation footprint according to layout drawings.

preparation.name: Receive and inspect delivered system
preparation.statement: Receive the delivered system and reconcile components against the purchase order, specification, and shipping manifest.
preparation.description: Unpack and physically inspect all delivered system components and accessories for completeness, specification conformance, and shipping damage, reconciling findings against the purchase order and shipping manifest before installation begins.
preparation.userGroup: Receiving technician, project engineer, or installation team lead.

preparation.name: Train operators on system
preparation.statement: Train operators on running cycles, alarm response, and chemical handling for the system.
preparation.description: Operators must be trained on running cycles, responding to alarms, and safe handling of cleaning chemicals.

preparation.name: Unpack and stage system components
preparation.statement: Unpack the cleaning and sterilization in place system components and stage them for inspection and installation.
preparation.description: Remove all system components from shipping packaging, account for all items, and stage them in the installation area ready for inspection and assembly.

maintenance.name: Clean exterior surfaces
maintenance.statement: Clean the system's exterior surfaces, panels, and framework.
maintenance.description: Operator cleans external housing, panels, and structural framework to maintain hygiene and allow visual inspection.

maintenance.name: Clean product-contact surfaces
maintenance.statement: Clean the wetted surfaces of the CIP/SIP system between production runs.
maintenance.description: Operator flushes, rinses, and cleans internal wetted surfaces to remove process residues between campaigns.

maintenance.name: Descale heat exchangers
maintenance.statement: Descale the heat exchangers and heated fluid pathways to restore thermal performance.
maintenance.description: Technician removes mineral scale and fouling deposits from heat-transfer surfaces to maintain heating and cooling efficiency.

maintenance.name: Document maintenance history
maintenance.statement: Record all maintenance, repair, and calibration activities in the equipment log.
maintenance.description: A complete service record supports regulatory compliance, warranty, and revalidation efforts.

maintenance.name: Filter and treat water supply
maintenance.statement: Maintain and replace the water filtration and treatment components feeding the system.
maintenance.description: Poor-quality feed water fouls the system, so filters and water treatment must be maintained.

maintenance.name: Inspect electrical and control connections
maintenance.statement: Inspect electrical wiring, terminal connections, and control cabinet components for integrity.
maintenance.description: Technician checks electrical connections, cable routing, terminal tightness, and control cabinet components for signs of corrosion, loosening, or damage.

maintenance.name: Inspect spray devices
maintenance.statement: Inspect spray balls and nozzles for clogging, wear, and correct coverage pattern.
maintenance.description: Clogged or misaligned spray balls and nozzles compromise coverage and cleaning efficacy.

maintenance.name: Inspect wetted components
maintenance.statement: Inspect wetted components for corrosion, erosion, and surface deterioration.
maintenance.description: Technician visually and dimensionally inspects pipes, fittings, vessels, and other product-contact components for signs of wear or degradation.

maintenance.name: Lubricate mechanical drive components
maintenance.statement: Lubricate bearings, seals, and mechanical drive components per service intervals.
maintenance.description: Technician applies appropriate lubricants to bearings, shaft seals, and drive assemblies to prevent wear and extend service life of rotating and reciprocating components.

maintenance.name: Monitor equipment condition
maintenance.statement: Monitor the system's equipment condition and performance trends.
maintenance.description: Maintenance administrator or technician tracks equipment health indicators and performance trends to identify emerging faults before failure.

maintenance.name: Monitor system status
maintenance.statement: Monitor system alarms, utility supply, and operating parameters in real time.
maintenance.description: Ongoing monitoring of alarms, pressures, and utility supply detects faults before they cause failed cycles.

maintenance.name: Preserve idle systems
maintenance.statement: Preserve the system during extended shutdowns.
maintenance.description: This limits corrosion, microbial growth, freezing damage, and component degradation while the system remains unused.

maintenance.name: Repair pump and valve faults
maintenance.statement: Repair or replace failed pumps, actuators, and automated valves in the system.
maintenance.description: Technician diagnoses and corrects mechanical or actuator failures in pumps and automated valves to restore system operation.

maintenance.name: Replace cleaning chemical supply
maintenance.statement: Replenish the caustic, acid, and sanitizer chemical reservoirs feeding the system.
maintenance.description: Detergent, acid, and sanitizer concentrates deplete and must be restocked for effective cycles.

maintenance.name: Replace filtration elements
maintenance.statement: Replace spent filtration elements in the water supply and process circuits.
maintenance.description: Technician removes and replaces exhausted filter cartridges or membranes in the system's water treatment and process filtration circuits.

maintenance.name: Replace worn seals and valve seats
maintenance.statement: Replace worn gaskets, O-rings, and valve seats throughout the fluid path.
maintenance.description: Technician replaces elastomeric seals, O-rings, and valve seats that have degraded through chemical exposure or mechanical wear to maintain leak-free operation.

maintenance.name: Revalidate cleaning cycles
maintenance.statement: Revalidate the cleaning and sterilization cycles against required performance criteria.
maintenance.description: Technician or validation specialist conducts formal revalidation studies to confirm CIP/SIP cycle parameters continue to meet regulatory and process requirements after changes or at scheduled intervals.

maintenance.name: Service automated valves
maintenance.statement: Service the system's automated valves per scheduled maintenance intervals.
maintenance.description: Technician inspects, lubricates, adjusts, and replaces worn components in automated valves to maintain reliable actuation and sealing.

maintenance.name: Service circulation pumps
maintenance.statement: Service the system's circulation pumps per scheduled maintenance intervals.
maintenance.description: Technician inspects, lubricates, adjusts, and replaces wear components in circulation pumps to maintain flow performance.

maintenance.name: Service steam traps
maintenance.statement: Service the steam traps in the system's steam supply and condensate circuits.
maintenance.description: Technician inspects, tests, and replaces steam traps to prevent steam loss and condensate buildup in SIP circuits.

maintenance.name: Test alarm and interlock response
maintenance.statement: Test the response of process alarms and safety interlocks under simulated fault conditions.
maintenance.description: Technician or engineer verifies that each process alarm triggers at the correct setpoint and that safety interlocks halt operation as designed when limit conditions are simulated.

maintenance.name: Update control software
maintenance.statement: Update the control system software and cycle recipe firmware.
maintenance.description: Controller firmware and recipe software require updates for security, compliance, and new cycle parameters.

maintenance.name: Verify cleaning and sterilization efficacy
maintenance.statement: Verify cleaning and sterilization efficacy through sampling and analytical testing.
maintenance.description: Technician collects swab, rinse, or process samples and conducts analysis to confirm the CIP/SIP cycle has achieved required cleanliness and sterility levels.

usage.name: Acknowledge presence alerts
usage.statement: Acknowledge a resolved presence-related alert through the system interface.
usage.description: This clears the active notification after the underlying operating condition has been addressed.

usage.name: Adjust detection sensitivity
usage.statement: Adjust detection sensitivity settings during operation to match the container being run.
usage.description: Technician modifies the sensitivity threshold of the presence control system during live operation to correctly detect the current container format without false positives or misses.

usage.name: Adjust detection timing
usage.statement: Adjust the presence control system's detection timing to synchronise with the current machine cycle.
usage.description: Controls technician modifies the detection timing parameters of the presence control system so that detection windows align correctly with the filling machine's cycle at the current production rate.

usage.name: Apply authorized bypass
usage.statement: Apply an authorized presence-control bypass through the system interface.
usage.description: This supports controlled diagnostic or recovery activity when normal detection must be temporarily suspended.

usage.name: Demonstrate detection operation
usage.statement: Demonstrate the operation of presence detection during operator training runs.
usage.description: New operators learn to read detection indicators and respond during supervised runs.

usage.name: Detect at line speed
usage.statement: Detect container presence reliably at high production line speeds.
usage.description: On high-speed lines the system must keep pace with rapid container indexing without missed detections.

usage.name: Detect container presence
usage.statement: Detect the presence of a container at the fill station during production.
usage.description: The core function of the system is confirming that a container is present at the fill point before dispensing product.

usage.name: Handle varied container types
usage.statement: Handle variations in container size, shape, and material during detection.
usage.description: Different container shapes, sizes, and materials require the system to handle varied inputs on the same line.

usage.name: Inhibit fill without container
usage.statement: Inhibit the fill cycle when no container is present at the station.
usage.description: The system must prevent dispensing when no container is detected to avoid spillage and product loss.

usage.name: Inspect diagnostic status
usage.statement: Inspect live diagnostic status for intermittent presence detections.
usage.description: This provides operating evidence when detections fluctuate without a persistent system fault.

usage.name: Log detection events
usage.statement: Log presence-detection events and fill outcomes during operation.
usage.description: The system records each presence-detection event, including detected, missed, and inhibited-fill instances, to support production reporting and traceability.

usage.name: Monitor presence signals
usage.statement: Monitor presence-detection status during a production run.
usage.description: Operator monitors the live status of presence-detection signals throughout the active production run to confirm correct system operation.

usage.name: Monitor system status
usage.statement: Monitor the presence control system's health and fault status.
usage.description: Live monitoring surfaces detection faults and drift before they cause fill errors.

usage.name: Operate across environments
usage.statement: Operate detection reliably across varying environmental conditions on the line.
usage.description: Detection must remain reliable across the plant's temperature, humidity, and washdown conditions.

usage.name: Override detection manually
usage.statement: Manually override or bypass presence detection during clearing or testing.
usage.description: Operators may temporarily bypass or override detection for jams, testing, or maintenance during use.

usage.name: Reconfigure detection parameters for changeover
usage.statement: Reconfigure detection parameters when changing over to a different container or product format.
usage.description: Technician updates the presence control system's detection parameters — including position, sensitivity, and timing — when the filling line is transitioning to a new container type or product.

usage.name: Relay signals to controls
usage.statement: Relay presence-detection signals to the filling and conveyor control system.
usage.description: The presence system passes detection signals to filling and conveyor controls to coordinate the fill cycle.

usage.name: Restore detection enforcement
usage.statement: Restore active presence-control enforcement after a temporary bypass.
usage.description: This returns the filling machine to normal presence-dependent operation before routine production resumes.

usage.name: Review detection records
usage.statement: Review recorded presence detections, misses, and inhibited-fill events.
usage.description: Line supervisor or operator reviews the stored log of presence-detection activity to assess system performance, identify recurring issues, and support production accountability.

usage.name: Review presence alerts
usage.statement: Review the details of a presence-related alert reported by the system.
usage.description: This identifies the detection condition that interrupted or affected the filling cycle.

usage.name: Run presence challenge
usage.statement: Run a controlled challenge through the presence control system.
usage.description: This demonstrates the system's response to a known present-container or absent-container condition.

usage.name: Start presence monitoring
usage.statement: Start the presence control system for the active filling operation.
usage.description: This activates container-presence verification when filling begins.

usage.name: Stop presence monitoring
usage.statement: Stop the presence control system when the filling operation ends.
usage.description: This places presence monitoring into the appropriate state during production shutdowns.

usage.name: Trigger missing-container alarm
usage.statement: Trigger an alarm or stop when a container is missing or improperly positioned.
usage.description: When a container is missing or misplaced, the system must trigger an alert or stop to prevent faulty fills.

usage.name: Troubleshoot false detections
usage.statement: Troubleshoot false or missed detections occurring during a production run.
usage.description: Operators need to distinguish genuine misses from false trips caused by debris, foam, or reflections during use.

usage.name: Validate atypical containers
usage.statement: Validate the system's detection response for an atypical container presented to the filling machine.
usage.description: This matters when damaged, deformed, transparent, reflective, undersized, or mispositioned containers may enter the detection zone.

usage.name: Verify control outputs
usage.statement: Verify the presence control system's output signals to the filling machine controls.
usage.description: Output verification confirms that detection states are transmitted correctly after service or control changes.

usage.name: Verify fill-control output
usage.statement: Verify the presence control system's fill-control output during machine operation.
usage.description: This confirms that the system is issuing the expected permission or inhibition signal to the filling machine.

preparation.name: Align detection sensors
preparation.statement: Align each presence sensor to the target container path so detection occurs at the correct position.
preparation.description: Sensors must be aligned to reliably detect container presence at the fill station.

preparation.name: Calibrate detection thresholds
preparation.statement: Calibrate the presence detection sensitivity and thresholds for the container type and line speed.
preparation.description: Detection thresholds must be tuned to reliably distinguish present from absent containers.

preparation.name: Configure control system operating parameters
preparation.statement: Configure the presence control system parameters such as timing windows, response actions, and fault modes.
preparation.description: Establishes the operational behaviour of the system for the specific production environment.

preparation.name: Configure safety interlocks
preparation.statement: Set up the safety interlock logic that governs line response to missing or misplaced containers.
preparation.description: Safety interlocks ensure the line halts safely when a fault is detected.

preparation.name: Define system requirements
preparation.statement: Define the required specifications for a filling machine presence control system.
preparation.description: Establish the technical, functional, environmental, and regulatory requirements that a presence control system must satisfy before initiating supplier evaluation.

preparation.name: Document as-installed system configuration
preparation.statement: Document the as-installed configuration of the presence control system for ongoing maintenance and audit reference.
preparation.description: Records final sensor positions, wiring schemes, parameter settings, and calibration values as the baseline reference document.

preparation.name: Import existing configurations
preparation.statement: Import existing detection configurations or recipes from a previous system into the new presence control system.
preparation.description: Existing settings from prior systems can speed deployment on upgraded lines.

preparation.name: Install control unit
preparation.statement: Install the presence control unit in the designated enclosure or panel on the production line.
preparation.description: The control unit needs to be installed in an accessible, protected location.

preparation.name: Learn presence control system operating procedures
preparation.statement: Learn the presence control system's operating procedures before assuming operational responsibility.
preparation.description: Enables operators to understand system functions, alerts, and responses through self-directed or guided learning.

preparation.name: Link to reject actuators
preparation.statement: Connect the presence control outputs to the line's reject, stop, or no-fill actuators.
preparation.description: The system must be linked to reject or stop mechanisms so it can act on detection.

preparation.name: Mount presence sensors
preparation.statement: Mount the presence detection sensors onto the filling machine frame at the specified detection positions.
preparation.description: Sensors and detectors must be physically mounted at the correct points along the filling line.

preparation.name: Set Response Timing
preparation.statement: Set detection response timing to match the filling-machine sequence.
preparation.description: Appropriate timing compensates for transport speed, sensor position, control latency, and actuation delay.

preparation.name: Set up container recipes
preparation.statement: Set up and save detection configuration profiles for each container format run on the line.
preparation.description: Different containers require stored recipes for quick changeover.

preparation.name: Test detection accuracy
preparation.statement: Run test containers through the line to verify the presence control system detects and responds correctly.
preparation.description: A validation run confirms the system detects and responds correctly before production.

preparation.name: Train operators on system
preparation.statement: Train line operators on operating, monitoring, and responding to alerts from the presence control system.
preparation.description: Operators need to understand how to run and monitor the system day to day.

preparation.name: Unpack and inspect delivered components
preparation.statement: Unpack the presence control system and verify all sensors, controllers, and mounting hardware against the packing list.
preparation.description: Confirms shipment completeness and physical integrity before installation begins.

preparation.name: Verify Site Readiness
preparation.statement: Verify the installation site against the presence-control system requirements.
preparation.description: This confirms that the machine location can support the system's mechanical, electrical, and environmental needs.

preparation.name: Wire signal connections
preparation.statement: Wire the sensor and output signal lines between the presence control system and the filling machine controller.
preparation.description: Sensor and control signals must be wired into the filling machine's control circuitry.

disposal.name: Archive equipment records
disposal.statement: Archive maintenance, calibration, and asset records for the retired control system.
disposal.description: Transfer all historical maintenance logs, calibration records, and asset documentation for the presence control system into long-term archival storage per applicable regulatory and organizational retention requirements.

disposal.name: Cancel connected services
disposal.statement: Cancel network licenses and monitoring subscriptions linked to the control system.
disposal.description: Matters when networked or subscription-based monitoring services tied to the system must be ended.

disposal.name: Cancel linked services
disposal.statement: Cancel services linked to the retired presence control system.
disposal.description: Terminate all network licenses, monitoring subscriptions, maintenance contracts, and connected services associated with the retired control system to prevent unnecessary charges.

disposal.name: Conduct final safety inspection before removal
disposal.statement: Conduct a final safety inspection of the presence control system before initiating physical removal.
disposal.description: Perform a documented pre-removal safety check to verify that all energy sources are isolated, hazardous conditions are controlled, and the work area is safe for disassembly — distinct from energy isolation (candidate 2) and deactivation (candidate 16).
disposal.userGroup: Technician / Environmental Health and Safety Technician

disposal.name: Deactivate the presence control system
disposal.statement: Deactivate the filling machine presence control system prior to physical decommissioning.
disposal.description: Execute the controlled shutdown and deactivation sequence for the presence control system, disabling all active control functions before physical removal begins.

disposal.name: Decommission the presence control system
disposal.statement: Decommission the filling machine presence control system from the production line.
disposal.description: Execute the formal decommissioning procedure to retire the presence control system from active service.

disposal.name: Decontaminate retired components
disposal.statement: Remove process contamination from retired presence control components before handling or disposal.
disposal.description: Clean or decontaminate sensors, housings, and other components that have been exposed to process media (e.g., food-grade, chemical, or pharmaceutical substances) to comply with handling and disposal requirements.

disposal.name: Dispose of hazardous materials
disposal.statement: Dispose of hazardous materials such as batteries and capacitors per regulations.
disposal.description: Matters when the system contains batteries, capacitors, or optical elements needing regulated handling.

disposal.name: Document final disposition
disposal.statement: Document the final condition and disposition of the presence control system at retirement.
disposal.description: Record the final operational state, condition assessment, and disposition decision (e.g., recycled, sold, scrapped) for the control system as part of the formal asset closure process.

disposal.name: Donate serviceable components
disposal.statement: Donate serviceable sensors and controllers to a training or charitable organization.
disposal.description: Matters when a functional unit can serve training or lower-priority lines rather than being scrapped.

disposal.name: Erase stored operational data
disposal.statement: Erase proprietary production, recipe, and log data from the control system memory before disposal.
disposal.description: Purge all proprietary and sensitive operational data from the control system's storage to prevent unauthorized access after the unit leaves the facility.

disposal.name: Export configuration and calibration data
disposal.statement: Export stored configuration, calibration, and sensor threshold data from the control system before retirement.
disposal.description: Retrieve and preserve all system configuration, calibration parameters, and operational threshold data stored in the control system for future reference or migration.

disposal.name: Notify affected stakeholders of system removal
disposal.statement: Notify operators, maintenance personnel, and supervisors of the control system removal.
disposal.description: Formally communicate the retirement and physical removal of the presence control system to all personnel whose work or responsibilities are affected by the change.

disposal.name: Recover reusable components
disposal.statement: Recover reusable components from the retired presence control system.
disposal.description: This preserves serviceable sensors, controllers, connectors, mounting hardware, or spare assemblies for future use.

disposal.name: Recycle electronic components
disposal.statement: Recycle the sensors, controllers, and wiring from the retired control system through electronic waste channels.
disposal.description: Route end-of-life electronic components — including sensors, controllers, and associated wiring — through certified e-waste recycling streams in compliance with applicable regulations.

disposal.name: Return under warranty or recall
disposal.statement: Return defective or recalled control components to the manufacturer.
disposal.description: Matters when a defective batch or recalled sensor module qualifies for warranty or manufacturer return.

disposal.name: Transfer ownership or resell
disposal.statement: Transfer ownership or resell the presence control components to another operator.
disposal.description: Matters when photoelectric or capacitive sensors and controllers still hold resale value on the secondary market.

disposal.name: Transition to replacement system
disposal.statement: Transition line operation from the old control system to its replacement.
disposal.description: Matters when replacing an aging system to ensure the new presence control unit is ready before removal.

disposal.name: Uninstall mounted hardware
disposal.statement: Uninstall sensors, brackets, and controllers physically mounted on the filling machine.
disposal.description: Remove all physically mounted components of the presence control system — including sensors, mounting brackets, and controllers — from the filling machine structure.

acquisition.name: Arrange system shipment delivery
acquisition.statement: Arrange delivery of the ordered presence control system to the designated receiving location.
acquisition.description: Coordinate with the supplier and logistics provider to schedule and confirm delivery of the ordered system to the facility's designated receiving location.

acquisition.name: Assess detection performance specifications
acquisition.statement: Assess the detection performance specifications of candidate presence control systems against line requirements.
acquisition.description: Evaluate whether a candidate system's published detection performance specifications meet the throughput, accuracy, and reliability requirements of the filling line.

acquisition.name: Assess supplier support and service capability
acquisition.statement: Assess the technical support and service capability of candidate presence control system suppliers.
acquisition.description: Evaluate suppliers' ability to provide application engineering support, on-site service, spare parts availability, and response time commitments before committing to a purchase.

acquisition.name: Compare candidate systems against selection criteria
acquisition.statement: Compare candidate filling machine presence control systems against defined selection criteria.
acquisition.description: Systematically evaluate shortlisted presence control systems against documented technical, commercial, and regulatory selection criteria to identify the preferred option.

acquisition.name: Compare pricing and quotes
acquisition.statement: Compare pricing and quotations from suppliers of presence control systems.
acquisition.description: Pricing across purchase, installation, and support drives the procurement decision.

acquisition.name: Complete purchase transaction
acquisition.statement: Complete the purchase transaction for the presence control system.
acquisition.description: The formal order must move through internal procurement and approval workflows.

acquisition.name: Confirm regulatory and certification compliance
acquisition.statement: Confirm the regulatory and certification compliance of candidate presence control systems.
acquisition.description: Verify that candidate systems hold the applicable regulatory certifications (e.g., safety, electrical, food-contact) required for the intended operating environment.

acquisition.name: Evaluate machine compatibility
acquisition.statement: Evaluate compatibility of the presence control system with the existing filling machine.
acquisition.description: The system must physically and electrically integrate with the existing filling machine and controls.

acquisition.name: Evaluate total cost of ownership
acquisition.statement: Evaluate the total cost of ownership of candidate presence control systems over their expected service life.
acquisition.description: Estimate and compare the full lifecycle costs of candidate systems — including purchase price, installation, maintenance, consumables, and expected service life — to support the purchase decision.

acquisition.name: Register system ownership with supplier
acquisition.statement: Register the acquired presence control system with the supplier to activate warranty and support entitlements.
acquisition.description: Complete the supplier's product registration process after delivery to establish ownership records and activate warranty coverage and support services.

acquisition.name: Request system demonstration or trial
acquisition.statement: Request a demonstration or trial of a candidate presence control system prior to purchase.
acquisition.description: Arrange for a supplier to demonstrate or provide a trial unit of a candidate presence control system so that performance can be assessed under realistic conditions before committing to a purchase.

acquisition.name: Research presence control options
acquisition.statement: Research available filling machine presence control system technologies and suppliers.
acquisition.description: Identify and gather information on available presence control system technologies and potential suppliers before making a selection.

acquisition.name: Review warranty and support terms
acquisition.statement: Review warranty and support terms for the presence control system.
acquisition.description: Warranty and service terms determine ongoing support obligations after purchase.

acquisition.name: Secure Purchase Approval
acquisition.statement: Secure authorization to purchase the selected filling machine presence control system.
acquisition.description: This satisfies internal budget, technical, operational, or capital-expenditure approval requirements.

acquisition.name: Seek supplier recommendations
acquisition.statement: Seek recommendations and references for presence control system suppliers.
acquisition.description: Reputation and reliability feedback from other operators reduce selection risk.

acquisition.name: Track Shipment Status
acquisition.statement: Track the shipment status of the ordered system.
acquisition.description: This allows installation resources or production schedules to be adjusted when fulfillment delays occur.

acquisition.name: Verify environmental and hygienic ratings
acquisition.statement: Verify the environmental and hygienic ratings of candidate presence control systems against the production environment requirements.
acquisition.description: Confirm that a candidate system's IP/NEMA rating, washdown tolerance, and hygienic construction meet the demands of the intended production environment before purchase.

maintenance.name: Back up configuration settings
maintenance.statement: Back up the presence control system's configuration and threshold settings.
maintenance.description: Configuration profiles and threshold settings must be preserved to recover after failures or replacements.

maintenance.name: Calibrate presence sensors
maintenance.statement: Calibrate the presence-detection sensors to their reference thresholds.
maintenance.description: Aging or misaligned sensors drift and misreport container presence, causing missed or double fills.

maintenance.name: Clean sensor lenses
maintenance.statement: Clean the presence-detection sensor lenses and optical windows.
maintenance.description: Remove contaminants from optical sensing surfaces to maintain reliable detection performance.

maintenance.name: Document maintenance history
maintenance.statement: Document the maintenance and calibration history of the presence control system.
maintenance.description: Service records support audits, warranty claims, and root-cause analysis of recurring faults.

maintenance.name: Inspect enclosure seals and ingress protection
maintenance.statement: Inspect the enclosure seals and ingress-protection ratings of presence control housings.
maintenance.description: Examine gaskets, cable glands, and housing integrity to confirm the system maintains its rated IP protection class in washdown and harsh environments.

maintenance.name: Inspect wiring and connectors
maintenance.statement: Inspect the sensor wiring, connectors, and cable seals for wear or damage.
maintenance.description: Vibration and washdown loosen connectors and cabling, causing intermittent signal loss.

maintenance.name: Protect components during washdown
maintenance.statement: Protect susceptible presence control components during equipment washdown.
maintenance.description: Apply covers, seals, or isolation measures to prevent ingress damage to non-rated components during scheduled washdown.

maintenance.name: Realign sensor position
maintenance.statement: Realign the sensor mounting position relative to the container path.
maintenance.description: Restore correct geometric alignment between the presence sensor and the detection zone after mechanical disturbance or changeover.

maintenance.name: Repair control wiring
maintenance.statement: Repair damaged wiring within the presence control system.
maintenance.description: Restore electrical continuity and signal integrity by repairing or splicing damaged conductors and terminations.

maintenance.name: Replace failed sensors
maintenance.statement: Replace failed or degraded presence-detection sensors.
maintenance.description: Swap out inoperative or out-of-spec sensing devices to restore system detection capability.

maintenance.name: Replace mounting hardware
maintenance.statement: Replace worn or damaged presence sensor mounting hardware.
maintenance.description: Serviceable mounting hardware keeps sensing components stable despite vibration or repeated adjustment.

maintenance.name: Schedule preventive service
maintenance.statement: Schedule preventive maintenance for the presence control system.
maintenance.description: Scheduled preventive service reduces unplanned line stoppages caused by detection failures.

maintenance.name: Store spare sensing components
maintenance.statement: Store spare presence-detection sensors and replacement parts in controlled conditions.
maintenance.description: Maintain an on-hand inventory of critical sensing components stored per environmental specifications to minimise unplanned downtime.

maintenance.name: Test detection response
maintenance.statement: Test the presence-detection response using reference containers.
maintenance.description: Verify correct sensor triggering by passing representative containers through the detection zone.

maintenance.name: Troubleshoot detection faults
maintenance.statement: Troubleshoot and diagnose recurring presence-detection faults.
maintenance.description: Isolate root causes of intermittent or persistent detection errors to guide corrective action.

maintenance.name: Update control firmware
maintenance.statement: Update the firmware and control software of the presence control system.
maintenance.description: Firmware updates fix detection logic bugs and add features to the control system.

maintenance.name: Upgrade detection modules
maintenance.statement: Upgrade the presence-detection modules or controller components.
maintenance.description: Install higher-capability sensing or control hardware to extend or improve system functionality.

usage.name: Activate breach response
usage.statement: Activate the isolator's containment response after a boundary breach.
usage.description: This limits release or exposure when glove integrity, transfer integrity, or enclosure integrity is lost.

usage.name: Activate containment operation
usage.statement: Activate the enclosure's designated operating mode for the current process.
usage.description: Operator initiates the appropriate operating mode — positive pressure, negative pressure, or recirculation — on the enclosure control system prior to production.

usage.name: Activate the isolator operating mode
usage.statement: Activate the isolator's operating mode for active containment.
usage.description: Operator initiates the isolator's active containment state, confirming airflow, pressure, and safety interlocks are engaged before work begins.

usage.name: Adapt work technique to isolator geometry
usage.statement: Adapt manual work technique to accommodate the spatial constraints of the isolator enclosure.
usage.description: Operator modifies grip, posture, and tool use to work effectively within the fixed geometry and reach limits imposed by the isolator's glove ports, sleeves, or half-suit entry points.

usage.name: Adjust airflow and pressure settings
usage.statement: Adjust the isolator's airflow and pressure settings for the task at hand.
usage.description: Operator modifies airflow rate and pressure differential setpoints via the control interface to match the requirements of the current task.

usage.name: Adjust operating setpoints
usage.statement: Adjust authorized setpoints on the enclosure control system for the active operation.
usage.description: Authorized operator modifies pressure, airflow, temperature, or alarm threshold setpoints via the control interface to match process requirements.

usage.name: Check glove integrity
usage.statement: Inspect enclosure gloves for signs of integrity loss during active operation.
usage.description: Operator visually and tactilely examines gloves and sleeve assemblies for tears, punctures, or deformation while the enclosure is running.

usage.name: Collect process samples
usage.statement: Withdraw in-process samples for testing while preserving containment.
usage.description: Sampling for quality testing must occur without breaking containment or introducing contamination.

usage.name: Contain hazardous compounds
usage.statement: Contain potent or hazardous materials while dispensing, weighing, or processing them inside the enclosure.
usage.description: Operator uses the enclosure to prevent operator and environmental exposure when handling highly potent or toxic substances during active processing.

usage.name: Coordinate multi-operator use
usage.statement: Coordinate simultaneous work by multiple operators at shared glove ports.
usage.description: Larger isolators support several operators working the same enclosure on collaborative tasks.

usage.name: Coordinate multi-operator work
usage.statement: Coordinate simultaneous or sequential operator access to the enclosure during a production run.
usage.description: Lead operator manages glove-port assignments, task sequencing, and communication among multiple operators working at the same enclosure.

usage.name: Document isolator operating activity
usage.statement: Record operational parameters and activities performed within the isolator.
usage.description: Operator or designated recorder logs operating conditions, material identities, and procedural actions taken during the isolator session.

usage.name: Document usage activity
usage.statement: Record operating parameters, batch details, and interventions during use.
usage.description: Regulatory and quality requirements mandate a record of what happened during each run.

usage.name: Handle hazardous substances within the isolator
usage.statement: Handle toxic, potent, or infectious substances within the contained workspace.
usage.description: Operator works with hazardous materials inside the isolator enclosure, relying on the containment barrier for protection.

usage.name: Handle varying batch scales
usage.statement: Process material across different batch volumes within the same enclosure.
usage.description: Different batch sizes and processes require the isolator to accommodate varying throughput.

usage.name: Maintain aseptic conditions
usage.statement: Maintain aseptic conditions inside the isolator throughout the operation.
usage.description: Sterile processing requires ongoing prevention of contamination during every handling step.

usage.name: Monitor environmental conditions
usage.statement: Monitor pressure, airflow, humidity, and particulate levels inside the enclosure during operation.
usage.description: Operator continuously reads and interprets environmental parameter displays to confirm the enclosure remains within specification.

usage.name: Observe interior workspace
usage.statement: Observe the interior workspace through the isolator's viewing panels during operation.
usage.description: Continuous visual monitoring is required to perform precise manipulations safely.

usage.name: Operate ancillary equipment inside
usage.statement: Operate instruments and equipment placed inside the isolator during use.
usage.description: Balances, pumps, and analyzers must be run from within the enclosure without breaking containment.

usage.name: Operate integrated equipment
usage.statement: Operate scales, filling equipment, and instruments installed inside the isolator.
usage.description: Instruments and process equipment placed inside must be operated within the sealed enclosure.

usage.name: Operate through glove ports
usage.statement: Operate the enclosed process using integrated glove-and-sleeve access ports.
usage.description: Operator inserts hands into glove ports to physically manipulate materials, tools, and equipment inside the sealed enclosure.

usage.name: Perform aseptic manipulations
usage.statement: Perform product handling and processing steps inside the enclosure.
usage.description: Operator conducts aseptic handling operations — weighing, filling, assembling — within the isolator workspace using glove-port access.

usage.name: Practice manipulation techniques
usage.statement: Practice glove-port manipulation and transfer procedures during training use.
usage.description: New personnel must build dexterity and aseptic technique before performing live work.

usage.name: Purge enclosure atmosphere
usage.statement: Purge the enclosure atmosphere with inert or conditioned gas prior to or during processing.
usage.description: Operator initiates a nitrogen or dry-air purge cycle to displace oxygen or moisture from the enclosure interior before introducing sensitive materials.

usage.name: Recover interrupted operation
usage.statement: Restore the isolator to its approved operating state after a utility interruption.
usage.description: This is required when power, ventilation, controls, or another supporting utility disrupts containment operation.

usage.name: Remove process waste
usage.statement: Remove process waste through the isolator's contained discharge interface.
usage.description: This limits operator exposure when extracting contaminated or hazardous residual material.

usage.name: Remove processed materials from the isolator
usage.statement: Remove processed materials and waste from the isolator via the transfer system.
usage.description: Operator extracts finished or waste materials from the isolator interior through the transfer port at the conclusion of a task.

usage.name: Respond to containment alarms
usage.statement: Respond to pressure, filter, or containment alarms triggered during use.
usage.description: Operator assesses and acts on active alarms generated by the isolator's monitoring systems to address potential containment deviations.

usage.name: Sustain high-volume campaigns
usage.statement: Run the isolator continuously through extended high-throughput production campaigns.
usage.description: Continuous or intensive campaigns push the isolator to sustained high-volume operation.

usage.name: Transfer materials into the isolator
usage.statement: Transfer materials and samples into the isolator interior through the transfer system.
usage.description: Operator moves materials from outside into the contained workspace using the designated transfer port or rapid-transfer system.

usage.name: Transfer materials through ports
usage.statement: Transfer components and materials in and out of the isolator through transfer ports.
usage.description: Operators must transfer materials in and out without breaching the containment barrier.

usage.name: Trigger emergency shutdown of the isolator
usage.statement: Trigger the isolator's emergency shutdown function during an unsafe operating event.
usage.description: Operator activates the emergency stop or shutdown sequence to halt isolator operation when a safety-critical condition is detected.

usage.name: Troubleshoot glove breaches
usage.statement: Troubleshoot glove or seal breaches detected during active use.
usage.description: Compromised gloves during work threaten containment and must be managed without halting critical tasks improperly.

usage.name: Troubleshoot operational alarms
usage.statement: Diagnose and resolve alarms and deviations that occur during operation.
usage.description: Deviations, alarms, or breaches must be resolved without compromising the batch or containment.

disposal.name: Archive lifecycle documentation
disposal.statement: Archive qualification, validation, calibration, change-control, maintenance, and decommissioning records associated with the isolator.
disposal.description: Consolidate and archive the full set of lifecycle documentation in a durable, retrievable format per applicable record-retention requirements.

disposal.name: Archive lifecycle records
disposal.statement: Archive qualification, calibration, and maintenance records associated with the retired isolator.
disposal.description: Consolidation and long-term storage of all lifecycle documentation — validation reports, calibration logs, maintenance histories — in a retrievable archive per applicable retention requirements.

disposal.name: Assess residual value of the isolator
disposal.statement: Assess the residual market or salvage value of the decommissioned isolator to inform disposition decisions.
disposal.description: Evaluate the isolator's condition, remaining useful life, and market demand to determine whether resale, donation, cannibalization for parts, or scrapping provides the best economic outcome.

disposal.name: Cancel service contracts
disposal.statement: Cancel maintenance, calibration, and monitoring service contracts tied to the isolator.
disposal.description: Matters when ongoing calibration, monitoring, or service contracts tied to the isolator must be ended.

disposal.name: Clear remaining materials from chamber
disposal.statement: Remove all remaining materials from the isolator chamber prior to decontamination and decommissioning.
disposal.description: Physical removal of any residual samples, reagents, equipment, or waste from the isolator interior to prepare for decontamination procedures.

disposal.name: Decommission the isolator
disposal.statement: Decommission the containment isolator from active production service.
disposal.description: Formally retire the isolator from operational use, completing all required decommissioning steps per facility and regulatory requirements.

disposal.name: Decontaminate isolator surfaces
disposal.statement: Decontaminate all interior and exterior surfaces of the containment isolator before removal from service.
disposal.description: Systematic surface decontamination using validated agents and procedures to render the isolator safe for handling, disassembly, or transfer.

disposal.name: Decontaminate the isolator prior to disposal
disposal.statement: Decontaminate the isolator interior and surfaces prior to disposal.
disposal.description: Apply approved decontamination procedures to all internal surfaces, pass-throughs, and external contact zones before the unit leaves the facility.

disposal.name: Disconnect facility services
disposal.statement: Disconnect the containment isolator from facility power, gases, ventilation, drainage, and data services.
disposal.description: This isolates the retired equipment from building systems before removal or dismantling.

disposal.name: Dismantle and remove
disposal.statement: Dismantle and physically remove the isolator from the facility.
disposal.description: Matters when the large, sealed enclosure must be safely disassembled and removed from a cleanroom environment.

disposal.name: Dispose of hazardous materials
disposal.statement: Dispose of contaminated filters, gloves, and consumables as regulated hazardous or biohazardous waste.
disposal.description: Matters because captured contaminants inside the isolator classify components as regulated waste.

disposal.name: Document final condition
disposal.statement: Document the final decontaminated and decommissioned state of the isolator for the disposal record.
disposal.description: Creation of a formal end-of-life record capturing physical condition, decontamination status, and disposition method, providing an auditable closure document.

disposal.name: Donate usable equipment
disposal.statement: Donate a serviceable decontaminated isolator to an educational or nonprofit institution.
disposal.description: Matters when equipment is retired for upgrade but remains functional for lower-demand users.

disposal.name: Export operational data
disposal.statement: Export logged environmental, alarm, and operational data from the isolator control system before shutdown.
disposal.description: Retrieval and secure transfer of all process and monitoring records stored on the isolator's control system to a facility data repository prior to decommissioning.

disposal.name: Export retained operational records
disposal.statement: Export historical monitoring, alarm, audit, and batch records from the isolator control system.
disposal.description: Extract all electronically stored operational records from the isolator's control system to a durable, facility-managed repository before decommissioning.

disposal.name: Notify stakeholders of isolator retirement
disposal.statement: Notify quality, regulatory, and facility stakeholders of the isolator's removal from service.
disposal.description: Communicate the planned or completed decommissioning to all relevant internal and external parties, including quality assurance, regulatory affairs, and facilities management.

disposal.name: Recycle recoverable isolator components
disposal.statement: Recycle eligible containment isolator components — including structural, glazing, and electronic parts — through qualified recovery channels.
disposal.description: Identify, segregate, and route recyclable materials and components from the decommissioned isolator to qualified recycling or material-recovery facilities in compliance with applicable regulations.
disposal.userGroup: EHS, facilities, and waste management teams

disposal.name: Recycle structural components
disposal.statement: Recycle the stainless steel, glass, and metal components of the isolator after decontamination.
disposal.description: Matters because bulk materials have recovery value and support sustainability targets.

disposal.name: Remove residual materials
disposal.statement: Remove residual pharmaceutical materials from the containment isolator before retirement.
disposal.description: This prevents remaining product, process waste, or cleaning agents from complicating decontamination and disposal.

disposal.name: Remove spent HEPA/ULPA filters
disposal.statement: Remove and contain the spent HEPA or ULPA filters from the isolator for controlled disposal.
disposal.description: Safe removal and bagging of high-efficiency particulate filters in accordance with containment protocols, preventing release of captured contaminants during extraction.

disposal.name: Remove stored access credentials
disposal.statement: Remove stored credentials and access data from the isolator control system before disposal.
disposal.description: Purging of user accounts, passwords, certificates, and any stored access credentials from the isolator's control or monitoring system to prevent unauthorised access post-disposal.

disposal.name: Return isolator through applicable return program
disposal.statement: Return the containment isolator to the manufacturer or an approved recipient under warranty, recall, trade-in, or equipment take-back terms.
disposal.description: Initiate and complete the formal return of the isolator through the applicable manufacturer or third-party return program, ensuring all contractual, regulatory, and logistical conditions are met.

disposal.name: Return isolator to provider
disposal.statement: Return the isolator or defective components to the designated provider under warranty or recall terms.
disposal.description: Coordination of the physical and administrative return of the isolator to the manufacturer or service provider, including RMA documentation, transport logistics, and receipt confirmation.

disposal.name: Segregate contaminated components
disposal.statement: Segregate containment isolator components according to their verified contamination status.
disposal.description: This prevents released materials from being mixed with components that still require controlled handling.

disposal.name: Segregate hazardous components
disposal.statement: Segregate hazardous isolator components for approved disposal pathways.
disposal.description: Identification and physical separation of components containing hazardous materials (e.g., contaminated panels, seals, electrical components with regulated substances) into appropriate waste streams prior to final disposal.
disposal.userGroup: Professional | Environmental Health and Safety Specialist | One-time

disposal.name: Transfer isolator ownership
disposal.statement: Transfer ownership of the isolator to a new operator, facility, or approved recipient.
disposal.description: Execute the legal and logistical transfer of isolator ownership, including title documentation, asset records, and any required regulatory notifications.

disposal.name: Update asset inventory
disposal.statement: Update the facility asset inventory to remove the disposed isolator from active records.
disposal.description: Modification of the asset management register to reflect the retirement, transfer, or disposal of the isolator, ensuring accurate inventory and compliance reporting.

disposal.name: Verify decontamination completion
disposal.statement: Verify and document that the isolator meets applicable decontamination criteria prior to disposal.
disposal.description: Independent review confirming that all decontamination validation criteria have been satisfied and the unit is cleared for further disposition steps.

acquisition.name: Arrange delivery logistics
acquisition.statement: Arrange delivery, rigging, and placement logistics for the purchased isolator.
acquisition.description: Coordinates the scheduling, transportation, rigging equipment, and access requirements needed to physically move and position the isolator within the laboratory.

acquisition.name: Assess supplier support capability
acquisition.statement: Assess the supplier's ability to provide installation support, training, spare parts, and ongoing service.
acquisition.description: Evaluates the supplier's service infrastructure — field service coverage, spare parts availability, application support, and training resources — as a factor in the purchasing decision, separate from product specifications.
acquisition.userGroup: Enterprise | Purchaser / Procurement Specialist | As needed

acquisition.name: Assess total cost of ownership
acquisition.statement: Assess the total cost of ownership of candidate isolators before committing to purchase.
acquisition.description: Estimate and compare lifecycle costs—including purchase price, installation, qualification, maintenance, and consumables—across candidate isolators to support an informed acquisition decision.
