acquisition.statement: Assess the total cost of ownership of candidate isolators before committing to purchase.
acquisition.description: Estimate and compare lifecycle costs—including purchase price, installation, qualification, maintenance, and consumables—across candidate isolators to support an informed acquisition decision.

acquisition.name: Compare candidate isolators
acquisition.statement: Compare candidate isolators across containment level, features, and price.
acquisition.description: Selection hinges on comparing containment level, footprint, and decontamination method across suppliers.

acquisition.name: Compare isolator configurations
acquisition.statement: Compare candidate isolator configurations against defined selection criteria.
acquisition.description: Systematically evaluates shortlisted isolator configurations — e.g., negative/positive pressure, glove-port count, pass-through options, construction materials — against the buyer's documented requirements to support a selection decision.
acquisition.userGroup: Professional | End User / Procurement Specialist | As needed

acquisition.name: Complete procurement transaction
acquisition.statement: Complete the purchase order and transaction for the selected isolator.
acquisition.description: Matters as the formal step that commits funds and initiates fabrication or delivery.

acquisition.name: Complete purchase transaction
acquisition.statement: Place the order and complete the purchase transaction for the approved pharmaceutical containment isolator.
acquisition.description: Issue the purchase order to the selected supplier and execute all contractual and financial steps required to conclude the procurement, following receipt of authorisation.

acquisition.name: Define containment requirements
acquisition.statement: Define the containment performance and dimensional requirements the isolator must satisfy.
acquisition.description: Capture and document all containment performance, airflow, pressure, and dimensional constraints before engaging suppliers.

acquisition.name: Evaluate facility fit
acquisition.statement: Evaluate whether a candidate isolator fits the available laboratory space, utilities, and infrastructure.
acquisition.description: Assesses physical and utility compatibility — footprint, weight, HVAC connections, power supply — between the candidate isolator and the intended installation site, prior to purchase.
acquisition.userGroup: Professional | Facilities Engineer / Administrator | As needed

acquisition.name: Evaluate process compatibility
acquisition.statement: Evaluate whether a candidate isolator is compatible with the intended process and facility.
acquisition.description: Assess isolator fit against process parameters, material flows, ergonomics, and facility constraints to confirm suitability before purchase.

acquisition.name: Evaluate total cost of ownership
acquisition.statement: Evaluate the total cost of ownership of candidate isolators across their expected service life.
acquisition.description: Estimates and compares acquisition price, installation, consumables, maintenance contracts, validation costs, and end-of-life disposal for each candidate isolator to support a financially sound selection decision.
acquisition.userGroup: Enterprise | Purchaser / Finance Analyst | As needed

acquisition.name: Obtain compliance documentation
acquisition.statement: Obtain the certificates, test reports, and validation documentation supplied with the isolator.
acquisition.description: Collects all regulatory certificates, factory acceptance test reports, material certifications, and other required documents from the supplier at or before delivery, to support site qualification and regulatory files.

acquisition.name: Qualify prospective suppliers
acquisition.statement: Qualify prospective containment isolator suppliers before placing an order.
acquisition.description: Assess and formally approve suppliers against quality, technical, regulatory, and financial criteria to create an approved supplier list prior to procurement.

acquisition.name: Register equipment asset
acquisition.statement: Register the isolator in asset and warranty tracking systems.
acquisition.description: Warranty and traceability require the equipment to be logged into asset and quality systems.

acquisition.name: Request commercial proposal
acquisition.statement: Request a formal commercial proposal for the selected isolator configuration and delivery terms.
acquisition.description: Solicits a complete commercial offer from the supplier, including pricing, lead time, payment terms, and contractual conditions, to support purchase authorization.
acquisition.userGroup: Enterprise | Purchaser / Procurement Specialist | As needed

acquisition.name: Request supplier proposals
acquisition.statement: Request configured proposals or quotations from qualified suppliers for the specified containment isolator.
acquisition.description: Formally approach shortlisted or qualified suppliers to obtain detailed technical and commercial proposals — including pricing, lead time, and configuration — for the required isolator specification.
acquisition.frequency: Once per procurement event, potentially repeated across multiple suppliers

acquisition.name: Research isolator options
acquisition.statement: Research available isolator configurations and technologies on the market.
acquisition.description: Survey the market landscape of containment isolator types, configurations, and suppliers to build an informed long list of candidates.

acquisition.name: Review design proposal
acquisition.statement: Review the vendor's proposed isolator design against the procurement specification.
acquisition.description: Evaluate the technical and commercial detail of a supplier's design proposal to determine conformance with specified requirements before committing to purchase.

acquisition.name: Review supplier qualifications
acquisition.statement: Review the isolator supplier's relevant qualifications.
acquisition.description: This verifies that the supplier can provide suitable quality controls, technical support, documentation, and lifecycle service.

acquisition.name: Secure purchase approval
acquisition.statement: Secure authorization to purchase the selected pharmaceutical containment isolator.
acquisition.description: This completes the applicable capital, quality, safety, technical, and procurement approval process.

acquisition.name: Seek supplier references
acquisition.statement: Seek references and reviews for candidate isolator suppliers.
acquisition.description: Peer references and factory visits inform confidence in a supplier's containment track record.

acquisition.name: Seek vendor references
acquisition.statement: Seek references and reviews from existing users of the isolator supplier.
acquisition.description: Matters because containment reliability and vendor support are validated through peer experience.

acquisition.name: Track isolator shipment
acquisition.statement: Track the containment isolator through delivery.
acquisition.description: This enables receiving personnel, rigging resources, controlled access, and installation schedules to be prepared for arrival.

acquisition.name: Transition operations to a replacement isolator
acquisition.statement: Transition active operations, validated configurations, and trained personnel to a replacement isolator.
acquisition.description: Manage the operational handover from the retiring isolator to its replacement, including configuration transfer, SOPs, and personnel familiarisation.

acquisition.name: Trial operator interfaces
acquisition.statement: Trial the isolator's operator interfaces before selection.
acquisition.description: This reveals whether glove ports, viewing panels, controls, alarms, transfer points, and work-zone dimensions suit expected users.

acquisition.name: Verify regulatory compliance
acquisition.statement: Verify the isolator meets applicable regulatory and containment standards.
acquisition.description: Confirm that a candidate isolator's design and certifications satisfy relevant regulatory requirements for the intended operating environment.

acquisition.name: Witness acceptance testing
acquisition.statement: Witness the specified pre-delivery acceptance tests for the isolator.
acquisition.description: This confirms that the completed unit meets agreed functional and containment criteria before shipment authorization.

preparation.name: Arrange isolator delivery
preparation.statement: Arrange delivery and rigging of the pharmaceutical containment isolator to the receiving site.
preparation.description: Coordinate all logistics, transport, and rigging activities required to move the ordered isolator from the supplier or staging location to the designated installation site.

preparation.name: Assemble isolator modules into complete unit
preparation.statement: Assemble the isolator chamber, frame, transfer components, and ancillary modules into the fully integrated unit per manufacturer assembly instructions.
preparation.description: The technician mechanically assembles all isolator sub-units — chamber sections, transfer hatches, support frame, and ancillary modules — into the complete, installation-ready isolator.

preparation.name: Assemble isolator sections
preparation.statement: Assemble the isolator chamber, gloveports, and transfer components.
preparation.description: Large isolators are shipped in sections that must be joined to form the complete enclosure.

preparation.name: Calibrate instrumentation
preparation.statement: Calibrate the isolator's pressure, flow, and monitoring instruments.
preparation.description: Pressure sensors, airflow meters, and particle monitors must be calibrated so containment readings are accurate.

preparation.name: Calibrate isolator monitoring instruments
preparation.statement: Calibrate the airflow, pressure, and sensor instrumentation of the isolator.
preparation.description: Calibration technicians verify and adjust the isolator's airflow meters, pressure transducers, and environmental sensors against traceable reference standards to ensure accurate readings.

preparation.name: Calibrate monitoring sensors against traceable references
preparation.statement: Calibrate the isolator's monitoring sensors against traceable reference standards prior to qualification or use.
preparation.description: The calibration technician verifies and adjusts pressure, temperature, airflow, and particle-count sensors using traceable reference instruments, generating calibration records required for regulatory compliance.

preparation.name: Complete installation and operational qualification documentation
preparation.statement: Complete the installation qualification (IQ) and operational qualification (OQ) documentation required to release the isolator for regulated use.
preparation.description: The qualification specialist executes and documents IQ and OQ protocols — verifying that the isolator was installed and operates as specified — to satisfy regulatory requirements prior to process validation.

preparation.name: Configure containment operating parameters
preparation.statement: Configure the isolator pressure, airflow, and alarm settings.
preparation.description: Technicians or administrators set the isolator's pressure differentials, airflow rates, alarm thresholds, and operating modes to match the intended containment application.

preparation.name: Configure user access and role assignments
preparation.statement: Configure user accounts and role-based access permissions for isolator operators and administrators.
preparation.description: The system administrator creates and assigns user profiles and role-based access controls on the isolator's control system so that only authorised personnel can operate, configure, or override isolator functions.

preparation.name: Connect to power
preparation.statement: Connect the isolator to its electrical power supply.
preparation.description: The isolator requires electrical supply for blowers, controls, and instrumentation before it can operate.

preparation.name: Connect utilities and ducting to isolator
preparation.statement: Connect the isolator to facility HVAC ducting, exhaust, gas, and other utility services.
preparation.description: The installer connects all required utility feeds — supply and exhaust HVAC ducting, inert gas lines, and other facility services — to the isolator in accordance with the site utility schedule.

preparation.name: Connect utilities and services
preparation.statement: Connect the isolator to facility utilities such as gas, air, and exhaust.
preparation.description: Isolators must be tied into building services such as compressed air, gas, exhaust ducting, or drainage.

preparation.name: Decontaminate isolator interior before initial use
preparation.statement: Decontaminate the isolator interior prior to initial use.
preparation.description: Operators apply an approved decontamination agent or cycle to all interior surfaces of the isolator to achieve the required biological or chemical cleanliness before first use.

preparation.name: Define and document material transfer protocols
preparation.statement: Define and document the material transfer procedures to be used for introducing and removing items through isolator transfer ports.
preparation.description: The process engineer or operator lead establishes written procedures specifying the sequence, dwell times, and decontamination steps for transferring materials in and out of the isolator through rapid transfer ports (RTPs) or pass-through chambers, ensuring containment is not compromised during transfers.

preparation.name: Define containment requirements
preparation.statement: Define the required containment level and performance specifications needed before selecting an isolator.
preparation.description: Captures the pre-selection job of establishing measurable containment and performance criteria so that candidate isolators can be evaluated objectively.
preparation.userGroup: Professional | End User / Safety Officer | One-time

preparation.name: Document isolator configuration baseline
preparation.statement: Document the as-installed configuration and parameter settings of the isolator.
preparation.description: Technicians or administrators record all installed components, software versions, configuration parameters, and physical dimensions to create a baseline configuration record for the isolator.

preparation.name: Establish internal environmental monitoring layout
preparation.statement: Establish the placement and configuration of internal environmental monitoring points within the isolator.
preparation.description: The validation or qualification specialist defines and documents the locations of particle counters, temperature probes, and other monitoring sensors inside the isolator chamber based on process risk assessment, ensuring representative monitoring during operation.

preparation.name: Install and configure isolator control software
preparation.statement: Install and configure the isolator control software on the associated control platform.
preparation.description: Technicians install the isolator's control or supervisory software onto the designated controller or HMI and configure it with site-specific parameters, user accounts, and audit-trail settings.

preparation.name: Install filtration media
preparation.statement: Install the HEPA/ULPA filters into the isolator air-handling system.
preparation.description: HEPA/ULPA filters must be mounted and seated before airflow containment is established.

preparation.name: Integrate process equipment within isolator enclosure
preparation.statement: Install and arrange process equipment within the isolator enclosure to support the intended process operation.
preparation.description: The integration specialist or operator mounts, positions, and connects process equipment — such as filling systems, dispensing tools, or sampling devices — inside the isolator chamber before process qualification or start-up.

preparation.name: Justify and secure capital purchase authorization
preparation.statement: Justify the isolator purchase within the organization's capital approval process and secure the required authorization to proceed.
preparation.description: Combines the act of building and presenting the business case for capital expenditure with the act of obtaining formal sign-off, which are sequential steps in the same approval workflow.
preparation.userGroup: Procurement managers, laboratory managers, finance approvers

preparation.name: Perform initial decontamination
preparation.statement: Perform the initial decontamination cycle inside the isolator chamber.
preparation.description: A first-time decontamination or bio-decontamination cycle establishes the sterile or clean baseline inside the chamber.

preparation.name: Position and level isolator
preparation.statement: Position the isolator in its installation site and level it.
preparation.description: The isolator must be moved to and leveled in its cleanroom or lab location so airflow and drainage behave as designed.

preparation.name: Practice enclosure techniques
preparation.statement: Rehearse glove-port handling and transfer procedures during operator training.
preparation.description: New operators must learn to work within the enclosure before performing live production.

preparation.name: Qualify isolator installation and operational performance
preparation.statement: Perform installation and operational qualification of the isolator.
preparation.description: Validation technicians execute formal IQ/OQ protocols to confirm the isolator is installed correctly and operates within its specified performance parameters, generating documented evidence for regulatory compliance.

preparation.name: Store isolator accessories properly
preparation.statement: Store isolator accessories and consumable spares under conditions that preserve their integrity.
preparation.description: Place gloves, sleeves, filter cartridges, gaskets, and other consumables in controlled storage conditions (temperature, humidity, contamination) to prevent premature degradation before installation.

preparation.name: Track isolator shipment
preparation.statement: Track the pharmaceutical containment isolator during shipment to its destination.
preparation.description: Monitor the transit status of the ordered isolator from dispatch to arrival, identifying delays or exceptions and communicating progress to relevant stakeholders.
preparation.frequency: Continuously during transit, once per shipment

preparation.name: Train operators in containment operating procedures
preparation.statement: Train operators in the containment and aseptic operating procedures specific to the pharmaceutical containment isolator.
preparation.description: The trainer delivers structured training to operators covering gowning, glove changes, material transfer, decontamination, alarm response, and emergency procedures relevant to isolator operation.

preparation.name: Train operators on use
preparation.statement: Train operators in safe operation and containment procedures for the isolator.
preparation.description: Users need training on safe glovebox technique, transfer procedures, and emergency response.

preparation.name: Unpack, inspect, and verify delivered isolator
preparation.statement: Unpack the delivered isolator, inspect it for transit damage or missing items, and verify it matches the ordered configuration.
preparation.description: Upon delivery, remove packaging, examine the isolator for physical damage, contamination, or shortages, and confirm that the unit received corresponds to the specified order — combining the incoming inspection and order-verification steps into one controlled receipt activity.
preparation.userGroup: Receiving/logistics staff and procurement or quality personnel

preparation.name: Verify containment integrity before use
preparation.statement: Verify that the enclosure boundary is intact before beginning a production operation.
preparation.description: Operator performs a pre-use pressure-hold or glove pressure test to confirm enclosure integrity is acceptable before introducing product or hazardous materials.

preparation.name: Verify containment integrity of assembled isolator
preparation.statement: Verify the leak-tightness and glove integrity of the assembled isolator prior to first use.
preparation.description: The technician performs pressure-hold or tracer-gas leak tests and glove integrity checks on the fully assembled isolator to confirm that the containment boundary meets specified performance requirements.

preparation.name: Witness factory acceptance test
preparation.statement: Witness the factory acceptance test of the fabricated isolator prior to shipment.
preparation.description: Attend and observe the supplier's factory acceptance test to confirm the built isolator meets specified performance and quality criteria before shipment.

maintenance.name: Back up configuration data
maintenance.statement: Back up the isolator control-system configuration data.
maintenance.description: Current backups support restoration of validated settings, recipes, user configurations, and audit records after a control-system failure.

maintenance.name: Calibrate pressure sensors
maintenance.statement: Calibrate the differential pressure and monitoring instrumentation.
maintenance.description: Accurate pressure readings are essential to prove containment and require periodic calibration.

maintenance.name: Clean and decontaminate isolator interior
maintenance.statement: Clean the isolator's interior surfaces and perform decontamination to remove particulate, chemical, and microbial residues.
maintenance.description: Execute scheduled or triggered cleaning of the interior working chamber followed by a validated decontamination cycle to restore the isolator to the required cleanliness and microbial-control standard between uses or maintenance events.
maintenance.frequency: Scheduled and event-driven; multiple times during service life

maintenance.name: Decontaminate interior working chamber
maintenance.statement: Decontaminate the isolator's interior working chamber between processes.
maintenance.description: Remove biological, chemical, or particulate contamination from the isolator's working chamber prior to or following a process run.

maintenance.name: Document maintenance history
maintenance.statement: Record all maintenance, calibration, and repair activities for the isolator.
maintenance.description: Maintain a complete, traceable log of maintenance events to support regulatory compliance and operational continuity.

maintenance.name: Inspect containment boundaries
maintenance.statement: Inspect the isolator containment boundary for deterioration or damage.
maintenance.description: Examine all structural and sealing surfaces of the containment envelope for signs of wear, chemical attack, or physical damage.

maintenance.name: Inspect door and port gasket seals
maintenance.statement: Inspect the isolator's door, port, and transfer-hatch gasket seals.
maintenance.description: Visually and physically inspect gasket seals on doors, glove ports, and transfer hatches for wear, cracking, or loss of elasticity.

maintenance.name: Inspect glove integrity
maintenance.statement: Inspect the isolator gloves and gauntlets for pinholes, tears, or degradation.
maintenance.description: Matters regularly because breached gloves compromise containment and operator safety.

maintenance.name: Inspect seals and gaskets
maintenance.statement: Inspect door seals, gaskets, and transfer port seals for wear or damage.
maintenance.description: Visually and physically examine all elastomeric and mechanical sealing surfaces for degradation, cracking, or deformation.

maintenance.name: Inspect viewing panels
maintenance.statement: Inspect the isolator windows and viewing panels for cracks or clouding.
maintenance.description: Panel integrity is part of the containment barrier and affects operator visibility.

maintenance.name: Lubricate mechanical moving parts
maintenance.statement: Lubricate the isolator's mechanical moving parts.
maintenance.description: Apply appropriate lubricants to hinges, latches, port mechanisms, and other moving components to prevent wear and ensure smooth, contamination-free operation.

maintenance.name: Record maintenance and calibration history
maintenance.statement: Record maintenance, calibration, and requalification activities for the isolator.
maintenance.description: Maintain a complete and auditable log of all maintenance interventions, calibration events, and requalification outcomes for the isolator.

maintenance.name: Register warranty coverage
maintenance.statement: Register the isolator with the manufacturer to activate warranty and support entitlements.
maintenance.description: Completes the formal product registration process so that the purchaser's warranty rights, service agreements, and technical support access are activated.

maintenance.name: Repair mechanical faults
maintenance.statement: Repair failed or damaged mechanical components of the isolator.
maintenance.description: Diagnose and restore failed transfer ports, doors, hinges, latches, or other mechanical assemblies to operational condition.

maintenance.name: Replace HEPA filters
maintenance.statement: Replace the isolator's HEPA or ULPA filter elements.
maintenance.description: Matters when filters reach end of life or fail integrity testing to maintain containment air quality.

maintenance.name: Replace air filtration media
maintenance.statement: Replace the isolator's HEPA, ULPA, or other air filtration media at end of service life or following a failed integrity test.
maintenance.description: Remove and safely dispose of exhausted or failed high-efficiency filtration units and install qualified replacement media to restore specified air-handling performance.
maintenance.frequency: Periodic, based on manufacturer intervals or test results

maintenance.name: Replace glove and sleeve assemblies
maintenance.statement: Replace worn or breached glove and sleeve assemblies on the isolator.
maintenance.description: Remove degraded glove and half-suit sleeve components and install new assemblies to maintain containment integrity.

maintenance.name: Replace worn gloves
maintenance.statement: Replace damaged or expired isolator glove and sleeve assemblies.
maintenance.description: Matters when gloves fail inspection to restore a leak-tight barrier.

maintenance.name: Replenish decontaminant supply
maintenance.statement: Replenish the sterilant, biocide, or cleaning agent used by the isolator.
maintenance.description: Matters when consumables run low to keep decontamination cycles available.

maintenance.name: Replenish sterilant supply
maintenance.statement: Replenish the sterilant agent supply used for chamber decontamination.
maintenance.description: Refill or replace the sterilant consumable reservoir to ensure decontamination cycles can be completed.

maintenance.name: Requalify containment performance
maintenance.statement: Requalify the isolator's containment and sterility performance.
maintenance.description: Execute formal requalification protocols to confirm the isolator continues to meet defined containment and environmental standards.

maintenance.name: Requalify isolator containment performance
maintenance.statement: Requalify the isolator against its containment and cleanliness specifications.
maintenance.description: Conduct periodic requalification testing to verify the isolator continues to meet its validated containment performance and cleanliness classification.

maintenance.name: Schedule preventive maintenance
maintenance.statement: Schedule preventive maintenance for the isolator.
maintenance.description: Plan and coordinate recurring preventive maintenance activities for the isolator to minimise unplanned downtime and sustain regulatory compliance.

maintenance.name: Schedule preventive service
maintenance.statement: Schedule preventive maintenance intervals for the isolator system.
maintenance.description: Planned servicing avoids unplanned downtime and keeps the unit in a qualified state.

maintenance.name: Service air-handling components
maintenance.statement: Service the isolator's air-handling components.
maintenance.description: Inspect, clean, lubricate, and replace blowers, dampers, ducting, and other air-handling elements to maintain airflow performance.

maintenance.name: Service isolator airflow system
maintenance.statement: Service the isolator airflow system.
maintenance.description: Inspect, clean, and maintain the isolator's airflow components — including fans, ducting, and flow paths — to sustain proper directional airflow and containment.

maintenance.name: Service transfer port and pass-through systems
maintenance.statement: Service the isolator's transfer ports and pass-through systems.
maintenance.description: Perform maintenance on transfer port mechanisms and pass-through hatches to ensure reliable, contamination-free material transfer.

maintenance.name: Store isolator for extended non-use
maintenance.statement: Store the isolator correctly during extended periods of non-use.
maintenance.description: Prepare and store the isolator according to proper shutdown procedures — including decontamination, desiccation, and protective measures — when it will not be operated for an extended period.

maintenance.name: Test enclosure leak tightness
maintenance.statement: Test the isolator enclosure for leak tightness.
maintenance.description: Conduct a leak-tightness test on the isolator enclosure to verify containment integrity is maintained within specification.

maintenance.name: Test enclosure leak-tightness
maintenance.statement: Perform a pressure-based leak test on the isolator enclosure.
maintenance.description: Conduct a pressure decay or equivalent leak-rate test on the full enclosure boundary to verify containment.

maintenance.name: Test glove and sleeve integrity
maintenance.statement: Test the isolator gloves and sleeves for leaks or breaches.
maintenance.description: Perform pressure or tracer-based integrity tests on all glove and sleeve assemblies to confirm containment.

maintenance.name: Test isolator alarm functions
maintenance.statement: Test the isolator alarm functions.
maintenance.description: Verify that all alarm and alert systems on the isolator activate correctly in response to out-of-specification conditions.

maintenance.name: Update control software
maintenance.statement: Update the isolator's control system software or firmware.
maintenance.description: Install revised software or firmware versions on the isolator control system to maintain functionality, security, and compliance.

maintenance.name: Update control system software
maintenance.statement: Update the isolator's control system software.
maintenance.description: Install available software or firmware updates to the isolator's control system to maintain functionality, security, and compliance.

maintenance.name: Verify protective control functions
maintenance.statement: Verify each protective control function of the isolator operates correctly.
maintenance.description: Test interlocks, alarms, emergency stops, and other engineered protective controls to confirm correct operation.

acquisition.name: Activate plant entitlements
acquisition.statement: Activate the purchased licenses, warranties, and service entitlements associated with the liquid product processing plant.
acquisition.description: Registers the plant with the supplier to unlock warranty coverage, software licenses, remote monitoring access, or support contracts included in the purchase.

acquisition.name: Arrange delivery logistics
acquisition.statement: Arrange delivery and logistics for the batch preparation system.
acquisition.description: Coordinate transportation, rigging, site-access, and delivery scheduling for the batch preparation system from the supplier to the installation site.

acquisition.name: Arrange pilot trial
acquisition.statement: Arrange and conduct a pilot or demonstration trial of each candidate plant with representative viscous product material.
acquisition.description: Organises and executes hands-on trials using the actual or representative viscous product on a candidate plant to empirically confirm processing performance before purchase commitment.
acquisition.userGroup: Process engineers, product development teams, procurement managers

acquisition.name: Arrange skid transport
acquisition.statement: Arrange transport and rigging of the skid to the receiving facility.
acquisition.description: Coordinate logistics providers, rigging contractors, and route surveys required to move the completed skid from the vendor's facility to the customer's site.

acquisition.name: Assess capacity requirements
acquisition.statement: Assess the batch capacity and throughput requirements the system must meet.
acquisition.description: Determine the volume, throughput, cycle time, and scalability requirements that a batch preparation system must satisfy before evaluating options.

acquisition.name: Assess customization scope
acquisition.statement: Assess the scope and feasibility of customization required for the skid.
acquisition.description: Determine what engineering modifications, material substitutions, or configuration changes must be made to a vendor's standard skid design in order to meet site-specific requirements.

acquisition.name: Assess process and material compatibility
acquisition.statement: Assess each candidate system's wetted materials, process conditions, and fluid chemistry suitability against the intended application.
acquisition.description: Combines wetted-material compatibility verification (#2) with process-condition and fluid-chemistry compatibility assessment (#21), which are inseparable aspects of the same compatibility evaluation activity.
acquisition.userGroup: Process engineers, materials engineers, procurement leads.
acquisition.frequency: Once per acquisition event, per candidate system evaluated.

acquisition.name: Assess site compatibility
acquisition.statement: Assess the proposed liquid product processing plant for compatibility with the installation site.
acquisition.description: This determines whether the proposed plant fits available space, utilities, interfaces, access routes, and environmental conditions.

acquisition.name: Assess site integration
acquisition.statement: Assess site integration constraints — including footprint, utility requirements, and interface conditions — for each candidate plant.
acquisition.description: Evaluates whether the candidate plant physically and operationally fits the intended facility, covering floor space, utility connections, structural loads, and interface requirements with existing site infrastructure.
acquisition.userGroup: Facilities engineers, project engineers, site planners
acquisition.frequency: Once per candidate plant during evaluation

acquisition.name: Compare competing systems
acquisition.statement: Compare competing batch preparation systems on specifications, features, and price.
acquisition.description: Evaluate and rank candidate batch preparation systems against each other using defined technical, operational, and commercial criteria.

acquisition.name: Compare plant configurations
acquisition.statement: Compare competing plant configurations against capacity, footprint, utility, and process-fit criteria.
acquisition.description: Systematic evaluation of alternative plant designs to identify the best-fit option before purchase.

acquisition.name: Compare throughput capacity
acquisition.statement: Compare the throughput and capacity ratings of competing plant offerings.
acquisition.description: Evaluate and rank candidate plants by their rated throughput, batch or continuous capacity, and scalability to processing requirements.

acquisition.name: Compare vendor proposals
acquisition.statement: Compare competing suppliers' specifications, pricing, terms, and technical documentation for the batching system.
acquisition.description: Requesting technical documentation (#19) is a step performed within vendor proposal comparison (#4). The output — a comparative assessment of suppliers — is identical. Retaining #4's framing, enriched to include documentation.
acquisition.userGroup: Procurement leads, process engineers, project managers.

acquisition.name: Complete purchase transaction
acquisition.statement: Complete the purchase transaction and finalize the order agreement.
acquisition.description: Completing the purchase agreement formalizes the transaction and delivery terms.

acquisition.name: Configure system options
acquisition.statement: Configure the required system options and specifications for the intended installation.
acquisition.description: Select and specify the configuration options, accessories, and technical parameters of the batch preparation system to match operational and site requirements before placing an order.

acquisition.name: Confirm controls compatibility
acquisition.statement: Confirm the batch preparation system's compatibility with existing control and communication infrastructure.
acquisition.description: Assess whether the candidate system's control interfaces, communication protocols, data formats, and security requirements are compatible with the site's existing automation and IT infrastructure.

acquisition.name: Confirm lead time and availability
acquisition.statement: Confirm the supplier's manufacturing lead time and component availability for the specified system configuration.
acquisition.description: Obtain a firm lead-time commitment and confirm that long-lead components (e.g., specialised instrumentation, custom vessels) are available to meet the project schedule.

acquisition.name: Confirm regulatory compliance
acquisition.statement: Confirm the system meets applicable regulatory and industry compliance standards.
acquisition.description: Regulated industries require the system to meet applicable hygiene and safety standards.

acquisition.name: Confirm spare-parts and service availability
acquisition.statement: Confirm the availability of spare parts and field service support from candidate suppliers.
acquisition.description: Verify that the vendor can supply critical spare parts and provide qualified field service technicians within acceptable lead times, as part of the pre-purchase evaluation.

acquisition.name: Coordinate delivery logistics and track progress
acquisition.statement: Coordinate shipment, freight, delivery scheduling, and progress tracking for plant components.
acquisition.description: Manages all outbound logistics from supplier to site, including freight arrangement, scheduling, and real-time progress monitoring, to ensure plant components arrive on time and in the correct sequence.
acquisition.userGroup: Procurement managers, project engineers, logistics coordinators
acquisition.frequency: Once per acquisition, throughout the delivery period

acquisition.name: Define plant specifications
acquisition.statement: Define the required specifications for the viscous product processing plant.
acquisition.description: Document the process, capacity, material-handling, utility, and compliance specifications that candidate plants must meet.

acquisition.name: Define process requirements
acquisition.statement: Define the process requirements the skid must satisfy before vendor engagement.
acquisition.description: Establish the functional, performance, and capacity requirements that any candidate skid must meet, forming the baseline for evaluation and procurement.

acquisition.name: Define system requirements
acquisition.statement: Define the required characteristics of the batch preparation system.
acquisition.description: Establish the technical, operational, regulatory, and site requirements that a batch preparation system must meet, forming the basis for supplier selection and evaluation.

acquisition.name: Evaluate and benchmark candidate skid options
acquisition.statement: Compare competing skid configurations, benchmark total cost of ownership, and assess vendor lead times against defined project requirements and milestones.
acquisition.description: Consolidates technical configuration comparison, cost-of-ownership benchmarking, and lead-time/schedule assessment into one structured evaluation activity performed before vendor selection.
acquisition.userGroup: Engineering, procurement, and project management teams conducting vendor selection.
acquisition.frequency: Once per acquisition cycle, during the vendor evaluation phase.

acquisition.name: Evaluate capacity and specification fit
acquisition.statement: Evaluate whether candidate systems' capacity, throughput, and technical specifications match defined production requirements.
acquisition.description: Combines evaluation of batch capacity and throughput (#1) with comparison of technical specifications against requirements (#20), which are performed in the same decision activity and overlap in scope.
acquisition.userGroup: Process engineers, procurement leads, project managers.

acquisition.name: Evaluate process fit
acquisition.statement: Evaluate whether a candidate plant fits the target liquid's physical and chemical properties.
acquisition.description: Matters when confirming the plant will handle the specific liquid's viscosity, temperature, and chemistry.

acquisition.name: Evaluate regulatory compliance evidence
acquisition.statement: Evaluate compliance documentation, certifications, and regulatory evidence supplied by candidate system suppliers.
acquisition.description: Combines confirming the system meets certification standards (#3) with evaluating the compliance documentation and evidence supplied (#23). Both jobs occur in the same evaluation step and address the same compliance gate.
acquisition.userGroup: Procurement leads, quality engineers, regulatory affairs personnel.
acquisition.frequency: Once per acquisition event, per candidate system evaluated.

acquisition.name: Evaluate regulatory compliance of candidate skid
acquisition.statement: Evaluate whether a candidate skid meets applicable regulatory and quality standards by reviewing the compliance evidence supplied by the prospective supplier.
acquisition.description: Assesses regulatory conformance of candidate skids by examining supplier-provided documentation, certificates, and test records against applicable standards and organizational quality requirements.
acquisition.userGroup: Quality assurance and regulatory affairs personnel supporting the acquisition.
acquisition.frequency: Once per vendor evaluated, during the acquisition selection phase.

acquisition.name: Evaluate rental options
acquisition.statement: Evaluate rental or lease arrangements for temporary or trial batching capacity.
acquisition.description: Some batching needs are temporary or trial, making rental or lease options relevant.

acquisition.name: Evaluate total cost of ownership
acquisition.statement: Evaluate the total cost of ownership for the processing plant.
acquisition.description: Assess capital, installation, operating, maintenance, and decommissioning costs over the plant's projected service life.

acquisition.name: Execute purchase contract
acquisition.statement: Execute the purchase contract for the selected viscous product processing plant.
acquisition.description: Formally sign and execute the agreed purchase contract with the selected plant supplier, completing the legal commitment to acquire the plant.

acquisition.name: Inspect delivered equipment
acquisition.statement: Receive and inspect delivered plant components for transit damage and conformance to the order specification and purchase documentation.
acquisition.description: Covers the complete goods-receipt inspection: checking for physical transit damage, verifying delivered configuration against purchase documentation, and reconciling components against the order specification — all performed at the point of delivery.
acquisition.userGroup: Site engineers, project managers, receiving inspection teams

acquisition.name: Inspect received equipment
acquisition.statement: Inspect the delivered plant equipment for completeness, specification conformity, and transit damage against the purchase order and bill of materials.
acquisition.description: Confirms that all ordered items have arrived undamaged and match the agreed specification and BOM before acceptance.
acquisition.userGroup: Procurement managers, site engineers, quality inspectors.

acquisition.name: Issue purchase order
acquisition.statement: Issue the purchase order for the selected skid.
acquisition.description: Formal transaction step authorizing the vendor to begin fabrication.

acquisition.name: Negotiate purchase contract
acquisition.statement: Negotiate the commercial, contractual, and warranty terms for acquiring the liquid product processing plant.
acquisition.description: Establishes the agreed price, scope of supply, delivery schedule, warranty, and legal terms with the chosen supplier.
acquisition.userGroup: Procurement managers, legal/commercial teams, capital project managers.

acquisition.name: Negotiate purchase terms
acquisition.statement: Negotiate purchase terms, pricing, and delivery schedule.
acquisition.description: Finalizes commercial conditions before issuing a purchase order.

acquisition.name: Obtain operating documentation and credentials
acquisition.statement: Obtain operating documentation and control-system access credentials for the plant.
acquisition.description: Collect all supplied technical manuals, process documentation, and control-system login credentials needed to operate and administer the plant.

acquisition.name: Obtain plant documentation
acquisition.statement: Obtain the technical documentation, drawings, and manuals for the plant.
acquisition.description: Matters when documentation and drawings must be received and cataloged for the plant.

acquisition.name: Obtain procurement approval
acquisition.statement: Obtain internal procurement and budget approval for the purchase.
acquisition.description: Procurement approval is typically required before a capital equipment order proceeds.

acquisition.name: Place plant order
acquisition.statement: Place the order for the selected liquid product processing plant.
acquisition.description: This creates the formal purchase commitment using the approved scope, configuration, price, schedule, and contractual terms.

acquisition.name: Place system order
acquisition.statement: Place the purchase order for the approved liquid batching and preparation system.
acquisition.description: Issue the formal purchase order to the selected supplier, confirming configuration, price, delivery schedule, and contractual terms.

acquisition.name: Qualify prospective supplier
acquisition.statement: Qualify the prospective batch preparation system supplier.
acquisition.description: This determines whether the supplier can satisfy required manufacturing quality, documentation, training, service, spare-parts, and delivery obligations.

acquisition.name: Qualify prospective suppliers
acquisition.statement: Qualify prospective suppliers of liquid batching and preparation systems against organisational procurement criteria.
acquisition.description: Assess supplier financial stability, quality management systems, manufacturing capability, references, and compliance with procurement standards before approving them for competitive evaluation.

acquisition.name: Receive and verify delivered skid
acquisition.statement: Receive the process skid at the facility, inspect it for transit damage and completeness, and verify conformity to the approved purchase order.
acquisition.description: Covers physical receipt of the skid shipment, inspection for damage or missing items, and confirmation that what was delivered matches the purchase order specifications and approved configuration.
acquisition.userGroup: Procurement, engineering, and quality personnel responsible for goods receipt.
acquisition.frequency: Once per skid acquisition, at time of delivery.

acquisition.name: Receive, inspect, and verify shipment
acquisition.statement: Receive the delivered batch preparation system, inspect it for shipping damage or missing components, and verify it against the order for completeness and correctness.
acquisition.description: Upon delivery, the buyer physically receives the system, checks each item against the purchase order and packing list, and examines all components for transit damage, incorrect parts, or missing items before accepting the consignment.
acquisition.userGroup: Receiving/logistics staff, procurement team, site installation coordinator.

acquisition.name: Register equipment warranty
acquisition.statement: Register the plant with the manufacturer to activate warranty and support.
acquisition.description: Warranty and support activation registers the plant with the manufacturer.

acquisition.name: Register the system
acquisition.statement: Register the acquired system with the vendor for warranty and support.
acquisition.description: Complete vendor registration of the batch preparation system to activate warranty coverage, technical support entitlements, and recall notifications.

acquisition.name: Request formal quotation
acquisition.statement: Request a formal quotation for the specified batch preparation system.
acquisition.description: A formal quote establishes pricing, terms, and lead time for the capital purchase.

acquisition.name: Request quotations
acquisition.statement: Request quotations and proposals for a liquid product processing plant.
acquisition.description: Formally invites shortlisted suppliers to submit pricing, delivery, and scope-of-supply proposals.

acquisition.name: Request supplier quotation
acquisition.statement: Request a quotation for the selected liquid batching and preparation system configuration.
acquisition.description: This obtains pricing, scope, options, exclusions, lead time, payment terms, warranty coverage, and delivery conditions for procurement review.

acquisition.name: Request technical proposal
acquisition.statement: Request a technical proposal and quotation tailored to process requirements.
acquisition.description: Solicit detailed technical proposals and pricing quotations from shortlisted plant suppliers based on defined process specifications.

acquisition.name: Research available system types
acquisition.statement: Research available batch preparation system types and configurations from prospective suppliers.
acquisition.description: Identify and gather information on the range of batch preparation system types, configurations, and suppliers present in the market.

acquisition.name: Research plant configurations
acquisition.statement: Research available viscous product processing plant configurations and technologies.
acquisition.description: Identify and review the range of plant configurations, processing technologies, and supplier offerings available on the market.

acquisition.name: Research plant options and suppliers
acquisition.statement: Research available liquid product processing plant configurations and the vendors capable of supplying them.
acquisition.description: Identifies the landscape of plant options, technology variants, and qualified suppliers to inform the evaluation and selection process.
acquisition.userGroup: Process engineers, procurement managers, capital project teams.
acquisition.frequency: Once per acquisition project, with periodic refresh as market changes.

acquisition.name: Research skid vendors
acquisition.statement: Research available process skid vendors and supply configurations.
acquisition.description: Identify and evaluate the landscape of vendors that fabricate and supply biopharmaceutical process skids, including their product ranges and track records.

acquisition.name: Review acquisition scope
acquisition.statement: Review the quoted acquisition scope for the liquid batching and preparation system.
acquisition.description: This identifies omissions, substitutions, assumptions, interfaces, optional components, documentation deliverables, and commercial limitations before commitment.

acquisition.name: Review design documentation
acquisition.statement: Review vendor-supplied design documentation and engineering drawings before purchase commitment.
acquisition.description: Examine the technical drawings, P&IDs, bill of materials, and other design deliverables provided by the vendor to confirm the proposed skid meets specifications prior to order placement.

acquisition.name: Secure capital approval
acquisition.statement: Secure internal budget and capital expenditure approval for the plant.
acquisition.description: Capital purchases require internal approval and budget sign-off before ordering.

acquisition.name: Secure purchase approval
acquisition.statement: Obtain budget authorization and complete the procurement approval process for the system.
acquisition.description: Large capital equipment purchases require formal budgeting, approval, and procurement processes.

acquisition.name: Seek peer recommendations
acquisition.statement: Seek recommendations and references from operators of comparable plants.
acquisition.description: Buyers seek references and reviews from operators with similar viscous product needs.

acquisition.name: Seek peer references
acquisition.statement: Seek references from existing users of candidate liquid batching and preparation systems.
acquisition.description: Contact reference customers provided by shortlisted suppliers, or peer networks, to gather first-hand operational experience with the system before committing to purchase.

acquisition.name: Seek vendor references
acquisition.statement: Seek references and reviews on vendor reliability and system performance.
acquisition.description: Peer feedback and case references reduce risk in a high-cost capital purchase.

acquisition.name: Shortlist qualified suppliers
acquisition.statement: Shortlist liquid processing plant suppliers that meet minimum technical, financial, and delivery capability criteria.
acquisition.description: Reduces the full supplier market to a qualified subset for detailed evaluation and proposal solicitation, typically based on pre-qualification questionnaires or capability assessments.

acquisition.name: Solicit vendor proposals
acquisition.statement: Solicit formal technical proposals and price quotations from shortlisted process skid suppliers.
acquisition.description: Combines the request for formal quotes and the solicitation of technical proposals into one outbound vendor engagement step that yields comparable commercial and technical submissions.
acquisition.userGroup: Procurement and engineering teams managing the vendor selection process.
acquisition.frequency: Once per acquisition cycle, prior to proposal evaluation.

acquisition.name: Specify system configuration
acquisition.statement: Specify the required components, options, and configuration for the batching system order.
acquisition.description: Custom-engineered systems require specification of options, materials, and configuration before ordering.

acquisition.name: Track order status
acquisition.statement: Track the manufacturing and shipment status of the ordered system.
acquisition.description: The buyer needs visibility into fabrication and shipment progress on a long lead-time order.

acquisition.name: Track plant delivery
acquisition.statement: Track fabrication progress and shipment status of the ordered liquid product processing plant through to site arrival.
acquisition.description: Maintains visibility of plant build and logistics milestones to anticipate delays and coordinate site readiness.
acquisition.userGroup: Procurement managers, project managers, site logistics coordinators.

acquisition.name: Track skid delivery
acquisition.statement: Track the delivery status of the skid en route to the facility.
acquisition.description: Keeps the project team informed to coordinate site readiness.

acquisition.name: Track system shipment
acquisition.statement: Track the shipment of the liquid batching and preparation system.
acquisition.description: This allows receiving personnel to prepare for arrival while responding to delays, split shipments, route changes, or special handling events.

acquisition.name: Validate process compatibility
acquisition.statement: Validate each candidate plant against the specified process envelope, including the target product's viscosity range and operating conditions.
acquisition.description: Assesses whether a candidate plant can process the target viscous product across the full required process envelope — viscosity range, temperature, pressure, shear, and throughput — to confirm technical suitability before selection.
acquisition.frequency: Once per candidate plant during the evaluation phase

acquisition.name: Validate supplier capability
acquisition.statement: Validate each supplier's capability to provide the liquid product processing plant.
acquisition.description: This reduces acquisition risk by examining relevant experience, engineering resources, fabrication capacity, references, and service coverage.

acquisition.name: Validate system performance
acquisition.statement: Validate the candidate batch preparation system under representative operating conditions.
acquisition.description: Conduct trials, demonstrations, or factory acceptance tests of the candidate system using representative materials and process conditions to confirm it meets defined performance requirements before purchase.

acquisition.name: Verify compliance certifications
acquisition.statement: Verify that each candidate plant holds the required hygiene, safety, regulatory, and conformity certifications.
acquisition.description: Confirms that candidate plants carry all mandatory compliance certifications and conformity evidence — including hygiene, safety, and regulatory approvals — relevant to the intended application and jurisdiction.
acquisition.userGroup: Quality managers, regulatory affairs teams, procurement engineers
acquisition.frequency: Once per candidate plant during evaluation

acquisition.name: Verify material compatibility
acquisition.statement: Verify the system's compatibility with the intended batch materials and formulations.
acquisition.description: Confirm that the candidate batch preparation system can handle the specific materials, chemistries, viscosities, temperatures, or other formulation properties required.

acquisition.name: Verify order documentation
acquisition.statement: Verify that all required order and turnover documentation accompanies the delivered skid.
acquisition.description: Confirm receipt and completeness of manuals, certificates, test reports, and other required documents delivered with the skid as specified in the purchase order.

acquisition.name: Verify site compatibility
acquisition.statement: Verify the batch preparation system against site space, utility, environmental, and structural constraints.
acquisition.description: Confirm that the candidate batch preparation system can be physically installed and operated within the available facility footprint, utility supply, environmental controls, and structural load limits.

acquisition.name: Verify utility compatibility
acquisition.statement: Verify the skid's compatibility with existing facility utilities and interfaces.
acquisition.description: Confirm that the candidate skid's utility connections, electrical requirements, and physical interfaces align with what the receiving facility provides, prior to committing to purchase.

acquisition.name: Witness factory acceptance test
acquisition.statement: Witness the factory acceptance test of the completed skid prior to shipment.
acquisition.description: Attend and observe the vendor-conducted factory acceptance test (FAT) to verify the skid performs to specification before it leaves the manufacturing facility.

acquisition.name: Witness system demonstration
acquisition.statement: Witness a demonstration of a candidate liquid batching and preparation system.
acquisition.description: This provides direct evidence of system operation, control behavior, batching performance, accessibility, and operator interaction before purchase.

usage.name: Adjust batch parameters
usage.statement: Adjust batch preparation parameters mid-cycle to correct for observed process variation.
usage.description: Operator modifies active process parameters during a running batch cycle in response to observed deviations from target conditions.

usage.name: Adjust control setpoints during operation
usage.statement: Adjust an authorized control setpoint during active skid operation.
usage.description: Modify a process control setpoint on the skid within authorized limits while the skid is actively running a process.

usage.name: Adjust mixing parameters
usage.statement: Adjust mixing parameters during the batch to achieve target consistency.
usage.description: Tuning speed, temperature, or timing during use accommodates ingredient behavior.

usage.name: Adjust process setpoints
usage.statement: Adjust an active process setpoint — including temperature, flow, timing, and dosing parameters — through the batching system controls.
usage.description: Combines controlling batch temperature (#56) with adjusting process setpoints (#74). Temperature control is a specific instance of setpoint adjustment; #74 is the correct Level-1 framing that subsumes #56.

usage.name: Adjust processing sequence for alternate formulation
usage.statement: Adapt the active processing sequence to handle a different viscous product formulation mid-run.
usage.description: The operator modifies setpoints, stage durations, or routing to switch the plant from one product grade or recipe to another without a full shutdown, covering partial changeovers or recipe transitions during a shift.

usage.name: Adjust processing throughput
usage.statement: Scale the plant's processing volume up or down during operation.
usage.description: Modify the plant's operating rate in response to demand, feed availability, or downstream capacity.

usage.name: Back up process data
usage.statement: Back up the process control and batch data generated by the processing plant.
usage.description: Process data logs must be backed up to preserve batch records and support traceability.

usage.name: Charge input materials
usage.statement: Charge input materials into the skid.
usage.description: Raw material, buffer, or media must be introduced into the skid at the correct point and rate for the process step.

usage.name: Clean batch system between runs
usage.statement: Clear residual material from the batch preparation system between consecutive batch cycles.
usage.description: Operator or cleaning system flushes, rinses, or purges residual batch material from the system's wetted components between production runs to prevent cross-contamination or carryover.

usage.name: Collect in-process samples
usage.statement: Withdraw an in-process sample through the skid's designated sampling interface.
usage.description: Collect a representative process sample via the skid's sampling port or assembly during an active run for quality or process monitoring purposes.

usage.name: Confirm batch system readiness before start
usage.statement: Verify that the batch preparation system is in a ready state before initiating a batch cycle.
usage.description: Operator checks that all system interlocks, material levels, and pre-conditions are satisfied before triggering the batch start, ensuring the system is safe and configured to run.

usage.name: Contain process spills
usage.statement: Contain liquid spills or leaks that occur during active plant operation.
usage.description: Identify and isolate unintended liquid releases at processing equipment, valves, or pipework to prevent product loss and maintain safe operation.

usage.name: Control material transfer
usage.statement: Control the skid's transfer of process material between vessels or process steps.
usage.description: Manage the initiation, rate, volume, and completion of process material transfers executed through the skid.

usage.name: Control process conditions
usage.statement: Control temperature, pressure, or timing conditions throughout the active batch cycle.
usage.description: Operator or control system manages key process variables such as temperature, pressure, and cycle timing to keep the batch within specified limits.

usage.name: Control process parameters
usage.statement: Regulate and adjust plant setpoints to maintain the required operating state during the processing cycle.
usage.description: Ensures temperature, pressure, flow, and other process variables remain within specified bounds throughout the run.

usage.name: Coordinate concurrent processing streams
usage.statement: Coordinate multiple simultaneous liquid processing streams within the plant.
usage.description: Manage and synchronise parallel processing lines or split-stream operations running at the same time, balancing loads and sequencing appropriately.

usage.name: Coordinate multi-operator use
usage.statement: Coordinate batching system use across multiple operators during production.
usage.description: Manage task handoffs, access, and sequencing when more than one operator is working with the batching system simultaneously or across shifts.

usage.name: Coordinate shift handover
usage.statement: Transfer operational control and status between operating shifts.
usage.description: Multiple operators, shifts, and control-room staff must coordinate control of the plant during operation.

usage.name: Coordinate shift operation
usage.statement: Coordinate operation of the plant across operators and shifts.
usage.description: Coordinating multiple operators and shifts across the plant maintains continuous, consistent production.

usage.name: Coordinate skid with connected process units
usage.statement: Coordinate the skid's operation with connected upstream and downstream process units.
usage.description: Synchronize material flows, timing, and status signals between the skid and interfaced upstream/downstream equipment during operation.

usage.name: Diagnose operating faults from plant indications
usage.statement: Identify the source of an operating fault from plant indications.
usage.description: The process technician interprets alarms, instrument readings, and observable plant behaviour to pinpoint the cause of a fault during or following a processing run.

usage.name: Diagnose process deviations
usage.statement: Diagnose a process deviation from the system's operational data.
usage.description: This helps isolate abnormal ingredient delivery, vessel conditions, control responses, or equipment behavior during use.

usage.name: Discharge completed batch
usage.statement: Discharge the completed batch from the system to downstream handling.
usage.description: Transferring the finished batch out frees the system and delivers the product.

usage.name: Discharge finished batch
usage.statement: Discharge the finished batch from the system to downstream processes.
usage.description: Operate the system's discharge or transfer function to move the completed liquid batch to the next process step, storage, or filling line.

usage.name: Discharge process output
usage.statement: Discharge the process output from the skid.
usage.description: Finished product or intermediate must be transferred out of the skid at the end of a process step.

usage.name: Discharge processed material from plant
usage.statement: Manage the discharge and collection of processed viscous product from the plant.
usage.description: The operator oversees the release of finished or intermediate viscous material from the plant through designated output paths, containers, or transfer lines.

usage.name: Divert nonconforming output
usage.statement: Divert nonconforming liquid output away from the normal discharge path.
usage.description: This prevents unsuitable output from entering downstream storage or distribution during process deviations.

usage.name: Document batch record
usage.statement: Document run data and events in the batch record during operation.
usage.description: Record process events, deviations, operator actions, and run data in the official batch record throughout the process run.

usage.name: Dose measured quantities
usage.statement: Dose measured quantities of each ingredient into the batch preparation system during preparation.
usage.description: Operator or automated system dispenses precise, pre-specified quantities of each material into the batch during the active preparation cycle.

usage.name: Dose processing inputs
usage.statement: Dose required processing inputs into the liquid stream.
usage.description: This applies when the plant introduces additives, treatment agents, catalysts, or other process inputs.

usage.name: Draw in-process sample
usage.statement: Draw an in-process sample from the system's designated sampling point.
usage.description: This supports intermediate evaluation of the liquid before the batch cycle is completed.

usage.name: Enter batch recipe
usage.statement: Enter the recipe or formulation parameters for the batch to be prepared.
usage.description: Operator inputs the target formulation or recipe into the batch preparation system prior to initiating the batch cycle.

usage.name: Execute addition sequence
usage.statement: Execute the batching system's programmed ingredient-addition sequence.
usage.description: Combines operating the agitation function (#55) with executing the ingredient-addition sequence (#73). Agitation is an embedded function within the addition sequence execution; both are sub-activities of running a batch cycle, with #73 being the correct Level-1 framing.

usage.name: Execute clean-in-place cycle
usage.statement: Execute a clean-in-place cycle on the skid's wetted flow path.
usage.description: Running an automated or manual CIP sequence to clean internal flow paths without disassembly, using appropriate cleaning agents and validated procedures.

usage.name: Execute emergency shutdown
usage.statement: Execute the plant's emergency shutdown function when continued operation is unsafe.
usage.description: This rapidly places active processing equipment into a defined protective state during a critical event.

usage.name: Execute emergency stop
usage.statement: Stop the plant through its emergency control when continued operation is unsafe.
usage.description: Immediate shutdown limits escalation during hazardous process or equipment conditions.

usage.name: Execute production batch
usage.statement: Execute a production batch through the process skid.
usage.description: Operate the skid to run a full production batch from initiation to completion.

usage.name: Execute steam-in-place sterilization
usage.statement: Execute a steam-in-place sterilization cycle on the skid.
usage.description: Running an SIP sequence to sterilize the skid's internal fluid paths using pressurized steam, ensuring microbial control before processing.

usage.name: Feed viscous material into processing path
usage.statement: Introduce raw viscous material into the plant's active processing path.
usage.description: The operator loads or pumps feedstock into the inlet of the plant at the start of or during a production run.

usage.name: Halt batch on fault
usage.statement: Halt the batch cycle when a fault or unsafe condition is detected.
usage.description: Stopping the process quickly prevents damage or unsafe output during abnormal events.

usage.name: Handle input variation
usage.statement: Process and compensate for liquid inputs that vary in viscosity, composition, or temperature.
usage.description: Maintains stable plant operation and output quality when incoming feedstock properties deviate from nominal values.

usage.name: Handle input variations
usage.statement: Adjust plant settings to accommodate variations in incoming material properties.
usage.description: Operators must respond to shifts in raw material properties by tuning plant parameters mid-run.

usage.name: Initiate production run
usage.statement: Initiate a liquid product processing run on the plant.
usage.description: Start-up sequence to begin a new processing run, including confirming readiness and activating the plant.

usage.name: Load batch ingredients
usage.statement: Load the specified ingredients into the batch preparation system.
usage.description: Operator physically introduces raw materials or ingredients into the batch preparation system at the start of or during a batch cycle.

usage.name: Load ingredient components
usage.statement: Load ingredient components into the batching system for a new batch.
usage.description: Physically or automatically load raw liquid and/or dry ingredients into the system's intake for a new production batch.

usage.name: Manage output flow
usage.statement: Direct and control the finished liquid output from the plant.
usage.description: Finished liquid must be routed, dosed, or transferred to downstream vessels or filling equipment as it exits the plant.

usage.name: Manage residual liquid streams
usage.statement: Direct residual liquid streams to their designated handling paths.
usage.description: Route off-spec, waste, or by-product liquid streams to appropriate disposal, recycle, or containment systems during operation.

usage.name: Meter process additions
usage.statement: Deliver a measured process addition through the skid's integrated dosing functions.
usage.description: Command the skid's dosing system to deliver a precise, metered quantity of a process addition (e.g., buffer, reagent) during operation.

usage.name: Mix batch contents
usage.statement: Mix or blend the batch contents to achieve the required consistency.
usage.description: Combining materials to a uniform state is central to producing a usable batch.

usage.name: Monitor batch progress
usage.statement: Monitor batch progress and system status during operation.
usage.description: Observe live system indicators, alarms, and process data to track batch status and detect anomalies throughout the batching cycle.

usage.name: Monitor process conditions
usage.statement: Monitor real-time process conditions during operation.
usage.description: Continuous observation of temperature, pressure, flow, and other variables is required to ensure the process stays within specification.

usage.name: Monitor product viscosity in-process
usage.statement: Monitor the viscosity of the product at defined points during processing.
usage.description: The operator or technician observes inline or at-line viscosity measurements to verify the product remains within specification during the run.

usage.name: Monitor skid condition
usage.statement: Monitor the skid's operating condition.
usage.description: Tracking equipment health indicators such as vibration, temperature, and runtime hours to detect early signs of degradation and support predictive maintenance decisions.

usage.name: Operate at high volume
usage.statement: Operate the processing plant at high throughput during peak production.
usage.description: Running the plant at higher throughput supports peak demand periods while maintaining product quality.

usage.name: Operate for training
usage.statement: Operate the skid in a training or demonstration context.
usage.description: Trainees operate the skid under supervision or in simulation to learn correct procedures before independent use.

usage.name: Operate manual controls
usage.statement: Control an individual batch preparation system function in manual operating mode.
usage.description: Authorized operator directly actuates a specific system function using manual controls, bypassing automatic sequencing, to complete a step or respond to a system need.

usage.name: Operate system in manual mode
usage.statement: Operate the batching system in manual mode to complete a batch step.
usage.description: Bypass automated sequencing and control individual system functions (pumps, valves, agitators) manually to complete a batch step during trials, failures, or non-standard conditions.

usage.name: Operate system under supervision
usage.statement: Operate the batch preparation system under supervision during operator training.
usage.description: Trainee operator runs the batch preparation system under the direct oversight of an experienced operator or trainer to develop hands-on proficiency.

usage.name: Override automatic control manually
usage.statement: Apply an authorized manual override to an automatic skid control function.
usage.description: Temporarily place a skid automated control function into manual mode to apply a direct command, within authorized limits, during operation.

usage.name: Pause batch cycle
usage.statement: Suspend the active batch preparation cycle.
usage.description: This is required when processing must be temporarily halted without intentionally terminating the batch.

usage.name: Process liquid feedstock
usage.statement: Pass liquid feedstock through the plant's active processing equipment.
usage.description: The core act of running liquid material through unit operations — mixing, heating, separating, reacting — to transform it into finished product.

usage.name: Recirculate process material
usage.statement: Return viscous material through a designated processing loop.
usage.description: Recirculation is used when material requires additional residence time or repeated treatment.

usage.name: Record batch data
usage.statement: Record the batch preparation parameters and execution results for the production record.
usage.description: Operator or system captures process parameters, ingredient quantities, and cycle outcomes during or immediately following a batch run to create an auditable production record.

usage.name: Record processing parameters and results
usage.statement: Record processing parameters and results for each production run.
usage.description: The operator or technician logs key process variables (temperatures, pressures, flow rates, batch IDs) and outcome data to maintain a traceable production record.

usage.name: Record production data
usage.statement: Document processing parameters and output data for each run.
usage.description: Log key operating data — flows, temperatures, pressures, yields — during and after each processing run for traceability.

usage.name: Recover interrupted batch
usage.statement: Resume an interrupted batch from an approved system state.
usage.description: This enables controlled continuation after a stoppage without restarting the entire run.

usage.name: Recover interrupted process sequence
usage.statement: Resume skid operation from an authorized recovery point after an interruption.
usage.description: Identify an authorized recovery point and resume the process sequence on the skid following an unplanned interruption, alarm, or hold condition.

usage.name: Regulate material flow rate through plant
usage.statement: Regulate the rate of viscous material flow through the plant.
usage.description: Adjusts pump speeds, valve positions, or other flow-control elements to set and maintain the required volumetric or mass flow rate of viscous material through the processing plant.
usage.frequency: Continuously or as needed during each production run

usage.name: Regulate material temperature during processing
usage.statement: Regulate the temperature of viscous material at defined control points during plant operation.
