usage.description: Monitors and adjusts thermal inputs to the process — via heating, cooling, or jacketing systems — to maintain material temperature within the specified processing range throughout a production run.

usage.name: Regulate mixing intensity
usage.statement: Regulate the agitation intensity applied to viscous material within the processing vessel.
usage.description: The operator adjusts mixer speed or agitation mode to achieve required shear, dispersion, or homogeneity of the viscous product during processing.

usage.name: Replace single-use assemblies
usage.statement: Replace the single-use tubing, filters, and bag assemblies on the skid.
usage.description: Removal and disposal of expired or spent single-use fluid-path components and installation of new assemblies to restore skid functionality.

usage.name: Respond to process alarms
usage.statement: Respond to plant alarms during active liquid processing.
usage.description: This enables timely operator intervention when monitored conditions exceed configured operating limits.

usage.name: Respond to system alarms
usage.statement: Acknowledge an active batch-system alarm.
usage.description: This enables the operator to recognize an abnormal condition before taking the permitted operational response.

usage.name: Rinse between batches
usage.statement: Rinse the batching system's wetted surfaces between consecutive batch runs.
usage.description: Flush or rinse product-contact surfaces, lines, and vessels between batches of the same or different formulations to prevent cross-contamination or carry-over.

usage.name: Route liquid streams
usage.statement: Route liquid streams through the required plant flow path.
usage.description: This directs feedstock, intermediate streams, or output to the appropriate processing stages.

usage.name: Run precision batches
usage.statement: Run small precision batches of viscous product to specification.
usage.description: Small precision batches for specialty or trial formulations demand tight control of every parameter.

usage.name: Sample batch material
usage.statement: Withdraw a sample of in-process batch material for testing.
usage.description: Technician or operator draws a representative sample from the batch preparation system during an active cycle to support in-process quality or process checks.

usage.name: Sample process stream
usage.statement: Draw in-process samples for quality checks while the plant runs.
usage.description: Collect representative liquid samples from designated sampling points during active operation for analytical testing.

usage.name: Scale batch size
usage.statement: Scale the batch size to match the required production volume.
usage.description: Adjust the system's batch quantity and associated parameters to produce a larger or smaller volume than the default recipe specifies.

usage.name: Select batch recipe
usage.statement: Select the required recipe from the batch preparation system's recipe library.
usage.description: Operator chooses the appropriate pre-stored recipe from the system's available formulations before initiating a batch cycle.

usage.name: Sequence multiple batches
usage.statement: Sequence consecutive batches to sustain continuous production throughput.
usage.description: Operator or production scheduler plans and initiates a series of consecutive batch cycles to maintain uninterrupted downstream production flow.

usage.name: Set batch quantity
usage.statement: Enter the required batch quantity into the batch preparation system.
usage.description: Operator specifies the target output quantity or batch size in the system before initiating the batch cycle.

usage.name: Set process parameters
usage.statement: Set process parameters on the skid controller before or during a run.
usage.description: Configure control setpoints, limits, and sequence parameters on the skid's process controller prior to or during operation.

usage.name: Shut down processing line
usage.statement: Shut down the viscous product processing line at the end of production.
usage.description: Safely shutting down the line at end of production protects equipment and prevents material solidifying in the system.

usage.name: Shut down production run
usage.statement: Bring the processing plant to a controlled stop at end of run.
usage.description: Bringing a run to a controlled end protects product quality and equipment integrity.

usage.name: Start batch cycle
usage.statement: Initiate the batch preparation cycle on the system.
usage.description: Triggering the run begins the automated or manual sequence of preparation steps.

usage.name: Start batching cycle
usage.statement: Start the batching cycle to begin preparing the liquid batch.
usage.description: Initiate the automated or manual batching cycle sequence after recipe and quantity parameters are confirmed.

usage.name: Start up processing line
usage.statement: Start up the viscous product processing line to begin production.
usage.description: This matters whenever the plant is brought online to begin transforming raw viscous materials into product.

usage.name: Switch between recipes
usage.statement: Switch the batching system between different batch recipes during production.
usage.description: Combines setting batch recipe parameters before a run (#62) with switching between recipes during production (#67). Setting parameters before a run is a sub-step performed when switching between recipes; both address the same usage-phase recipe-selection activity.
usage.frequency: Per recipe change event during production.

usage.name: Switch product formulation
usage.statement: Switch the batch preparation system to a different product formulation between batch cycles.
usage.description: Operator reconfigures the batch system to run a different product formulation, including updating recipe parameters and verifying readiness before starting the next batch.

usage.name: Switch product formulations
usage.statement: Switch the plant between different viscous product formulations.
usage.description: Switching between different formulations or product grades requires reconfiguring the plant between runs.

usage.name: Switch skid configuration between products
usage.statement: Switch the skid configuration between different processes or products.
usage.description: Reconfigure the skid's flow path, parameters, and assemblies to accommodate a different process or product.

usage.name: Synchronize timing of connected processing stages
usage.statement: Coordinate the operating timing of connected processing stages.
usage.description: The control-room operator aligns the start, rate, and pacing of sequential or parallel processing stages to prevent bottlenecks, hold-ups, or material overflow between stages.

usage.name: Transfer batch to intermediate vessel
usage.statement: Transfer the prepared batch to an intermediate holding or surge vessel.
usage.description: Operate the system's transfer function to move a completed batch into a buffer tank or intermediate vessel when direct downstream processing is not immediately available.

usage.name: Transfer material between stages
usage.statement: Move viscous material from one processing stage to the next.
usage.description: Stage-to-stage transfer is required in plants containing sequential vessels or processing units.

usage.name: Trigger emergency shutdown
usage.statement: Activate the batch preparation system's emergency shutdown function.
usage.description: This provides immediate operational intervention when continued equipment activity presents an urgent hazard.

usage.name: Troubleshoot batching faults
usage.statement: Troubleshoot batching faults that occur during a production run.
usage.description: Identify and resolve faults or alarms that interrupt or degrade the batching process while the system is in operation.

usage.name: Troubleshoot process faults
usage.statement: Troubleshoot faults that arise during viscous product processing.
usage.description: Detecting and resolving faults such as blockages or pump cavitation keeps the line running during operation.

usage.name: Troubleshoot process upsets
usage.statement: Diagnose and correct process faults that occur during operation.
usage.description: Identify the root cause of deviations or upsets and apply corrective actions to restore normal processing.

usage.name: Verify batch accuracy
usage.statement: Verify that the batching system delivers the correct quantity and composition of each batch within specified tolerances.
usage.description: Periodic end-to-end accuracy verification of batch outputs against target recipes to confirm that cumulative instrument drift or mechanical wear has not degraded dosing performance.

usage.name: Verify batch endpoint
usage.statement: Verify the batch endpoint from the batching system's process readings.
usage.description: Confirm that the batch has reached its defined endpoint criteria using process readings available on the system before authorising discharge.

usage.name: Verify batch quality
usage.statement: Verify batch quality against specification during preparation.
usage.description: Sampling or in-line measurement during use confirms the batch meets specification.

usage.name: Verify ingredient dosing
usage.statement: Confirm each ingredient reaches its specified target quantity during batch preparation.
usage.description: Operator or automated system verifies that each ingredient has been dispensed to its specified batch quantity before or during the active cycle, ensuring formulation accuracy.

maintenance.name: Align rotating equipment
maintenance.statement: Align coupled rotating equipment within specified tolerances.
maintenance.description: Alignment is required after installation disturbance or detected vibration to prevent premature drivetrain wear.

maintenance.name: Back Up Control Configurations
maintenance.statement: Back up the plant's control configurations.
maintenance.description: Current backups allow controller programs, operating parameters, recipes, and interface settings to be restored after a failure or replacement.

maintenance.name: Back up control and recipe data
maintenance.statement: Back up batch recipes, calibration records, controller settings, user configurations, and event logs from the batching system's control platform.
maintenance.description: Periodic export and secure storage of all critical process data and system configurations to enable recovery from data loss, hardware failure, or software corruption.

maintenance.name: Back up control configurations
maintenance.statement: Back up the skid's control configurations.
maintenance.description: This enables recovery of validated settings, logic, parameters, and operator-interface configurations after a failure or change.

maintenance.name: Back up recipe data
maintenance.statement: Back up recipe, configuration, and batch record data.
maintenance.description: Recipe data, batch records, and configuration settings must be backed up to prevent loss.

maintenance.name: Calibrate measurement instruments
maintenance.statement: Calibrate the flow meters, load cells, level sensors, temperature probes, and dosing devices to their reference standards to maintain measurement accuracy.
maintenance.description: Combines initial calibration at commissioning (#44, Preparation) with periodic recalibration during service life (#80, Maintenance). Periodic recalibration is the recurring Maintenance job; initial calibration at commissioning is a sub-step of Preparation commissioning covered by #46. Homing the canonical job in Maintenance.
maintenance.userGroup: Instrumentation technicians, maintenance engineers, quality personnel.
maintenance.frequency: At scheduled calibration intervals (typically annually or per quality plan).

maintenance.name: Calibrate process instrumentation
maintenance.statement: Calibrate the plant's sensors, meters, and control instrumentation against traceable reference standards.
maintenance.description: Ensures measurement accuracy and regulatory traceability for all process instruments used in plant operation.
maintenance.frequency: At defined calibration intervals per instrument management plan.

maintenance.name: Calibrate process instruments
maintenance.statement: Calibrate the skid's sensors, meters, and control instruments.
maintenance.description: Performing scheduled calibration of all process measurement instruments on the skid to ensure accuracy and traceability to reference standards.

maintenance.name: Clean external skid surfaces
maintenance.statement: Clean the skid's external surfaces.
maintenance.description: Wiping down or washing the external housing, frames, and exposed panels of the skid to remove dust, spills, and surface contamination.

maintenance.name: Clean product-contact surfaces
maintenance.statement: Clean the interior surfaces of tanks, vessels, and product-contact components of the processing plant.
maintenance.description: Removal of residue, deposits, and contaminants from all surfaces that contact the liquid product to maintain hygiene and prevent cross-contamination.

maintenance.name: Clean, sanitize, and decontaminate product-contact surfaces
maintenance.statement: Clean, sanitize, and decontaminate the batch preparation system's product-contact vessels and surfaces to maintain hygienic conditions and release the system for continued or new service.
maintenance.description: Covers all hygiene restoration activities on product-contact surfaces and vessels, whether performed manually (clean and sanitize) or via automated clean-in-place cycle, and including decontamination before releasing the system from service. Executed between batch runs, on schedule, or as required by product-changeover or regulatory protocols.
maintenance.userGroup: Operators, sanitation/hygiene technicians, quality assurance personnel.
maintenance.frequency: Between batch runs and/or on a scheduled maintenance cycle; decontamination as required before service release.

maintenance.name: Descale heated surfaces
maintenance.statement: Descale and remove buildup from heat-transfer and jacketed surfaces.
maintenance.description: Thermal processing of viscous product leaves deposits that reduce heat transfer efficiency and must be removed.

maintenance.name: Document maintenance history
maintenance.statement: Document maintenance actions, part replacements, and service records for the plant.
maintenance.description: Accurate records support compliance, warranty, and predictive planning for the processing equipment.

maintenance.name: Drain and flush process lines
maintenance.statement: Drain and flush the batch preparation system's process lines prior to shutdown or product changeover.
maintenance.description: Evacuate residual product, solvents, or process fluids from pipework, valves, and vessels to prevent contamination, corrosion, or solidification during idle periods or between product runs.

maintenance.name: Flush and preserve plant circuits
maintenance.statement: Flush residual viscous material from pipes, pumps, and transfer lines, and preserve plant circuits prior to extended shutdown.
maintenance.description: Clears residual product and process fluids from the plant's transfer and process circuits using flush media, then applies preservation measures to protect internal surfaces and components during idle or extended shutdown periods.
maintenance.frequency: At end of each production campaign or before extended shutdown

maintenance.name: Inspect electrical and control connections
maintenance.statement: Inspect electrical wiring, terminal connections, conduit, and control panel components of the batching system for integrity and safety.
maintenance.description: Periodic physical inspection of all electrical infrastructure associated with the batching system to detect loose connections, insulation degradation, corrosion, and potential failure points before they cause downtime or safety incidents.

maintenance.name: Inspect for wear
maintenance.statement: Inspect the system for wear, corrosion, leaks, and damage.
maintenance.description: Visual and physical inspection catches corrosion, cracks, or leaks before they compromise a batch.

maintenance.name: Inspect for wear and damage
maintenance.statement: Inspect the plant's vessels, piping, and components for wear, corrosion, and leaks.
maintenance.description: Systematic visual and physical inspection of pressure vessels, pipework, and mechanical components to detect deterioration before failure.

maintenance.name: Inspect for wear and leaks
maintenance.statement: Inspect the batching system's tanks, piping, pumps, valves, mixers, sensors, and fittings for leaks, corrosion, deterioration, and component wear.
maintenance.description: Combines #81 (inspect for leaks, corrosion, wear) with #95 (inspect tanks, piping, pumps, valves, mixers, sensors, fittings for deterioration, wear, damage). Both describe the same periodic inspection job; #95 adds component specificity to #81.

maintenance.name: Inspect plant components for deterioration
maintenance.statement: Inspect plant components — including mixing elements, agitators, heat-transfer surfaces, and structural parts — for signs of wear, deterioration, or damage.
maintenance.description: Performs structured visual and physical inspection of all critical plant components to detect wear, corrosion, erosion, or damage that could affect processing performance or safety, enabling timely intervention.

maintenance.name: Inspect skid components
maintenance.statement: Inspect skid components for deterioration.
maintenance.description: Conducting visual and tactile inspection of the skid's structural, mechanical, and fluid-path components to identify signs of wear, corrosion, or damage.

maintenance.name: Lubricate moving components
maintenance.statement: Lubricate designated moving components at specified intervals.
maintenance.description: Proper lubrication limits friction-related wear in pumps, drives, bearings, and actuators.

maintenance.name: Manage spare parts inventory
maintenance.statement: Manage the critical spare parts inventory for the skid.
maintenance.description: Tracking stock levels, reorder points, and shelf life of critical replacement components to ensure availability when maintenance is required.

maintenance.name: Monitor equipment condition
maintenance.statement: Monitor the operating condition and health of plant equipment.
maintenance.description: Tracks key operating parameters — including vibration, temperature, pressure, and output quality — and equipment condition indicators to detect early signs of degradation, enabling proactive maintenance decisions.
maintenance.userGroup: Plant operators, maintenance engineers, reliability engineers
maintenance.frequency: Continuously during operation; supplemented by scheduled checks

maintenance.name: Monitor plant health
maintenance.statement: Monitor the operating status and equipment condition of the processing plant through plant health indicators.
maintenance.description: Detects emerging faults, degradation trends, and anomalies to enable timely intervention before failure.
maintenance.userGroup: Maintenance engineers, plant operators, reliability teams.
maintenance.frequency: Continuously during plant operation; reviewed at defined intervals.

maintenance.name: Monitor system health
maintenance.statement: Monitor diagnostic alerts, component runtime, process trends, and equipment condition indicators of the batching system.
maintenance.description: Combines #83 (monitor operational health and status) with #97 (monitor diagnostic alerts, component runtime, process trends, condition indicators). #97 adds specificity to #83; both describe the same continuous monitoring job.

maintenance.name: Optimize run performance across batches
maintenance.statement: Optimize run performance across successive batches.
maintenance.description: Analyze run data and adjust process parameters across batches to improve yield, cycle time, and process consistency.

maintenance.name: Perform routine service
maintenance.statement: Perform scheduled preventive service on the plant's mechanical and electrical equipment.
maintenance.description: Pumps, valves, agitators, and drives need scheduled servicing to avoid unplanned failures.

maintenance.name: Preserve idle equipment
maintenance.statement: Preserve and store plant equipment and components correctly during planned or extended shutdowns.
maintenance.description: Prevents corrosion, contamination, and degradation of plant equipment while it is not in active service.
maintenance.frequency: At each planned or extended shutdown.

maintenance.name: Preserve idle skid
maintenance.statement: Preserve the skid during extended inactivity.
maintenance.description: This limits corrosion, contamination, material degradation, and damage while the skid is stored or shut down.

maintenance.name: Protect Idle Equipment
maintenance.statement: Protect idle plant equipment during shutdown periods.
maintenance.description: Shutdown protection limits corrosion, contamination, moisture intrusion, seizure, freezing, and deformation while equipment is not operating.

maintenance.name: Purge and flush process lines
maintenance.statement: Purge and flush process lines to remove residual liquid product before maintenance or changeover.
maintenance.description: Displace residual liquid product from pipework and vessels using water, gas, or a displacement medium prior to maintenance access or product changeover, to ensure worker safety and prevent cross-contamination.

maintenance.name: Recalibrate dosing system after component change
maintenance.statement: Recalibrate the batch preparation system's dosing system following component replacement or repair.
maintenance.description: Re-establish dosing accuracy by running calibration routines after any pump, valve, meter, or control component has been replaced, adjusted, or serviced.

maintenance.name: Repair faults
maintenance.statement: Diagnose and repair faults in the processing plant's equipment.
maintenance.description: When faults occur, damaged equipment must be repaired to restore the plant to operating condition.

maintenance.name: Repair faults and failures
maintenance.statement: Diagnose and repair faults, breakdowns, and failed components in the system.
maintenance.description: Faults, breakdowns, and failed components must be diagnosed and repaired to restore operation.

maintenance.name: Repair plant faults
maintenance.statement: Repair mechanical, electrical, or process faults affecting the processing plant.
maintenance.description: Diagnoses and rectifies faults in the plant's mechanical, electrical, and process systems to restore normal operating condition, covering both reactive repairs following breakdowns and corrective actions identified during inspection.
maintenance.userGroup: Maintenance technicians, plant engineers, electrical technicians

maintenance.name: Repair skid faults
maintenance.statement: Repair faults affecting the skid.
maintenance.description: This restores intended operation after mechanical, electrical, pneumatic, instrumentation, or control failures occur.

maintenance.name: Repair system faults
maintenance.statement: Diagnose and repair mechanical, electrical, and control faults.
maintenance.description: When faults or breakdowns occur, the system must be diagnosed and repaired to restore production.

maintenance.name: Replace consumable parts
maintenance.statement: Replace worn filters, seals, gaskets, and mixing consumables.
maintenance.description: Filters, seals, gaskets, and mixing elements wear out and must be swapped to maintain performance.

maintenance.name: Replace filtration elements
maintenance.statement: Replace filters, cartridges, and other consumable filtration elements in the fluid path of the batching system.
maintenance.description: Swap-out of clogged, expired, or damaged filter media and cartridges to sustain flow rate, particulate control, and fluid quality.

maintenance.name: Replace seals and gaskets
maintenance.statement: Replace worn seals, gaskets, O-rings, diaphragms, and packing on pumps, valves, and connections within the batching system.
maintenance.description: Combines #79 (replace seals, gaskets, O-rings on pumps, valves, connections) with #96 (replace worn seals, gaskets, diaphragms, packing). Both describe the same sealing-component replacement job with overlapping component scope.

maintenance.name: Replace worn or consumed plant components
maintenance.statement: Replace plant components — including consumables such as filters, screens, and pump elements, and structural components that have reached their service limits.
maintenance.description: Covers the full spectrum of component replacement: routine consumables (filters, screens, seals, pump elements) replaced on schedule, and durable components replaced when inspection or service-life criteria indicate end of serviceable life.
maintenance.frequency: At defined service intervals or on condition

maintenance.name: Replace worn skid components
maintenance.statement: Replace worn, degraded, or failed components — including seals, gaskets, O-rings, and other skid parts — to restore the skid to its qualified operating condition.
maintenance.description: Covers the full range of component replacement tasks during preventive and corrective maintenance, from elastomeric seals and gaskets to mechanical parts identified as degraded during inspection or service.
maintenance.userGroup: Maintenance technicians and engineering staff responsible for skid upkeep.
maintenance.frequency: As required per preventive maintenance schedule or corrective maintenance findings.

maintenance.name: Requalify after maintenance
maintenance.statement: Requalify the skid after maintenance or component replacement.
maintenance.description: Matters because regulated processes require re-verification that the skid still performs to specification.

maintenance.name: Revalidate process performance after maintenance
maintenance.statement: Revalidate plant process performance following significant maintenance interventions.
maintenance.description: Run qualification batches or performance tests after major repairs, component replacements, or upgrades to confirm the plant returns to its specified process capability before resuming production.

maintenance.name: Run clean-in-place cycle
maintenance.statement: Run the clean-in-place cycle across the plant's piping and process circuit.
maintenance.description: Automated clean-in-place cycles must be run to sanitize the wetted process circuit without disassembly.

maintenance.name: Sanitize product-contact surfaces for changeover
maintenance.statement: Sanitize the product-contact surfaces and vessels of the plant to hygienic standards during product changeover.
maintenance.description: Sanitize all product-contact surfaces to the required hygienic standard before introducing a new product, preventing cross-contamination.

maintenance.name: Schedule preventive maintenance
maintenance.statement: Schedule and plan preventive maintenance activities for the batching system using service intervals, operating hours, condition data, and component life limits.
maintenance.description: Systematic planning and calendaring of all scheduled maintenance tasks to minimize unplanned downtime and extend equipment service life.

maintenance.name: Service pumps and agitators
maintenance.statement: Perform routine service, lubrication, and mechanical inspection on pumps, agitators, and mechanical drives.
maintenance.description: Combines lubrication of bearings, gearboxes, shafts, and drives (#85) with routine service and lubrication of pumps and agitators (#82). Lubrication is a sub-activity within pump/agitator servicing; they are the same Maintenance job.
maintenance.frequency: At scheduled service intervals per manufacturer recommendations.

maintenance.name: Service pumps and valves
maintenance.statement: Service the pumps, valves, and actuated equipment on the skid.
maintenance.description: Performing scheduled lubrication, adjustment, seal replacement, and functional checks on pumps, valves, and other mechanical assemblies.

maintenance.name: Store system properly
maintenance.statement: Store and preserve the system when not in use.
maintenance.description: Idle or seasonal equipment must be preserved and protected from contamination and corrosion.

maintenance.name: Test Protective Systems
maintenance.statement: Test the plant's protective systems for correct operation.
maintenance.description: Periodic testing confirms that interlocks, alarms, emergency stops, guards, and pressure-protection devices remain functional.

maintenance.name: Test fluid-path integrity
maintenance.statement: Test the integrity of the skid's fluid pathways.
maintenance.description: Performing pressure hold, bubble point, or other integrity tests on the skid's fluid paths to confirm absence of leaks or breaches before and after use.

maintenance.name: Test protective functions
maintenance.statement: Test the skid's protective functions.
maintenance.description: This confirms that alarms, interlocks, emergency stops, relief devices, and shutdown functions respond as intended.

maintenance.name: Test safety interlocks
maintenance.statement: Test plant safety interlocks under controlled conditions.
maintenance.description: Periodic testing confirms that protective functions will respond correctly during abnormal operating conditions.

maintenance.name: Update control software
maintenance.statement: Update the skid's control system software and firmware.
maintenance.description: Matters to apply patches, recipe changes, and functionality improvements to the automation layer.

maintenance.name: Upgrade plant components
maintenance.statement: Upgrade individual components or modules of the processing plant.
maintenance.description: Aging components can be upgraded to increase capacity, efficiency, or compliance without full replacement.

maintenance.name: Upgrade system components
maintenance.statement: Upgrade hardware components and automation modules on the batch system.
maintenance.description: Replace or add hardware, sensors, drives, or automation modules to improve or extend the capability of the batch preparation system.

maintenance.name: Validate sanitization
maintenance.statement: Verify and validate that sanitization of the plant meets required hygiene standards.
maintenance.description: Sanitization validation confirms cleaning cycles meet hygiene standards required for liquid products.

maintenance.name: Verify cleaning effectiveness
maintenance.statement: Verify the effectiveness of cleaning through sampling of the skid's wetted surfaces.
maintenance.description: Collecting swab, rinse, or environmental samples from the skid after cleaning to confirm residue levels are within validated acceptance criteria.

maintenance.name: Verify dosing accuracy
maintenance.statement: Verify the batch preparation system's dosing accuracy.
maintenance.description: Accuracy checks reveal drift or mechanical issues affecting feeders, pumps, dispensers, and metering devices.

maintenance.name: Verify process safety interlocks
maintenance.statement: Verify that process safety interlocks and shutdown systems operate correctly.
maintenance.description: Periodically test and verify that all process safety interlocks — overpressure relief, over-temperature shutdown, agitator overload trip — respond correctly to protect the plant and personnel.

preparation.name: Anchor and level equipment
preparation.statement: Anchor and level the plant skids and vessels to their mounting points.
preparation.description: Matters to ensure mechanical stability and proper drainage of viscous media once processing begins.

preparation.name: Anchor equipment foundations
preparation.statement: Anchor the plant equipment to its foundations and mounts.
preparation.description: Foundations and mounts must hold heavy tanks and machinery against vibration and load.

preparation.name: Assemble process modules
preparation.statement: Assemble the individual process modules into the complete plant configuration.
preparation.description: Mechanically join modular plant sections, skids, and sub-assemblies into the full equipment arrangement as specified in the plant layout drawings.

preparation.name: Assemble shipped-loose components onto the skid
preparation.statement: Assemble the skid components supplied separately for transportation onto the skid structure.
preparation.description: A mechanical installer fits and fastens instrumentation, piping spools, panels, or other components that were removed or shipped loose to meet transport constraints.

preparation.name: Assemble system modules
preparation.statement: Assemble the supplied tanks, pumps, mixers, dispensers, sensors, and support modules into the complete batching system.
preparation.description: Mechanically assemble all major and ancillary system components — including structural supports, vessels, rotating equipment, instrumentation, and dispensing hardware — into the integrated system configuration.

preparation.name: Calibrate measurement instruments
preparation.statement: Calibrate the system's measurement instruments for accurate batch control.
preparation.description: Sensors for weight, temperature, level, and flow must be calibrated so batch accuracy meets specification.

preparation.name: Calibrate skid instrumentation at installation
preparation.statement: Calibrate the skid's sensors, gauges, and measurement instruments against applicable reference standards before first operation.
preparation.description: Performs initial calibration of all skid instrumentation as part of the installation and qualification sequence, establishing traceable baselines prior to IQ/OQ execution.
preparation.userGroup: Metrology, instrumentation, and quality teams executing the installation qualification.
preparation.frequency: Once per installation; repeated after any instrument replacement or major maintenance event.

preparation.name: Clean product-contact surfaces
preparation.statement: Clean the tanks, lines, pumps, mixers, valves, and dispensing points before introducing batch materials.
preparation.description: Pre-use cleaning removes residues or contaminants that could compromise the first subsequent batch.

preparation.name: Commission system with trial batch
preparation.statement: Run a trial batch to commission and validate the batch preparation system before production use.
preparation.description: Commissioning technician executes a qualification trial batch to verify that all system functions — mixing, temperature, dosing, control — perform within specification under representative process conditions.

preparation.name: Commission system with validation batch
preparation.statement: Commission the assembled system by running a validation batch to verify correct operation before production use.
preparation.description: Execute a controlled test or validation batch through the fully assembled and configured system to confirm that all components, controls, interlocks, and measurements perform to specification.

preparation.name: Commission the plant
preparation.statement: Commission and test the plant to verify readiness for production.
preparation.description: Execute the full commissioning sequence — wet trials, functional tests, performance verification — to confirm the installed plant meets design intent before handover to operations.

preparation.name: Commission the plant with acceptance testing
preparation.statement: Commission the plant by conducting trial runs and verifying performance against defined acceptance criteria.
preparation.description: Structured commissioning activities including media trials, process function verification, and documented acceptance testing to confirm the plant meets its performance specification before handover to operations.

preparation.name: Configure batch parameters
preparation.statement: Configure the operating parameters and control settings for the intended batch process.
preparation.description: Operating parameters such as mixing speed, temperature, dosing sequence, and batch size must be set before production.

preparation.name: Configure batch recipes
preparation.statement: Configure the system-specific quantities, sequences, mixing times, temperatures, and dosing rates for each batch recipe in the control system.
preparation.description: Combines configuring batch parameters (#30 — recipes, fill volumes, mixing sequences) with configuring batch recipes (#36 — quantities, sequences, mixing times, temperatures, dosing rates). Both describe the same preparation activity at equivalent specificity.
preparation.userGroup: Process engineers, system operators, batch technicians.
preparation.frequency: Once at commissioning; repeated when new recipes are added.

preparation.name: Configure control sequences
preparation.statement: Configure the plant control logic for the intended processing sequences.
preparation.description: This prepares automated equipment to execute the required startup, processing, transfer, cleaning, and shutdown steps.

preparation.name: Configure control system
preparation.statement: Configure the plant's control system and process parameters.
preparation.description: The control system governs sequencing, dosing and monitoring of the process.

preparation.name: Configure safety interlocks
preparation.statement: Configure the plant safety interlocks for the installed equipment arrangement.
preparation.description: This prepares protective responses for unsafe pressure, temperature, flow, access, level, or equipment states.

preparation.name: Configure skid setpoints and operating parameters
preparation.statement: Configure the skid's setpoints and operating parameters for the intended process.
preparation.description: A process engineer or operator enters the target values for temperature, pressure, flow, and other controlled variables into the skid's control system ahead of operation.

preparation.name: Connect control networks
preparation.statement: Connect the plant control devices to the designated communications network.
preparation.description: This enables coordinated control, monitoring, diagnostics, and production-data exchange across connected equipment.

preparation.name: Connect electrical power
preparation.statement: Connect the processing plant to its electrical power supply.
preparation.description: Run power cables, install disconnects, and terminate all electrical power connections for plant drive motors, heaters, and control panels per electrical specifications.

preparation.name: Connect fluid piping
preparation.statement: Connect the inlet, outlet, and recirculation piping to the batching system.
preparation.description: Fluid pathways must be joined so ingredients can be transferred through the system.

preparation.name: Connect power and utility supplies
preparation.statement: Connect the batching system to its required electrical, pneumatic, and other utility supply sources.
preparation.description: Terminate and verify all electrical, pneumatic, and ancillary utility connections needed to energise the system, following applicable codes and specifications.

preparation.name: Connect process piping
preparation.statement: Connect the system to the required material inlet, transfer, recirculation, and discharge piping.
preparation.description: Install and connect all process fluid piping runs — including inlet feeds, inter-vessel transfer lines, recirculation loops, and discharge headers — between the batching system and plant infrastructure.

preparation.name: Connect process piping between unit operations
preparation.statement: Connect process piping, manifolds, and fittings between plant unit operations.
preparation.description: Mechanical assembly and connection of all in-process piping, manifolds, hoses, and sanitary fittings that route viscous media between the processing plant's unit operations.

preparation.name: Connect utility services
preparation.statement: Connect the batch preparation system to electrical power, process fluids, compressed air, ventilation, and drainage utilities.
preparation.description: Technician establishes all required utility connections — power, fluid supply, pneumatics, ventilation, and drainage — to bring the system to an operable state.

preparation.name: Connect utility services to the plant
preparation.statement: Connect the plant to required utility services including power, steam, cooling water, and compressed air.
preparation.description: Termination and connection of all utility supply and return lines — electrical power, process steam, cooling water, and instrument air — to the plant's designated utility interfaces.

preparation.name: Document system configuration
preparation.statement: Document the as-installed configuration, calibration records, and parameter settings of the batching system.
preparation.description: Capture and record the final installed configuration — including pipe routing, instrument calibration certificates, control parameter settings, recipe data, and access permission structures — as the baseline reference for future maintenance, audits, and regulatory review.

preparation.name: Document the as-built plant configuration
preparation.statement: Document the as-built configuration of the installed processing plant for operational and regulatory records.
preparation.description: Creation of as-built drawings, instrument loop diagrams, equipment datasheets, and configuration records that accurately reflect the plant as physically installed and commissioned, for use in operations, maintenance, and regulatory compliance.

preparation.name: Establish cleaning-in-place or washdown procedures
preparation.statement: Establish and document the cleaning-in-place or washdown procedure for the batch preparation system prior to initial production.
preparation.description: Technician or process engineer defines, validates, and documents the step-by-step cleaning procedure — chemical selection, concentrations, contact times, temperatures, rinse cycles — appropriate to the system's materials and intended product, ensuring hygienic readiness before first use.

preparation.name: Establish operating procedures
preparation.statement: Establish standard operating procedures and workflows for the skid.
preparation.description: Matters because regulated production requires documented, repeatable operating workflows.

preparation.name: Establish operator access and permission levels
preparation.statement: Establish operator access credentials and permission levels within the batch preparation system's control platform.
preparation.description: Administrator creates user accounts, assigns role-based access rights, and configures authentication controls within the control system to ensure only authorized personnel can execute or modify recipes, parameters, and safety settings.

preparation.name: Establish startup and shutdown operating procedures
preparation.statement: Establish written startup and shutdown procedures for the processing plant based on its installed configuration.
preparation.description: Development and documentation of step-by-step startup and shutdown operating procedures specific to the installed plant configuration, to guide operators in safe and repeatable pre-production preparation.

preparation.name: Establish user access
preparation.statement: Establish user accounts and access permissions for the control system.
preparation.description: Access levels and user credentials control who can edit recipes and operate the system.

preparation.name: Import process recipes
preparation.statement: Import approved process recipes into the plant control system.
preparation.description: This prepares recipe-driven plants with reusable parameter sets for applicable liquid products.

preparation.name: Import recipe data
preparation.statement: Import existing recipes and process data into the system's control software.
preparation.description: Existing formulations, batch records, and process data are imported so historical recipes are available.

preparation.name: Install and anchor system
preparation.statement: Install the batch preparation system in its production location and anchor it to the floor or structure.
preparation.description: The system must be physically positioned, leveled, and anchored within the production area before operation.

preparation.name: Install control software
preparation.statement: Install the required control software on the batch preparation system's computing infrastructure.
preparation.description: Administrator or technician installs, licenses, and performs initial configuration of the control software platform on the system's dedicated hardware prior to recipe and parameter setup.

preparation.name: Install process instrumentation
preparation.statement: Install and wire the plant's process instrumentation and sensors.
preparation.description: Mount, pipe, and wire all flow, temperature, pressure, level, and analytical instruments on the plant per the instrument loop diagrams.

preparation.name: Install single-use assemblies onto the skid
preparation.statement: Install disposable bags, tubing sets, and single-use assemblies onto the skid before operation.
preparation.description: An operator or process technician mounts and connects single-use product-contact components — including biocontainer bags, manifolds, and tubing — to the skid per the assembly drawing.

preparation.name: Integrate control system with plant network
preparation.statement: Integrate the batch preparation system's controls and instrumentation with the plant control and data network.
preparation.description: Integration specialist configures communication links, I/O mapping, and data exchange protocols to connect the system's control architecture to the plant-wide control, historian, or MES network.

preparation.name: Integrate with control system
preparation.statement: Integrate the skid controls with the facility's automation and data historian systems.
preparation.description: Matters because centralized monitoring and batch records depend on control system connectivity.

preparation.name: Integrate with external business systems
preparation.statement: Integrate the batch preparation system with designated inventory, quality, production scheduling, or enterprise resource planning systems.
preparation.description: Integration specialist establishes data connections and exchange workflows between the batch preparation system and external plant-level or enterprise-level systems to enable automated data sharing and process coordination.

preparation.name: Integrate with plant systems
preparation.statement: Integrate the plant with existing enterprise and supervisory systems.
preparation.description: Integrating with plant-wide systems enables coordinated data and production management.

preparation.name: Map ingredient sources
preparation.statement: Map each ingredient source to its corresponding tank, line, pump, valve, or dosing channel.
preparation.description: Accurate source mapping prevents control commands from routing or dispensing the wrong liquid.

preparation.name: Map ingredient sources to system inputs
preparation.statement: Map ingredient sources to the corresponding system inputs and storage locations.
preparation.description: Process engineer identifies and documents which raw material supply line, tank, or container feeds each system inlet or ingredient addition port, ensuring correct material routing before batch production.

preparation.name: Perform installation and operational qualification tests
preparation.statement: Perform installation qualification and operational qualification tests on the assembled skid.
preparation.description: A validation engineer executes IQ/OQ protocols to confirm that the skid was installed correctly and operates within its specified parameters, generating documentation for regulatory submission.

preparation.name: Perform pre-startup risk assessment
preparation.statement: Perform a pre-startup safety review of the installed skid before first operation.
preparation.description: A safety or process engineer conducts a formal pre-startup safety review (PSSR) to confirm that all installation, documentation, training, and interlock requirements are satisfied before the skid is placed into service.

preparation.name: Position and mount equipment
preparation.statement: Position and mount the liquid batching system components at the designated installation location.
preparation.description: Place and secure tanks, pumps, metering hardware, and support structures in their specified positions, accounting for floor loading, access, and process flow.

preparation.name: Position plant equipment
preparation.statement: Position the processing plant equipment in its designated operating location.
preparation.description: Move and precisely locate major plant equipment items on the prepared foundation or floor area according to the layout plan.

preparation.name: Position plant modules on prepared foundations
preparation.statement: Position plant vessels, skids, and process modules on prepared foundations according to the approved installation layout.
preparation.description: Rigging and placement of processing plant vessels, skids, and modular units onto their prepared civil foundations or structural supports in accordance with the installation drawing.

preparation.name: Position system within operating space
preparation.statement: Position the batch preparation system within its designated operating space according to layout and access requirements.
preparation.description: Installer maneuvers and positions the system into its final location, accounting for clearance, workflow access, maintenance access, and facility layout constraints.

preparation.name: Prepare installation site
preparation.statement: Prepare the installation site to receive the liquid product processing plant.
preparation.description: Site conditions determine whether the plant can be safely installed and operated.

preparation.name: Prime fluid circuits
preparation.statement: Prime the required pumps, dosing lines, valves, and transfer circuits with the designated liquid.
preparation.description: Priming removes trapped air and prepares fluid-handling components for consistent delivery.

preparation.name: Prime ingredient feeds
preparation.statement: Prime and charge the ingredient feed lines and supply tanks.
preparation.description: Raw ingredient supply tanks and feed lines must be primed and charged before the first batch.

preparation.name: Prime pumps and lines
preparation.statement: Prime the plant's pumps and transfer lines with the viscous media.
preparation.description: Matters before production to fill high-resistance lines and prevent cavitation or dead spots at startup.

preparation.name: Program batch recipes
preparation.statement: Program batch recipes and process sequences into the system's control platform.
preparation.description: Operator or process engineer encodes batch recipes — ingredient quantities, addition sequences, agitation profiles, timing, and endpoints — into the control system prior to production use.

preparation.name: Sanitize and passivate
preparation.statement: Sanitize, passivate, and prepare product-contact surfaces before first use.
preparation.description: Matters because biopharma surfaces must be cleaned and passivated to prevent contamination and corrosion.

preparation.name: Sanitize process surfaces
preparation.statement: Clean and sanitize the plant's product-contact surfaces before first production use.
preparation.description: Perform initial cleaning, rinsing, and sanitization of all product-contact surfaces to remove manufacturing residues and verify hygienic readiness before the first production run.

preparation.name: Set operating limits
preparation.statement: Set the system limits for capacity, pressure, temperature, flow, timing, and dosing tolerance.
preparation.description: Operating limits constrain the equipment to ranges appropriate for its configuration and handled liquids.

preparation.name: Set operating parameters
preparation.statement: Set the plant operating parameters for the planned production run.
preparation.description: This establishes equipment-specific values such as temperatures, pressures, flow rates, mixing speeds, or timing limits before use.

preparation.name: Set up and verify safety systems
preparation.statement: Set up and verify the batch preparation system's safety interlocks and protective devices.
preparation.description: Technician programs, tests, and documents all safety interlock logic, pressure relief, emergency stop, and over-temperature protection to confirm correct protective function before commissioning.

preparation.name: Set up safety systems
preparation.statement: Set up the plant's safety interlocks and protective systems.
preparation.description: Interlocks, guards and emergency stops protect operators from hazards.

preparation.name: Test plant containment integrity after assembly
preparation.statement: Test the assembled plant's piping, vessels, and seals for containment integrity before commissioning.
preparation.description: Pressure or leak testing of the fully assembled plant to confirm that all vessels, piping, gaskets, and seals maintain containment integrity under operating and test conditions.

preparation.name: Test system integrity
preparation.statement: Test assembled vessels, seals, fittings, hoses, and piping for leaks or pressure loss.
preparation.description: Integrity testing detects connection or containment defects before process liquids are handled.

preparation.name: Train operating personnel
preparation.statement: Train personnel to operate the viscous product processing plant safely and correctly.
preparation.description: Matters before handover so staff can run, monitor, and respond to the plant without incident.

preparation.name: Train operators
preparation.statement: Train operators to run the batch preparation system safely and correctly.
preparation.description: Operators need to understand controls, procedures, and safety practices to run the system correctly.

preparation.name: Train operators on skid
preparation.statement: Train operators and maintenance staff to run and service the skid.
preparation.description: Matters because safe, compliant operation depends on personnel understanding the specific skid.

preparation.name: Train plant operators
preparation.statement: Train plant personnel to operate the installed liquid product processing plant.
preparation.description: Builds the operating competency needed to run the plant safely and correctly before production begins.
preparation.userGroup: Plant operators, shift supervisors, training coordinators.
preparation.frequency: Once at initial commissioning; repeated when personnel change or plant is modified.

preparation.name: Train system operators
preparation.statement: Train operators to prepare, start, monitor, stop, clean, and reset the batching system.
preparation.description: Deliver structured training to process operators covering system startup, batch execution, monitoring, shutdown, cleaning, and fault response procedures.

preparation.name: Verify in-process integrity of single-use assembly
preparation.statement: Verify the integrity of the single-use flow path assembly during or immediately before active operation.
preparation.description: Perform a pressure hold or bubble-point test on the single-use assembly at the point of use to confirm integrity before committing process material, without referencing a specific test instrument brand.

preparation.name: Verify safety interlocks and shutdown functions
preparation.statement: Verify the configured safety interlocks, alarms, emergency stop functions, and containment sensors before system startup.
preparation.description: Combines setting up safety interlocks (#32) with verifying them before startup (#50). Verification necessarily follows and encompasses setup; at a Level-1 job granularity these are one activity.
preparation.userGroup: Safety engineers, commissioning engineers, system operators.
preparation.frequency: Once at commissioning; repeated after maintenance affecting safety systems.

disposal.name: Archive compliance records
disposal.statement: Archive batch, validation, and maintenance records for the retired plant.
disposal.description: Regulatory and quality records tied to the plant must be retained after it is retired.

disposal.name: Archive lifecycle records
disposal.statement: Archive required lifecycle, maintenance, and disposal compliance records for the retired system.
disposal.description: Combines documenting disposal activities for compliance records (#114) with archiving required lifecycle records (#103). Both describe the same record-archiving obligation at end of life.
disposal.userGroup: Quality assurance, compliance officers, records management.

disposal.name: Archive process and lifecycle records
disposal.statement: Archive batch, quality, compliance, and lifecycle records from the plant control system at end of service.
disposal.description: Preserves required documentation for regulatory, legal, and operational reference after the plant is retired.
disposal.userGroup: Plant managers, compliance officers, records management teams.

disposal.name: Cancel plant services
disposal.statement: Cancel active service, licensing, and support agreements associated with the retired plant.
disposal.description: Terminates ongoing contractual obligations to avoid unnecessary costs after the plant is decommissioned.

disposal.name: Cancel service contracts
disposal.statement: Cancel service, monitoring, and software contracts tied to the system.
disposal.description: Matters when maintenance, monitoring, or software service contracts must be ended.

disposal.name: Clean wetted surfaces
disposal.statement: Clean and flush wetted surfaces of tanks, pumps, and lines before disposal.
disposal.description: Perform a final clean-in-place or manual flush of all product-contact surfaces to remove residues and meet hygiene, safety, or handover standards.

disposal.name: Conduct end-of-life safety review
disposal.statement: Conduct a safety review of the system before initiating decommissioning activities.
disposal.description: Assess residual hazards — pressure, chemical, electrical, mechanical — present in the system to establish safe decommissioning procedures and protective measures.

disposal.name: Conduct final safety inspection
disposal.statement: Conduct a final safety inspection of the decommissioned plant site before handover or closure.
disposal.description: Perform a structured inspection to confirm all hazards have been eliminated, all utilities disconnected, all materials removed, and the site is safe for handover to facility management or new occupants.

disposal.name: Decommission the plant
disposal.statement: Decommission the viscous product processing plant at end of service life.
disposal.description: Execute a formal decommissioning procedure that retires the plant from active service, covers all required safety and regulatory steps, and closes out site obligations.

disposal.name: Decommission the process skid
disposal.statement: Decommission the process skid from active production service.
disposal.description: Formally remove the skid from the active equipment register and production service.

disposal.name: Decommission the system
disposal.statement: Decommission the batch preparation system from active service.
disposal.description: Execute all steps required to formally take the batch preparation system out of operational service.

disposal.name: Decontaminate product-contact surfaces
disposal.statement: Decontaminate the skid's product-contact surfaces prior to removal.
disposal.description: Apply validated decontamination procedures to wetted and product-contact surfaces to achieve required release conditions before disposal handling.

disposal.name: Decontaminate wetted components
disposal.statement: Decontaminate tanks, lines, and mixing components before disposal.
disposal.description: Apply appropriate decontamination procedures to all wetted surfaces and internals to meet safety and regulatory requirements prior to disposal.

disposal.name: Disconnect process interfaces
disposal.statement: Disconnect the system from upstream feeds, downstream lines, and utilities.
disposal.description: Safely isolate the system from all connected process streams and utility supplies before removal.

disposal.name: Disconnect utility connections
disposal.statement: Disconnect power, fluid, and process utility connections from the retired batch preparation system.
disposal.description: Safely isolate and cap all external utility feeds — electrical, water, steam, compressed air, and process lines — as part of decommissioning.

disposal.name: Dismantle plant structure
disposal.statement: Dismantle and remove the plant's structures, tanks, and piping from the site.
disposal.description: Physically break down and remove all plant equipment, vessels, piping, and structural elements from the installation site.

disposal.name: Dismantle the equipment
disposal.statement: Disconnect and dismantle the plant equipment for removal from site.
disposal.description: Mechanically disconnect, unbolt, and break down the plant into removable assemblies, facilitating transport, recycling, resale, or disposal.

disposal.name: Dismantle the skid into handling assemblies
disposal.statement: Disassemble the process skid into manageable assemblies for final disposition.
disposal.description: Break down the skid structure into sub-assemblies of appropriate size and weight for transport, recycling, or disposal.

disposal.name: Dispose of hazardous residues
disposal.statement: Dispose of hazardous solvents, lubricants, and product residues per regulation.
disposal.description: Solvents, lubricants, and product residues classed as hazardous require compliant disposal.

disposal.name: Dispose of residual waste
disposal.statement: Dispose of nonrecoverable plant waste through authorised channels.
disposal.description: Arrange compliant disposal of all nonrecoverable solid and liquid waste streams generated during plant decommissioning, including manifesting, transport, and confirmation of receipt.

disposal.name: Document final plant condition
disposal.statement: Document the plant's physical condition and decommissioned state at the end of service.
disposal.description: Creates a formal record of the plant's final configuration, condition, and site status for handover, legal, and regulatory purposes.
disposal.userGroup: Plant managers, project engineers, compliance officers.

disposal.name: Document final state
disposal.statement: Document the final condition and disposition of the skid.
disposal.description: Records the final condition, decontamination, and disposition of the skid for regulatory and audit traceability.

disposal.name: Document final system condition
disposal.statement: Record the system's physical and operational condition at retirement.
disposal.description: Capture the final state of all major components and subsystems to support asset records, warranty claims, or future reference.

disposal.name: Drain residual process fluids
disposal.statement: Drain and purge residual process and cleaning fluids from the skid.
disposal.description: Removes residual process fluids, buffers, solvents, and CIP chemicals safely from the piping and vessels.

disposal.name: Drain residual process liquids
disposal.statement: Drain residual process liquids from tanks, vessels, and lines.
disposal.description: Remove all remaining process fluids from the plant's storage, processing, and transfer systems prior to dismantling.

disposal.name: Drain residual product
disposal.statement: Drain and evacuate residual viscous product from vessels and pipework before decommissioning.
disposal.description: Remove all residual viscous material from tanks, vessels, and interconnecting pipework to prepare the plant for cleaning, disassembly, or handover.

disposal.name: Erase sensitive operational data
disposal.statement: Erase sensitive operational data from plant control systems before decommissioning.
disposal.description: Perform a verified erasure or sanitisation of proprietary process parameters, personnel records, and sensitive operational data stored on control-system hardware before it is retired, transferred, or scrapped.

disposal.name: Export batch data
disposal.statement: Export batch records, recipes, and process data from the control system prior to decommissioning.
disposal.description: Extract all operational data stored in the system's controllers and data historian for retention or migration.

disposal.name: Export batch records
disposal.statement: Export historical batch data and recipe records from the control system prior to decommissioning.
disposal.description: Extract all stored batch histories and production records from the system's control platform to ensure data is preserved after the system is retired.

disposal.name: Export control system data
disposal.statement: Export recipes, control logic, and operational data from the plant's automation system.
disposal.description: Extract and preserve all automation programs, process recipes, historian data, and configuration files from the plant's control systems before decommissioning.

disposal.name: Export process data
disposal.statement: Export recipes, batch records, and control-system configurations before decommissioning.
disposal.description: Extract and transfer all operationally valuable data — process recipes, batch histories, and control-system configurations — from the plant's control systems prior to shutdown.

disposal.name: Isolate energy sources
disposal.statement: Isolate the plant from each connected energy source before disassembly.
disposal.description: Apply lockout/tagout or equivalent energy-isolation procedures to electrical, pneumatic, hydraulic, and thermal energy sources to ensure safe decommissioning.

disposal.name: Isolate energy sources on the skid
disposal.statement: Isolate the process skid from every connected energy source prior to decommissioning.
disposal.description: Apply lockout/tagout or equivalent energy isolation procedures to all energy sources — electrical, pneumatic, hydraulic — connected to the skid before decommissioning work begins.

disposal.name: Migrate control configuration
disposal.statement: Transfer the retiring system's required control configuration to its replacement.
disposal.description: Extract and port control logic, parameters, and automation settings from the retiring system to ensure continuity of operations on the replacement platform.

disposal.name: Notify affected stakeholders
disposal.statement: Notify operations, quality, and safety stakeholders of the system removal.
disposal.description: Operations, quality, and safety teams need notice before the system goes offline.

disposal.name: Notify regulatory authorities
disposal.statement: Notify regulatory and environmental authorities of the plant's decommissioning.
disposal.description: Environmental, safety, and regulatory authorities must be informed of the plant's retirement.

disposal.name: Notify stakeholders of decommissioning
disposal.statement: Notify regulators, insurers, and relevant stakeholders of the plant's decommissioning.
disposal.description: Issue formal notifications to all required parties — regulatory bodies, insurers, utility providers, and internal stakeholders — that the plant has been permanently retired from service.

disposal.name: Notify stakeholders of retirement
disposal.statement: Notify quality, regulatory, and operations stakeholders of the skid's retirement.
disposal.description: Informs regulators, quality, and downstream stakeholders that the skid is no longer in production use.

disposal.name: Obtain disposal permits
disposal.statement: Obtain required regulatory permits and approvals for plant decommissioning and site clearance.
disposal.description: Secure all necessary environmental, demolition, and waste-disposal permits from relevant authorities before commencing physical decommissioning activities.

disposal.name: Obtain regulatory disposal approvals
disposal.statement: Obtain required regulatory approvals and permits before disposing of the system.
disposal.description: Identify and secure all permits, notifications, or regulatory clearances required by applicable environmental and safety authorities prior to decommissioning activities.

disposal.name: Physically remove the system
disposal.statement: Physically remove the batch preparation system from its installed location.
disposal.description: Coordinate rigging, disassembly, and removal of the system's structural and process assemblies from the facility floor, including any embedded or skid-mounted sub-systems.

disposal.name: Prepare for extended storage
disposal.statement: Drain, flush, and protect the batching system for an extended period of inactivity.
disposal.description: Execution of lay-up procedures including draining fluid paths, purging residues, applying protective measures, and securing the system against deterioration during prolonged non-use.

disposal.name: Purge and clean process equipment
disposal.statement: Purge and clean process equipment of remaining product traces prior to removal.
disposal.description: Remove residual product traces from all process-contact equipment using appropriate flushing, purging, or cleaning methods to ensure safe handling during dismantling.

disposal.name: Recover and recycle decommissioned components
disposal.statement: Salvage reusable components from the retired batch preparation system for reuse or resale and recycle eligible materials and remaining components through appropriate channels.
disposal.description: After decommissioning, components and materials are sorted: reusable parts are tagged for internal reuse or sold, while eligible materials (metals, electronics, etc.) are directed to recycling streams in accordance with environmental and regulatory requirements.
disposal.userGroup: Facilities/asset management team, environmental health and safety, procurement.

disposal.name: Recover reusable components
disposal.statement: Recover serviceable components from the decommissioned plant for reuse.
disposal.description: Identify, remove, test, and catalogue mechanical components — pumps, valves, instruments, motors — that retain serviceability and can be redeployed or sold as spares.

disposal.name: Recycle eligible skid materials
disposal.statement: Route eligible skid materials into approved recycling streams.
disposal.description: Sort and route skid materials — metals, electronics, plastics — into appropriate approved recycling streams after disassembly.

disposal.name: Recycle recoverable components
disposal.statement: Recover and recycle metals, motors, and reusable components from the plant.
disposal.description: Segregate and route recoverable plant materials — structural metals, motors, instrumentation — to appropriate recycling streams.

disposal.name: Recycle system materials
disposal.statement: Route recoverable system materials into appropriate recycling streams.
disposal.description: Separate and route metals, piping, electronics, and other recyclable materials from the decommissioned system into applicable recycling channels.

disposal.name: Remediate the site
disposal.statement: Decontaminate and remediate the plant site after equipment removal.
disposal.description: The site may require decontamination and remediation to return it to a safe, reusable condition.

disposal.name: Remove personal protective infrastructure
disposal.statement: Remove plant-specific safety systems and protective infrastructure prior to site handover.
disposal.description: Decommission and remove interlocks, safety showers, gas detection systems, and other plant-specific safety infrastructure that are not part of the general facility.

disposal.name: Remove proprietary data
disposal.statement: Remove proprietary formulas, access credentials, and sensitive information from the system's controllers.
disposal.description: Sanitise the system's onboard storage to prevent disclosure of intellectual property or credentials after transfer or disposal.

disposal.name: Remove stored proprietary data
disposal.statement: Remove stored proprietary recipes, formulations, and configuration data from the system controller.
disposal.description: Purge all confidential process data and intellectual property from onboard storage before the system is transferred, resold, or scrapped.

disposal.name: Remove system identification markings
disposal.statement: Remove or obliterate identification markings, asset tags, and labels from the system before transfer or disposal.
disposal.description: Strip asset tags, serial-number plates, proprietary identification, and facility-specific markings from the system to protect organisational information and comply with asset management policies.

disposal.name: Remove the skid from the facility
disposal.statement: Rig and remove the skid from the process facility.
disposal.description: Use appropriate rigging and handling equipment to safely remove the decommissioned skid from the facility floor.

disposal.name: Resell or transfer equipment
disposal.statement: Resell or transfer usable process equipment to a new owner or facility.
disposal.description: Functional equipment may be sold or transferred to another facility to extend its service life.

disposal.name: Resell the plant
disposal.statement: Transfer ownership of the plant to a buyer or the used-equipment market.
disposal.description: List, negotiate, and complete the sale of the decommissioned plant or its major assemblies through the secondary equipment market, recovering residual asset value.

disposal.name: Return Affected Equipment
disposal.statement: Return the batch preparation system to the responsible supplier.
disposal.description: This applies when a warranty claim, recall, lease termination, or supplier take-back program requires return.

disposal.name: Return eligible equipment
disposal.statement: Return eligible equipment through its designated return channel.
disposal.description: This fulfills warranty, recall, lease, rental, exchange, or supplier take-back requirements.

disposal.name: Return under take-back program
disposal.statement: Return the system to the manufacturer or a designated take-back program at end of life.
disposal.description: Coordinate return logistics and documentation to comply with manufacturer take-back or extended producer responsibility requirements.

disposal.name: Salvage reusable components
disposal.statement: Recover reusable pumps, valves, sensors, and vessels from the system.
disposal.description: Identify and extract components that retain service value for reuse in other systems or as spare parts.

disposal.name: Sanitize digital systems
disposal.statement: Sanitize sensitive data from the plant's controllers and storage devices.
disposal.description: Securely erase or overwrite proprietary process data, credentials, and confidential configurations from all plant digital systems prior to transfer or disposal.

disposal.name: Segregate hazardous materials
disposal.statement: Segregate hazardous materials removed from the plant for compliant disposal.
disposal.description: Sort and separate hazardous substances — contaminated insulation, asbestos-containing materials, process chemicals — into compliant waste streams prior to authorised disposal.

disposal.name: Store skid components properly
disposal.statement: Store disassembled or spare skid components properly during periods of inactivity.
disposal.description: Ensuring removed assemblies, single-use components, and spare parts are stored under appropriate conditions (temperature, humidity, contamination control) to prevent degradation before reinstallation.

disposal.name: Terminate linked services
disposal.statement: Terminate services, maintenance contracts, calibration agreements, and consumable supply arrangements linked to the retired batching system.
disposal.description: Combines cancelling specific service/calibration/consumable agreements (#101) with terminating all services linked to the retired system (#117). Both describe the same end-of-life service termination activity.

disposal.name: Transfer documentation package
disposal.statement: Transfer manuals, drawings, and certifications to the new owner of the plant.
disposal.description: Compile and hand over all technical documentation — operating manuals, P&IDs, as-built drawings, calibration certificates — to the incoming owner or operator.

disposal.name: Transfer equipment ownership
disposal.statement: Transfer ownership of the batch preparation system to a new facility or buyer.
disposal.description: Matters when the equipment retains resale value to another facility or plant.

disposal.name: Transfer plant ownership
disposal.statement: Transfer legal ownership of the processing plant to its next custodian.
disposal.description: Execute legal instruments — bills of sale, title transfers, asset registers — to formally convey ownership of the plant or its components to a buyer, lessee, or donee.

disposal.name: Transfer processed output
disposal.statement: Transfer processed liquid from the plant to the designated receiving system.
disposal.description: Move finished or semi-finished liquid product from the plant's discharge point to downstream storage, packaging, or further processing.

disposal.name: Transfer system ownership
disposal.statement: Transfer or resell the system to a new owner.
disposal.description: Execute the administrative and physical steps required to transfer legal ownership of the system to another party.

disposal.name: Transition batching operations
disposal.statement: Transition batching operations to the replacement system.
disposal.description: Coordinate the handover of production workload, process parameters, and operational responsibilities from the retiring system to its replacement.

disposal.name: Transition to replacement
disposal.statement: Transition production from the retired skid to its replacement unit.
disposal.description: Manages the changeover from the retired skid to its replacement so production continuity is maintained.

disposal.name: Verify decontamination clearance before removal
disposal.statement: Confirm that the decontaminated skid meets applicable release criteria before further handling.
disposal.description: Conduct or review the decontamination verification steps required to confirm the skid is cleared for safe handling, transport, or disposal.

usage.name: Adjust capture parameters
usage.statement: Adjust identifier capture parameters for current line conditions.
usage.description: This maintains reliable data capture when package materials, code placement, line speed, or environmental conditions vary.

usage.name: Adjust operating settings
usage.statement: Adjust scanning, marking, and rejection settings during operation to maintain capture performance.
usage.description: Modifies reader sensitivity, marking parameters, or rejection thresholds mid-run to compensate for line-speed changes, material variation, or environmental drift.

usage.name: Aggregate packaging hierarchy
usage.statement: Aggregate individual units into cases and pallets to build the packaging hierarchy record.
