usage.description: Groups scanned units into their parent packaging levels (case, pallet) to maintain an accurate hierarchical traceability record during production.

usage.name: Annotate traceability exceptions
usage.statement: Annotate a traceability exception with relevant operational details.
usage.description: This provides context for later investigation, review, or record disposition.

usage.name: Capture unit identifiers
usage.statement: Capture unique identifiers on each unit as it passes along the packaging line.
usage.description: Core function performed every shift as products move down the line and must be individually or batch-identified.

usage.name: Coordinate multi-line operation
usage.statement: Coordinate traceability data across multiple packaging lines or sites during concurrent production.
usage.description: Manages simultaneous traceability sessions across parallel lines or geographically distributed sites, ensuring consistent identifier scoping and data integrity across all active runs.

usage.name: Demonstrate system operation
usage.statement: Demonstrate traceability system operation to new operators during training runs.
usage.description: New operators need supervised runs to learn how to capture, verify, and reject correctly.

usage.name: Export traceability records
usage.statement: Export selected traceability records from the system.
usage.description: This makes package histories available for external review, exchange, analysis, or evidence needs.

usage.name: Generate production reports
usage.statement: Generate reports on read rates, rejects, and throughput from captured traceability data.
usage.description: Produces structured operational summaries covering scanner read-rate performance, unit rejection counts, and line throughput metrics derived from the traceability data store.

usage.name: Handle duplicate identifiers
usage.statement: Resolve duplicate identifier exceptions detected during line operation.
usage.description: This prevents conflicting identity records from compromising package traceability.

usage.name: Load batch parameters
usage.statement: Load the batch, lot, and product parameters into the system before starting a run.
usage.description: Each new order or SKU requires loading the correct coding and traceability parameters before running.

usage.name: Monitor line status
usage.statement: Monitor traceability system status and read rates during active production runs.
usage.description: Observes real-time system indicators, camera/scanner health, and code-read success rates to detect degradation before it affects traceability integrity.

usage.name: Query package genealogy
usage.statement: Query the traceability system for a package genealogy record.
usage.description: This retrieves the recorded relationships and events associated with a selected identifier.

usage.name: Reconcile missing codes
usage.statement: Reconcile units with missing or damaged codes by manually re-identifying them during use.
usage.description: Damaged codes or missed units sometimes require manual re-identification to keep records complete.

usage.name: Reconcile package counts
usage.statement: Reconcile tracked package counts against physical line processing counts.
usage.description: Compares the traceability system's unit count records with actual line counters or physical tallies to identify and resolve discrepancies in recorded versus processed quantities.

usage.name: Record packaging events
usage.statement: Record each packaging event against the relevant unit identifier in the traceability system.
usage.description: Writes timestamped process events — such as filling, sealing, labelling, and inspection results — against the corresponding unit record to maintain a complete event log.

usage.name: Record traceability data
usage.statement: Record captured identifiers and event data to the traceability repository during runs.
usage.description: Regulatory and audit requirements demand that captured serialization data be recorded and stored.

usage.name: Recover interrupted tracking
usage.statement: Recover traceability tracking after an operational interruption.
usage.description: This preserves record continuity after power loss, network failure, line stoppage, or system restart.

usage.name: Reject non-conforming units
usage.statement: Reject units that fail identification or verification checks from the packaging line.
usage.description: Non-conforming units must be automatically removed to keep untraceable product out of the flow.

usage.name: Report serialization data
usage.statement: Report captured serialization and aggregation data to upstream or downstream systems.
usage.description: Transmits or exports serialization and hierarchical aggregation records to connected internal or regulatory-facing systems at the close of a production event.

usage.name: Resolve missed reads
usage.statement: Resolve package records affected by missed identifier reads.
usage.description: This restores traceability when a package passes a capture point without a valid reading.

usage.name: Switch production context
usage.statement: Switch the active production context within the traceability system at changeover.
usage.description: Selects or activates a new product, batch, or line configuration profile in the system when transitioning between production runs, ensuring identifier scoping and parameters are correctly reset.

usage.name: Trace individual units
usage.statement: Trace an individual unit or lot through the packaging line using recorded traceability data.
usage.description: Queries the recorded identifier and event data to reconstruct the full processing history of a specific unit or lot, supporting investigation or verification workflows.

usage.name: Track package movement
usage.statement: Track each identified package as it moves through the packaging line stations.
usage.description: Maintains a real-time position and status record for each identified package across defined line stations, enabling in-flight visibility into where each unit is in the process.

usage.name: Troubleshoot capture errors
usage.statement: Troubleshoot read failures and reject errors that occur during operation.
usage.description: Faults such as missed reads or jams must be diagnosed and cleared to resume traceable production.

usage.name: Validate aggregation completeness
usage.statement: Validate that each parent packaging level contains the expected number of child units before sealing.
usage.description: Checks that the count of associated child identifiers within a case or pallet record matches the configured fill quantity prior to closing or palletising, preventing under- or over-filled shipment units from leaving the line.

usage.name: Verify code readability
usage.statement: Verify that each applied code is present and machine-readable during production.
usage.description: Confirms that the code on each unit is present and readable so unmarked product does not proceed.

disposal.name: Archive compliance data
disposal.statement: Archive the traceability compliance data to long-term storage for the required retention period.
disposal.description: Matters when retention rules require traceability history to be preserved for a mandated period after decommissioning.

disposal.name: Cancel software licenses and associated services
disposal.statement: Cancel the traceability software licenses, service subscriptions, and any other paid services associated with the retired system.
disposal.description: Formally terminate all active software license agreements, maintenance contracts, and subscription services tied to the retired traceability system to stop ongoing charges and obligations.
disposal.frequency: Once at end of system lifecycle

disposal.name: Deregister serialization identifiers
disposal.statement: Deregister the system's serialization identifiers from external tracking repositories.
disposal.description: Matters when serialization data was registered with government or industry repositories that must be updated at end of life.

disposal.name: Disconnect system integrations
disposal.statement: Disconnect the traceability system from connected enterprise and line-control systems.
disposal.description: Sever all data and control interfaces between the retiring traceability system and upstream/downstream systems such as ERP, MES, and line automation controllers.

disposal.name: Dispose of hazardous materials
disposal.statement: Dispose of hazardous consumables and components from marking and coding units according to regulations.
disposal.description: Matters when marking systems contain inks, solvents, or batteries classified as hazardous materials.

disposal.name: Document decommissioning
disposal.statement: Document the final state and decommissioning of the traceability system for audit records.
disposal.description: Matters because auditors and validation teams require documented evidence of proper decommissioning.

disposal.name: Notify affected stakeholders of system retirement
disposal.statement: Notify internal and external stakeholders of the traceability system retirement.
disposal.description: Communicate the planned or completed retirement of the traceability system to all parties whose operations, compliance obligations, or supply-chain visibility depend on it.

disposal.name: Purge sensitive data from retired system
disposal.statement: Purge sensitive and proprietary data from the traceability system's storage devices.
disposal.description: Remove all confidential, proprietary, and regulated data from system storage to prevent unauthorized access after the system leaves organizational control.

disposal.name: Recycle electronic components
disposal.statement: Recycle the electronic hardware components of the traceability system through certified channels.
disposal.description: Route recoverable circuit boards, displays, sensors, and other electronic assemblies through a certified e-waste recycling process in compliance with applicable environmental regulations.

disposal.name: Remove installed hardware
disposal.statement: Remove traceability scanners, cameras, and marking hardware from the packaging line.
disposal.description: Physically detach and retrieve all hardware components of the traceability system — including readers, cameras, and coders — from their installed production locations.

disposal.name: Return equipment under vendor recovery program
disposal.statement: Return eligible traceability system equipment to the original vendor through an applicable take-back or trade-in program.
disposal.description: Coordinate the logistics, documentation, and credit or disposition terms required to return end-of-life traceability hardware to the original equipment manufacturer or authorized recovery channel.

disposal.name: Revoke access artifacts
disposal.statement: Invalidate all authentication artifacts associated with the retired system.
disposal.description: This prevents obsolete accounts, certificates, keys, or tokens from retaining access to connected resources.

disposal.name: Transfer system assets to designated recipient
disposal.statement: Reassign ownership of the traceability system assets to the designated recipient.
disposal.description: Formally transfer title, documentation, and custody of traceability system hardware and associated assets to the receiving party, whether an internal facility, reseller, or third party.

disposal.name: Transition traceability operations to replacement system
disposal.statement: Transition traceability operations from the retired system to its replacement.
disposal.description: Coordinate the cutover of active traceability functions — including serial number ranges, operational rules, and workflows — from the retiring system to the incoming replacement.

maintenance.name: Archive historical traceability records
maintenance.statement: Archive aged traceability records and logs to maintain system performance and meet long-term retention requirements.
maintenance.description: Move historical serialization records, event logs, and audit trails to long-term storage tiers to free active system capacity while preserving regulatory access.

maintenance.name: Back up system configuration
maintenance.statement: Back up the traceability system configuration.
maintenance.description: Configuration backups enable recovery from hardware failure, corruption, or unintended administrative changes.

maintenance.name: Back up traceability data
maintenance.statement: Back up the serialization, aggregation, and event data captured by the traceability system according to the configured retention policy.
maintenance.description: Perform scheduled or triggered backups of all production traceability records to prevent data loss and support regulatory retention requirements.

maintenance.name: Calibrate vision readers
maintenance.statement: Calibrate the vision systems and code readers of the traceability line.
maintenance.description: Vision and reader accuracy drifts, requiring periodic recalibration against reference standards to maintain read reliability.

maintenance.name: Clean scanning components
maintenance.statement: Clean the cameras, scanners, and optical sensors of the traceability system.
maintenance.description: Remove contamination, dust, and debris from optical reading components to sustain read performance.

maintenance.name: Document maintenance history
maintenance.statement: Document the maintenance, calibration, and validation history of the traceability system.
maintenance.description: Record completed maintenance activities, calibration results, and validation events to support regulatory audit trails and ongoing reliability tracking.

maintenance.name: Inspect hardware for wear
maintenance.statement: Inspect the conveyors, rejectors, sensors, and cabling for wear or damage.
maintenance.description: Conveyors, rejectors, sensors, and cabling wear over time and must be checked to prevent unplanned line stoppages.

maintenance.name: Manage storage capacity
maintenance.statement: Manage the storage capacity allocated to traceability records, images, logs, and backups.
maintenance.description: Monitor consumed and available storage, enforce retention policies, and expand or reconfigure storage resources to prevent capacity-related system failures.

maintenance.name: Monitor system health
maintenance.statement: Monitor the operational status, performance metrics, and alert conditions of the traceability system.
maintenance.description: Continuously observe system health indicators — uptime, read rates, error counts, queue depths, and hardware status — to detect and respond to degradation before line impact.

maintenance.name: Protect system components from environmental damage
maintenance.statement: Protect traceability system hardware from moisture, dust, vibration, and other environmental hazards present in the packaging environment.
maintenance.description: Apply protective covers, enclosures, or environmental controls to cameras, readers, controllers, and cabling to prevent premature failure from harsh production conditions.

maintenance.name: Repair failed components
maintenance.statement: Repair or replace failed readers, printers, controllers, or reject mechanisms.
maintenance.description: Faulty readers, printers, or controllers must be repaired to restore full traceability coverage on the line.

maintenance.name: Replace lighting elements
maintenance.statement: Replace the illumination and lighting elements supporting code reading.
maintenance.description: Reader illumination and lighting elements degrade and require replacement to maintain consistent read quality.

maintenance.name: Replace printing consumables
maintenance.statement: Replace the ink, ribbons, or label media used by the marking and coding units.
maintenance.description: Marking and coding units consume ink, ribbons, or label stock that must be replenished to keep printing serialized codes.

maintenance.name: Revalidate after changes
maintenance.statement: Revalidate the traceability system following configuration or code-format changes.
maintenance.description: After changes to code formats or line configuration, the system must be revalidated to confirm accurate reads and data integrity.

maintenance.name: Schedule preventive maintenance
maintenance.statement: Schedule preventive maintenance activities for the traceability line equipment.
maintenance.description: Plan and coordinate recurring preventive maintenance windows for all traceability hardware and software components to minimize unplanned downtime.

maintenance.name: Store system components properly
maintenance.statement: Store spare traceability system components and consumables under conditions that preserve their functionality and shelf life.
maintenance.description: Follow manufacturer and facility storage guidelines for spare readers, printers, controllers, lighting elements, and consumables to prevent degradation before deployment.

maintenance.name: Synchronize system time
maintenance.statement: Synchronize traceability-system clocks with the designated time source.
maintenance.description: Accurate time synchronization preserves the sequence and correlation of packaging-line events across connected components.

maintenance.name: Test recovery procedures
maintenance.statement: Test the procedures used to restore traceability services after a failure.
maintenance.description: Periodic testing verifies that backups, replacement infrastructure, and recovery instructions can return the system to operation.

maintenance.name: Update application software
maintenance.statement: Update the traceability application software to an approved release.
maintenance.description: Software updates correct defects, improve compatibility, and address security vulnerabilities.

maintenance.name: Update device firmware
maintenance.statement: Update firmware on supported traceability devices to an approved release.
maintenance.description: Firmware maintenance preserves device stability, interoperability, and security over the equipment lifecycle.

maintenance.name: Upgrade system components
maintenance.statement: Upgrade the hardware or software modules of the traceability system.
maintenance.description: Growing volumes, new products, or new coding standards require upgrading modules or capacity to keep pace.

maintenance.name: Verify read performance
maintenance.statement: Verify that the traceability system reliably reads representative package identifiers under production conditions.
maintenance.description: Run structured read-rate verification tests using representative samples to confirm that code readers meet defined performance thresholds before and after maintenance or changeover.

acquisition.name: Arrange support agreement
acquisition.statement: Arrange a service and support agreement for the traceability system.
acquisition.description: Negotiate and finalize post-sale service, maintenance, and support commitments with the traceability system supplier before or at time of purchase.

acquisition.name: Assess integration requirements
acquisition.statement: Assess the system's integration requirements with existing plant systems.
acquisition.description: Identify data interfaces, communication protocols, and middleware needs for connecting a candidate traceability system to plant-floor and enterprise data systems.

acquisition.name: Compare competing systems
acquisition.statement: Compare competing traceability systems on features, accuracy, and total cost.
acquisition.description: Evaluate multiple candidate traceability systems side-by-side using a defined set of acquisition criteria covering technical capabilities, performance, and cost.

acquisition.name: Complete purchase transaction
acquisition.statement: Complete the transaction for the selected packaging line traceability system.
acquisition.description: Execute the final purchase order, payment, and documentation steps to formally acquire the selected traceability system.

acquisition.name: Conduct pre-installation site survey
acquisition.statement: Conduct a pre-installation site survey to verify line dimensions, utilities, and mounting surfaces meet system requirements.
acquisition.description: Assess the physical installation site before equipment arrival to confirm infrastructure readiness and identify any gaps requiring remediation.

acquisition.name: Define system requirements
acquisition.statement: Define the requirements the packaging line traceability system must satisfy.
acquisition.description: Document functional, technical, regulatory, and integration requirements that a traceability system must meet before initiating vendor evaluation.

acquisition.name: Determine total cost of ownership
acquisition.statement: Determine the total cost of ownership for the traceability system.
acquisition.description: Estimate capital, installation, integration, training, maintenance, and end-of-life costs to assess the full financial commitment of acquiring a packaging line traceability system.

acquisition.name: Evaluate line compatibility
acquisition.statement: Evaluate whether a traceability system fits the existing packaging line configuration.
acquisition.description: Assess mechanical, electrical, and software compatibility of candidate traceability systems against the current packaging line layout and throughput requirements.

acquisition.name: Negotiate purchase contract
acquisition.statement: Negotiate the purchase contract and service terms for the traceability system.
acquisition.description: Complex projects require negotiating installation, commissioning, and support terms within the purchase contract.

acquisition.name: Obtain purchase approval
acquisition.statement: Obtain internal budget and procurement approval for the traceability system.
acquisition.description: Capital equipment of this type typically requires internal budget approval and justification.

acquisition.name: Provision administrator access
acquisition.statement: Provision initial administrative access to the acquired packaging line traceability system.
acquisition.description: This establishes the accounts and credentials needed for subsequent system configuration, integration, and user administration.

acquisition.name: Register and license system
acquisition.statement: Register the traceability system and activate its software licenses.
acquisition.description: Software-based systems require license activation and registration before commissioning can begin.

acquisition.name: Request pilot or demonstration
acquisition.statement: Request a pilot or demonstration of the traceability system on sample products.
acquisition.description: A pilot or demo on representative products confirms read rates and code quality before full commitment.

acquisition.name: Research traceability system options
acquisition.statement: Research available packaging line traceability systems and their capabilities.
acquisition.description: Identify and gather information on traceability systems for packaging lines, including vendors, architectures, and feature sets.

acquisition.name: Seek vendor references
acquisition.statement: Seek references and reviews from other users of the traceability system.
acquisition.description: Peer references and case studies reduce risk when committing to a capital traceability investment.

acquisition.name: Select system configuration
acquisition.statement: Select the packaging line traceability system configuration required for the intended deployment.
acquisition.description: Determine the specific hardware, software, and integration configuration of the traceability system to be purchased based on requirements and evaluation outcomes.

acquisition.name: Track order fulfillment
acquisition.statement: Track fulfillment of the packaging line traceability system order.
acquisition.description: This provides visibility into component availability, shipment status, license delivery, and expected receipt dates.

acquisition.name: Verify received equipment
acquisition.statement: Verify that received traceability equipment is complete, undamaged, and conforms to the purchase order.
acquisition.description: Unpack delivered traceability system hardware, inspect all components against the packing list and purchase order, and confirm no items are missing or damaged before proceeding to installation.

acquisition.name: Verify regulatory compliance support
acquisition.statement: Verify that a traceability system supports required regulatory coding and reporting standards.
acquisition.description: Buyers must confirm the system supports the regulatory coding and reporting standards applicable to their industry.

preparation.name: Calibrate vision inspection
preparation.statement: Calibrate cameras and vision systems for accurate code reading and verification.
preparation.description: Proper calibration prevents false rejects and missed defects at production speeds.

preparation.name: Configure coding parameters
preparation.statement: Configure code formats, serialization schemes, and data structures for products.
preparation.description: Regulatory and customer coding standards dictate the encoded data on each unit.

preparation.name: Configure packaging-line station topology
preparation.statement: Configure the packaging-line station layout within the traceability system.
preparation.description: Define the physical and logical layout of packaging stations so the traceability system correctly tracks product flow.

preparation.name: Configure rejection thresholds and station behavior
preparation.statement: Configure reject thresholds and station behavior for non-conforming units.
preparation.description: Define the conditions under which units are rejected and the automated response at each reject station.

preparation.name: Connect to network
preparation.statement: Connect the traceability system to the plant network and data infrastructure.
preparation.description: Serialization and aggregation data must flow to upstream and downstream systems.

preparation.name: Connect to power supply
preparation.statement: Connect the traceability equipment to the facility power supply.
preparation.description: Devices require stable power before any commissioning or configuration can occur.

preparation.name: Define and configure serialization numbering pools
preparation.statement: Define and configure the serialization number pools and allocation rules used to generate unique identifiers.
preparation.description: Set up the numeric ranges, check-digit rules, and allocation logic that govern how unique serial numbers are generated and reserved to prevent duplication.

preparation.name: Deploy traceability software
preparation.statement: Deploy the traceability software within the designated computing environment.
preparation.description: Deployment makes the system services and operator applications available for configuration.

preparation.name: Establish exception-handling workflows
preparation.statement: Establish the system workflows used to handle traceability exceptions.
preparation.description: Define and configure the process flows the system follows when traceability errors, misreads, or out-of-sequence events occur.

preparation.name: Import product master data
preparation.statement: Import product, batch, and packaging master data into the traceability system.
preparation.description: Accurate product definitions are required before jobs can run against them.

preparation.name: Integrate with enterprise systems
preparation.statement: Integrate the traceability system with ERP, MES, and warehouse management systems.
preparation.description: Serialization data must reconcile with production orders and inventory records.

preparation.name: Integrate with line equipment
preparation.statement: Integrate the traceability system with existing packaging line machinery and PLCs.
preparation.description: Reject stations, conveyors, and labelers must coordinate with the traceability controller.

preparation.name: Load code templates
preparation.statement: Load and assign printing and marking templates for each product SKU.
preparation.description: Correct templates ensure the right layout and data print on each package.

preparation.name: Mount hardware at line stations
preparation.statement: Mount scanners, cameras, and marking devices onto the packaging line at the correct positions.
preparation.description: Physically position and secure traceability hardware components at designated stations along the packaging line.

preparation.name: Set up aggregation hierarchy
preparation.statement: Set up the parent-child aggregation hierarchy linking units to cartons and pallets.
preparation.description: Aggregation defines how individual codes roll up into cases and shipments for traceability.

preparation.name: Set up user access roles
preparation.statement: Set up user accounts, roles, and access permissions for system operators.
preparation.description: Access control ensures only authorized personnel change coding or override rejects.

preparation.name: Synchronize system clocks
preparation.statement: Synchronize the clocks used by traceability system components.
preparation.description: Consistent timestamps are necessary to sequence events correctly across stations, devices, and connected systems.

preparation.name: Train operators on system
preparation.statement: Train line operators to run, monitor, and troubleshoot the traceability system.
preparation.description: Operators must handle job changeovers, rejects, and alarms without stopping production.

preparation.name: Validate system performance
preparation.statement: Validate the traceability system against regulatory and quality qualification protocols.
preparation.description: Regulated industries require documented IQ/OQ/PQ before production release.

usage.name: Adjust changeover configuration
usage.statement: Adjust the filling machinery configuration for a container or product changeover.
usage.description: Reconfiguring filling machine parameters, tooling, and settings to accommodate a different container format or fill product.

usage.name: Adjust operating settings
usage.statement: Adjust the filling machinery settings to meet current operating conditions.
usage.description: Fine adjustments to machine settings are made while observing operation to meet running conditions.

usage.name: Authorize corrective scope
usage.statement: Authorize changes to the maintenance scope during the intervention.
usage.description: Approval is required when diagnosis reveals work beyond the previously accepted scope.

usage.name: Capture maintenance guidance
usage.statement: Capture operating guidance provided by maintenance personnel during a service visit.
usage.description: Recording instructions, tips, and cautions communicated by service technicians to equipment operators for future reference.

usage.name: Communicate machine symptoms
usage.statement: Communicate observed filling-machinery symptoms to maintenance personnel.
usage.description: This gives the service team direct evidence about the condition requiring attention.

usage.name: Coordinate concurrent service teams
usage.statement: Coordinate access among concurrent maintenance teams working on the filling machinery.
usage.description: Coordination prevents interference when multiple specialists service interconnected machinery at the same time.

usage.name: Demonstrate fault behavior
usage.statement: Demonstrate the filling machinery’s fault behavior for maintenance personnel.
usage.description: A controlled demonstration is useful when a condition is intermittent or difficult to describe.

usage.name: Document service performed
usage.statement: Document the service work, parts used, and readings taken during the maintenance visit.
usage.description: Recording all tasks completed, components replaced, measurements captured, and observations made during a maintenance service visit.

usage.name: Execute scheduled maintenance service
usage.statement: Execute a scheduled maintenance service on the filling machinery per the active service plan.
usage.description: Carrying out all planned maintenance tasks within a scheduled service visit to preserve machinery reliability and performance.

usage.name: Monitor maintenance progress
usage.statement: Monitor the maintenance service’s progress against the active work scope.
usage.description: Ongoing visibility supports operational coordination during prolonged or high-impact interventions.

usage.name: Prioritize maintenance task sequence
usage.statement: Prioritize the sequence of maintenance tasks based on machine condition and available downtime.
usage.description: Deciding the order in which service activities are performed to maximize coverage within constrained maintenance windows and address the most critical issues first.

usage.name: Provide remote support
usage.statement: Provide remote diagnostic and troubleshooting support for the filling machinery.
usage.description: Remote diagnostics allow support staff to assess and guide fixes without an on-site visit.

usage.name: Record service outcomes
usage.statement: Record the diagnostic conclusions and corrective actions from the maintenance intervention.
usage.description: Capturing final service outcomes, diagnoses confirmed, and corrective measures taken to create a permanent record for the equipment history.

usage.name: Replace worn components
usage.statement: Replace worn or failed components on the filling machinery during a service intervention.
usage.description: Removing and substituting degraded or failed parts to restore filling machinery to operational specification.

usage.name: Report residual faults
usage.statement: Report faults that remain visible during post-maintenance testing.
usage.description: Prompt reporting allows unresolved conditions to be addressed before the service intervention closes.

usage.name: Restore machinery to production-ready state
usage.statement: Return the filling machinery to a production-ready configuration after maintenance work is complete.
usage.description: Reassembling guards, resetting operating parameters, and confirming all service tools and materials are cleared so the machine is ready to resume production.

usage.name: Review diagnostic findings
usage.statement: Review diagnostic findings produced during the maintenance intervention.
usage.description: This allows the customer to understand the identified condition before further service work proceeds.

usage.name: Run supervised test cycles
usage.statement: Run supervised filling-machine test cycles during service validation.
usage.description: Controlled cycles let maintenance personnel assess performance under normal operating inputs.

usage.name: Supply equipment operating history
usage.statement: Supply the filling machinery's relevant operating history to maintenance personnel.
usage.description: Providing historical run data, prior fault records, and previous service summaries to technicians to support accurate diagnosis and planning.

usage.name: Supply representative test materials
usage.statement: Supply representative production materials for maintenance testing.
usage.description: Actual fill product, containers, closures, or labels may be needed to validate machinery behavior under realistic conditions.

usage.name: Support remote diagnosis
usage.statement: Transmit requested machine evidence during a remote maintenance session.
usage.description: Remote evidence enables maintenance personnel to evaluate machinery when on-site access is unavailable or delayed.

usage.name: Suspend unsafe service work
usage.statement: Suspend maintenance work when an unsafe condition emerges.
usage.description: This interaction is necessary when changing site or machinery conditions create an immediate hazard.

usage.name: Test machine under load
usage.statement: Test the serviced filling machinery under operating load to verify performance.
usage.description: After service, machine performance must be checked under load to confirm the work resolved the issue.

usage.name: Update control software
usage.statement: Update the control software and firmware of the filling machinery during a service visit.
usage.description: Installing current software and firmware releases on filling machinery controllers to address known issues, add features, or maintain vendor support.

usage.name: Verify restored machine function
usage.statement: Verify that the filling machinery performs as expected after maintenance.
usage.description: Customer verification confirms that the serviced machinery is ready for operational release.

usage.name: Verify safety systems
usage.statement: Verify that the safety interlocks and guards on the filling machinery function correctly.
usage.description: Testing each safety interlock, guard, and emergency-stop circuit to confirm correct operation prior to releasing the machine for production.

maintenance.name: Align mechanical assemblies
maintenance.statement: Align filling heads, conveyors, guides, drive components, and container-handling assemblies to specified tolerances.
maintenance.description: This corrects positional deviations that can cause interference, uneven wear, or unreliable machine operation.

maintenance.name: Back up machine settings
maintenance.statement: Back up the filling machinery's recipes and configuration settings.
maintenance.description: Copy and securely store control-system configurations, product recipes, calibration values, and operational parameters to enable rapid recovery after hardware failure or software update.

maintenance.name: Calibrate filling controls and dispensing accuracy
maintenance.statement: Calibrate filling controls, measuring devices, sensors, actuators, and dispensing volume against applicable references to restore target accuracy.
maintenance.description: Unifies the calibration of the filling machinery's volumetric dispensing accuracy with the calibration of its associated controls, sensors, and actuators, ensuring both dimensional and functional accuracy are addressed in a single calibration job.
maintenance.frequency: Per scheduled calibration interval or after corrective intervention

maintenance.name: Diagnose machinery faults
maintenance.statement: Diagnose the source of filling machinery alarms, malfunctions, abnormal behavior, or performance deterioration.
maintenance.description: This isolates the failed or misadjusted element before corrective work begins.

maintenance.name: Document maintenance history
maintenance.statement: Document the filling machinery's maintenance and service history.
maintenance.description: Record inspections, service actions, replaced parts, measurements, faults, and outcomes to support traceability, compliance, and future maintenance planning.

maintenance.name: Flush and purge fluid circuits
maintenance.statement: Flush and purge the filling machinery's fluid circuits, manifolds, and dead legs between product changeovers or extended shutdowns.
maintenance.description: Clear residual product, cleaning agents, or process fluids from internal circuits, manifolds, valves, and dead-leg zones to prevent cross-contamination, corrosion, or biological growth.

maintenance.name: Inspect machinery components and wear parts
maintenance.statement: Inspect filling machinery components and wear parts for wear, damage, leaks, looseness, corrosion, and contamination.
maintenance.description: Combines the broad structural inspection of the filling machinery (wear, leaks, looseness, corrosion, contamination, damage) with the focused inspection of high-wear consumable parts such as nozzles, pistons, and seals, producing a unified condition assessment.
maintenance.frequency: Per maintenance event; at scheduled inspection intervals

maintenance.name: Lubricate moving components
maintenance.statement: Lubricate designated bearings, guides, chains, gears, and other moving components.
maintenance.description: Apply specified lubricants to all designated lubrication points on the filling machinery according to the maintenance schedule and manufacturer lubrication map.

maintenance.name: Monitor machinery health
maintenance.statement: Monitor filling machinery condition indicators, alarms, operating hours, vibration, temperature, and fault trends.
maintenance.description: Continuously track real-time and trended machine health data to detect degradation, predict failures, and trigger timely maintenance interventions.

maintenance.name: Repair machinery defects
maintenance.statement: Repair defective mechanical, electrical, pneumatic, hydraulic, or control-system elements of filling machinery.
maintenance.description: This restores serviceability after a fault, breakdown, or damaged component is confirmed.

maintenance.name: Replace filters and screens
maintenance.statement: Replace the filling machinery's filters and screens on schedule.
maintenance.description: Remove and replace inline filters, strainer screens, and particulate-capture media that protect product pathways and pneumatic/hydraulic circuits.

maintenance.name: Replace scheduled maintenance consumables
maintenance.statement: Replace scheduled maintenance consumables — excluding filters and screens — such as lubricants, belts, and drive components.
maintenance.description: Swap out consumable maintenance items (lubricants, drive belts, timing components, etc.) at prescribed intervals to prevent unplanned downtime; filters and screens are addressed under a separate job.

maintenance.name: Replace seals and gaskets
maintenance.statement: Replace worn seals, gaskets, and O-rings on the filling machinery.
maintenance.description: Seals, gaskets, and O-rings degrade over time and must be swapped to prevent leaks and drips.

maintenance.name: Run clean-in-place cycle
maintenance.statement: Run a clean-in-place sanitation cycle on the filling machinery.
maintenance.description: Execute an automated or manual clean-in-place (CIP) cycle to sanitize internal fluid pathways, tanks, and product-contact circuits without disassembly.

maintenance.name: Schedule preventive service
maintenance.statement: Schedule preventive maintenance for the filling machinery.
maintenance.description: Plan and assign preventive maintenance tasks for filling machinery based on operating hours, calendar intervals, or condition triggers.

maintenance.name: Store machinery properly
maintenance.statement: Store and preserve the filling machinery when not in use.
maintenance.description: When idle or between campaigns the machine must be stored and preserved against corrosion and contamination.

maintenance.name: Test electrical and pneumatic connections
maintenance.statement: Test and reseat electrical connectors, terminal blocks, and pneumatic fittings on the filling machinery.
maintenance.description: Inspect and functionally test wiring harness connectors, terminal-block connections, and pneumatic push-in or threaded fittings for looseness, corrosion, or leakage that may cause intermittent faults.

maintenance.name: Test safety devices
maintenance.statement: Test guards, interlocks, emergency stops, protective sensors, and shutdown devices for correct operation.
maintenance.description: This confirms that machinery safeguards remain functional after service or during preventive inspections.

maintenance.name: Tighten and torque fasteners
maintenance.statement: Inspect and re-torque fasteners, clamps, and fittings on the filling machinery to specified values.
maintenance.description: Check and re-tighten bolted joints, tri-clamp fittings, nozzle retainers, and frame fasteners that may loosen due to vibration and thermal cycling during production.

maintenance.name: Upgrade components
maintenance.statement: Upgrade the filling machinery's components and modules.
maintenance.description: Aging or high-wear components can be upgraded to extend service life or improve performance.

maintenance.name: Verify sensors and controls
maintenance.statement: Verify and adjust the filling machinery's sensors and control systems.
maintenance.description: Check proximity sensors, level sensors, pressure transducers, and control-system outputs for correct response and adjust or replace out-of-tolerance devices.

disposal.name: Archive maintenance records
disposal.statement: Archive the accumulated filling machinery maintenance and inspection records.
disposal.description: Matters when the maintenance relationship ends and accumulated equipment history must be retained by the owner.

disposal.name: Cancel maintenance service agreement
disposal.statement: Cancel the filling machinery maintenance service agreement.
disposal.description: Formally terminate the contractual relationship with the maintenance service provider, ensuring all notice periods and exit clauses are fulfilled.

disposal.name: Disconnect service integrations
disposal.statement: Disconnect maintenance-service integrations from filling machinery systems and business applications.
disposal.description: This stops obsolete data exchanges, automated notifications, or remote connections after the service ends.

disposal.name: Dispose of maintenance waste materials
disposal.statement: Dispose of used lubricants, solvents, and worn parts generated by filling machinery maintenance activities.
disposal.description: Ensures regulated and non-regulated waste produced during maintenance is disposed of through appropriate channels, including hazardous-material handling as required.

disposal.name: Export maintenance history and condition-monitoring data
disposal.statement: Export filling machinery maintenance history and condition-monitoring data from the service system prior to termination.
disposal.description: Ensures the customer retains a portable copy of all maintenance records and sensor/condition data for regulatory compliance, warranty, or handover to a successor provider.

disposal.name: Liquidate spare parts inventory
disposal.statement: Liquidate the remaining spare parts inventory held for filling machinery maintenance.
disposal.description: Matters when leftover spare parts inventory dedicated to the maintenance program must be disposed of or resold.

disposal.name: Notify stakeholders of maintenance service termination
disposal.statement: Notify affected internal stakeholders and regulators that the filling machinery maintenance service has ended.
disposal.description: Ensures all parties dependent on the maintenance service — including operations, compliance, and regulatory bodies — are formally informed of the service end date and implications.

disposal.name: Recover customer-owned materials
disposal.statement: Recover customer-owned parts, documentation, or materials held through the maintenance service.
disposal.description: This restores custody of retained spares or other customer property before the service relationship closes.

disposal.name: Redeploy reusable service assets
disposal.statement: Redeploy reusable maintenance assets to another filling line or facility.
disposal.description: Matters when reusable service assets can be redeployed to another site rather than discarded.

disposal.name: Request final service inspection
disposal.statement: Request a final inspection of the maintained filling machinery before service termination.
disposal.description: This provides a defined closing assessment when machinery condition or service completion must be verified.

disposal.name: Request service data deletion
disposal.statement: Request deletion of confidential data retained by the maintenance service.
disposal.description: This limits continued retention of machinery, facility, operational, or personnel information after termination.

disposal.name: Return provider-owned tooling and equipment
disposal.statement: Return maintenance tooling, diagnostic devices, and loaned equipment to the service provider upon service termination.
disposal.description: Ensures all provider-owned assets used during the maintenance service are collected and returned, closing out asset accountability at service end.

disposal.name: Revoke provider access
disposal.statement: Revoke the maintenance provider's access to facilities and control systems.
disposal.description: Matters when access credentials or system integrations granted to the maintenance provider must be revoked.

disposal.name: Settle final maintenance service charges
disposal.statement: Settle the final charges and close the financial account associated with the filling machinery maintenance service.
disposal.description: Ensures all outstanding invoices, credit notes, and financial obligations related to the maintenance service are resolved before contract closure.

disposal.name: Transfer maintenance responsibility
disposal.statement: Transfer maintenance responsibility and equipment documentation to a successor service provider.
disposal.description: Matters when the current maintenance provider is replaced and continuity of care must pass to a successor.

disposal.name: Transfer open work orders
disposal.statement: Transfer unresolved filling machinery work orders to the responsible recipient.
disposal.description: This prevents active maintenance needs from being abandoned during a provider or ownership transition.

disposal.name: Verify maintenance waste disposition
disposal.statement: Verify the disposition of regulated waste generated through the filling machinery maintenance service.
disposal.description: This provides evidence that removed lubricants, chemicals, contaminated parts, or similar materials received appropriate final handling.

preparation.name: Arrange site access
preparation.statement: Arrange site access for authorized maintenance personnel.
preparation.description: Access preparation prevents security or facility controls from delaying the scheduled service.

preparation.name: Authorize work permits
preparation.statement: Authorize the permits required for the planned maintenance activities.
preparation.description: Review and formally approve the work permits — including hot-work, confined-space, or lockout/tagout authorisations — necessary before maintenance personnel begin servicing the filling machinery.

preparation.name: Configure service notifications
preparation.statement: Configure maintenance service notifications for designated personnel.
preparation.description: Set up automated alerts, escalation rules, and communication channels so that relevant stakeholders are notified of upcoming, in-progress, or completed maintenance events.

preparation.name: Define maintenance scope
preparation.statement: Define the maintenance activities authorised for each filling machinery asset.
preparation.description: Establish and document the boundaries of maintenance tasks — including permissible interventions, exclusions, and escalation thresholds — for each filling machine prior to service.

preparation.name: Disassemble machine guards
preparation.statement: Disassemble the guards and covers to access serviceable components.
preparation.description: Provides physical access to internal mechanisms that require servicing.

preparation.name: Drain residual product
preparation.statement: Drain and purge residual product from the filling system before servicing.
preparation.description: Removes residual product to allow safe and clean access to internal components.

preparation.name: Establish a parts quarantine area
preparation.statement: Establish a designated quarantine area for removed components awaiting inspection or disposal.
preparation.description: Identify and mark a physical area near the work site where disassembled or replaced components are segregated from serviceable parts, preventing accidental reinstallation and enabling traceability.

preparation.name: Gather spare parts
preparation.statement: Gather the spare parts, seals, and consumables required for the maintenance task.
preparation.description: Prevents delays by confirming all consumables and replacement parts are available prior to service.

preparation.name: Inspect filling machine condition
preparation.statement: Inspect the filling machinery to assess wear, damage, and maintenance requirements.
preparation.description: Establishes the baseline condition and scope of work before maintenance begins.

preparation.name: Isolate power sources
preparation.statement: Isolate and lock out the machine's electrical, pneumatic, and hydraulic power sources.
preparation.description: Protects personnel from hazardous energy during maintenance on filling equipment.

preparation.name: Measure component wear
preparation.statement: Measure component wear and tolerances to determine maintenance scope.
preparation.description: Confirms wear-level data to prioritize which components require attention first.

preparation.name: Record baseline settings
preparation.statement: Record the machine's baseline settings and control parameters before adjustment.
preparation.description: Captures pre-service settings so the machine can be restored to production spec afterward.

preparation.name: Register filling machinery assets
preparation.statement: Register each filling machinery asset in the maintenance service record.
preparation.description: Formally enrol each covered filling machine into the maintenance service programme, capturing asset identifiers, location, and configuration baseline for ongoing service tracking.

preparation.name: Retrieve diagnostic data
preparation.statement: Retrieve diagnostic and fault log data from the machine's control system.
preparation.description: Enables condition-based servicing by extracting operational and fault data from the machine.

preparation.name: Review maintenance documentation
preparation.statement: Review the machine's service manuals and maintenance history before starting work.
preparation.description: Ensures the correct manufacturer procedures and specifications are on hand before servicing.

preparation.name: Sanitize work area
preparation.statement: Sanitize the machine surfaces and surrounding work area prior to maintenance.
preparation.description: Establishes a hygienic work zone required when servicing food, beverage, or pharmaceutical fillers.

preparation.name: Schedule maintenance window
preparation.statement: Schedule the maintenance window to align with production downtime.
preparation.description: Plan and coordinate a maintenance time slot that minimises disruption to filling line production schedules.

preparation.name: Set service intervals
preparation.statement: Set the required maintenance interval for each filling machinery asset.
preparation.description: Determine and document the frequency of scheduled maintenance events for each filling machine based on operational parameters, manufacturer guidance, and regulatory requirements.

preparation.name: Stage tools, instruments, and replacement parts
preparation.statement: Stage the tools, calibration instruments, and replacement parts required for the planned maintenance work.
preparation.description: Covers the physical assembly and staging of all service tooling, calibration equipment, and replacement components needed before maintenance begins, ensuring technicians have everything on hand at the point of work.

preparation.name: Train service technicians on maintenance procedures
preparation.statement: Train service technicians on the maintenance procedures for filling machinery.
preparation.description: Deliver structured instruction to maintenance personnel covering filling machinery service methods, safety protocols, and equipment-specific procedures before they perform work.

preparation.name: Verify calibration status of measurement instruments
preparation.statement: Verify the calibration status of measurement instruments before using them during maintenance.
preparation.description: Confirm that gauges, flow meters, torque wrenches, and other measurement tools are within their calibration validity period and traceable to applicable standards before the maintenance task begins.

acquisition.name: Assign service contacts
acquisition.statement: Assign the operational contacts responsible for maintenance coordination.
acquisition.description: Named contacts give technicians clear points of communication for approvals, access, and escalation.

acquisition.name: Compare service proposals
acquisition.statement: Compare filling machinery maintenance service proposals against documented requirements.
acquisition.description: Proposal comparison distinguishes differences in coverage, service levels, pricing, parts treatment, response commitments, and exclusions.

acquisition.name: Complete filling machinery maintenance service purchase
acquisition.statement: Complete the transaction for the selected filling machinery maintenance service.
acquisition.description: Finalise payment or purchase-order issuance to secure the maintenance service agreement.

acquisition.name: Evaluate in-house versus outsourced maintenance options
acquisition.statement: Evaluate whether to outsource filling machinery maintenance or perform it in-house.
acquisition.description: Assess the operational, financial, and compliance trade-offs between self-performing maintenance and engaging a third-party service provider.

acquisition.name: Evaluate service fit for installed machinery
acquisition.statement: Evaluate whether the maintenance service fits the installed filling machinery.
acquisition.description: Assess the compatibility and suitability of a provider's service scope against the specific filling machinery in the facility.

acquisition.name: Negotiate service contract
acquisition.statement: Negotiate the maintenance service contract scope and pricing.
acquisition.description: Negotiating scope and pricing aligns the service level with budget and operational needs.

acquisition.name: Obtain compliance documentation
acquisition.statement: Obtain the provider's insurance and compliance documentation.
acquisition.description: Insurance and compliance documentation must be obtained before allowing service on site.

acquisition.name: Request service quote
acquisition.statement: Request a quote for the filling machinery maintenance service.
acquisition.description: A formal quote clarifies scope, labor rates, parts, and terms before commitment.

acquisition.name: Research maintenance service providers
acquisition.statement: Research available filling machinery maintenance service providers.
acquisition.description: Identify and gather information on providers offering filling machinery maintenance services prior to evaluation.

acquisition.name: Review service agreement terms
acquisition.statement: Review the maintenance service agreement terms and conditions.
acquisition.description: Contract terms, SLAs, and liability clauses must be reviewed to protect operations.

acquisition.name: Schedule initial service visit
acquisition.statement: Schedule the initial filling machinery maintenance service visit.
acquisition.description: Arrange the timing and logistics for the first on-site maintenance visit following contract execution.

acquisition.name: Seek references and reviews
acquisition.statement: Seek references and reviews for the maintenance service provider.
acquisition.description: References and reviews reveal a provider's reliability and quality of past maintenance work.

acquisition.name: Select filling machinery maintenance service provider
acquisition.statement: Select the filling machinery maintenance service provider that satisfies the purchase criteria.
acquisition.description: Make the final supplier selection decision based on evaluated proposals, credentials, pricing, and coverage fit.

acquisition.name: Verify activation of purchased maintenance service
acquisition.statement: Verify that the purchased filling machinery maintenance service has been activated.
acquisition.description: Confirm with the provider that the service agreement is live, access is provisioned, and the first service cycle is scheduled.

acquisition.name: Verify provider credentials and technician qualifications
acquisition.statement: Verify the maintenance provider's credentials and technician qualifications.
acquisition.description: Confirm that the service provider holds appropriate certifications and that assigned technicians meet required qualification standards.

usage.name: Adjust dose parameters mid-delivery
usage.statement: Modify a permitted dose rate or quantity parameter on the dosing system while delivery is underway.
usage.description: Authorized operator makes an in-session adjustment to dosing parameters without stopping the current delivery cycle.

usage.name: Deliver a bolus dose
usage.statement: Administer a rapid supplemental dose through the dosing system on demand during an active session.
usage.description: Covers the operator action of commanding the dosing system to deliver a discrete supplemental quantity above the ongoing programmed rate, as authorised and configured within the system.
usage.frequency: On demand during active dosing sessions; frequency varies by clinical need

usage.name: Deliver dose in transit
usage.statement: Operate the dosing system while the patient is being transported or moving.
usage.description: Sustains uninterrupted precision dosing in mobile or field conditions.

usage.name: Diagnose active dosing fault
usage.statement: Use the dosing system's operational data to identify the cause of an active fault during a dosing session.
usage.description: Technician or operator reviews system-reported fault information to determine root cause and appropriate corrective action without stopping operation if possible.

usage.name: Handle dosing system input variation
usage.statement: Adjust dosing system operation to accommodate variation in the physical or chemical properties of the substance being delivered.
usage.description: Operator modifies settings or technique when the substance's viscosity, concentration, temperature, or other properties fall outside standard parameters during active delivery.

usage.name: Initiate dosing cycle
usage.statement: Start the configured dosing cycle on the dosing system to begin active delivery.
usage.description: Covers the operator action of commanding the dosing system to commence a programmed delivery cycle, transitioning the system from a ready/configured state to active dosing.
usage.userGroup: Clinical staff, pharmacists, process operators, manufacturing technicians
usage.frequency: Once per dosing session or batch run

usage.name: Load Pharmaceutical Source
usage.statement: Load the pharmaceutical source container into the dosing system.
usage.description: This places the required medication, formulation, ingredient, or solution into the system before dosing begins.

usage.name: Manage dosing system output
usage.statement: Manage the routed output of the dosing system to ensure delivered substance reaches the intended target pathway.
usage.description: Operator oversees or adjusts the output routing — tubing, port, channel assignment — of the dosing system during active delivery to confirm substance is directed correctly.

usage.name: Monitor active delivery status
usage.statement: Observe the dosing system's real-time delivery status during active administration.
usage.description: Operator continuously tracks delivery progress indicators on the dosing system throughout a live dosing session.

usage.name: Monitor system status
usage.statement: Monitor the operating status and health of the system.
usage.description: Real-time monitoring of dose accuracy, pressure, and error states enables early detection of drift or failure.

usage.name: Operate concurrent dosing channels
usage.statement: Run multiple simultaneous dose deliveries across parallel channels on the dosing system.
usage.description: Operator manages several independent delivery channels running in parallel on a multi-channel dosing system.

usage.name: Operate dosing system in training mode
usage.statement: Use the dosing system's simulation or training mode to demonstrate or rehearse dosing procedures without delivering actual substance.
usage.description: Covers operating the dosing system in a non-delivery simulation mode for the purpose of training, competency assessment, or procedure rehearsal by operators or trainees.
usage.userGroup: Clinical educators, training coordinators, operators-in-training, biomedical engineers conducting demonstrations
usage.frequency: Periodically; during onboarding and competency re-assessment cycles

usage.name: Record active delivery events
usage.statement: Capture the dosing system's administration events and delivered volumes during an active session.
usage.description: Operator or system logs real-time delivery data including timestamps, quantities, and delivery events for audit and review.

usage.name: Respond to active delivery alarm
usage.statement: Acknowledge and act on an alarm raised by the dosing system during active operation.
usage.description: Operator reviews alarm information and takes corrective or confirmatory action to address the alert condition.

usage.name: Resume dose delivery
usage.statement: Resume a previously paused dose delivery cycle.
usage.description: This continues dosing from a controlled interruption without initiating an unrelated cycle.

usage.name: Run repeated-dose sequence
usage.statement: Execute a configured sequence of repeated dosing cycles on the dosing system.
usage.description: Operator initiates and oversees an automated sequence of multiple consecutive dosing cycles as defined by the delivery protocol.

usage.name: Select dosing mode
usage.statement: Select the intended operating mode on the dosing system for the upcoming delivery cycle.
usage.description: Operator chooses among available dosing modes (e.g., continuous, intermittent, bolus) to match the required delivery profile.

usage.name: Self-administer scheduled dose
usage.statement: Operate the dosing system to receive one's own medication at prescribed intervals.
usage.description: Supports patient self-dosing outside clinical supervision.

usage.name: Switch medication reservoir
usage.statement: Replace or change the medication source feeding the dosing system during use.
usage.description: Maintains continuous dosing across different agents or refills mid-therapy.

usage.name: Terminate dosing session
usage.statement: Stop and close out the dosing system's active delivery at the end of or before the configured endpoint of an administration session.
usage.description: Covers the operator action of ending an active dosing session, whether at the planned endpoint or earlier than configured, and transitioning the system to a stopped/safe state.
usage.userGroup: Clinical staff, pharmacists, process operators, manufacturing technicians

usage.name: Verify delivered dose quantity
usage.statement: Verify that the quantity delivered by the dosing system matches the programmed target value.
usage.description: Operator cross-checks the dosing system's reported output against the intended dose to confirm accuracy.

disposal.name: Archive device history records
disposal.statement: Archive the complete device history record of the retired system for the required regulatory retention period.
disposal.description: Compile and store all maintenance logs, calibration records, repair history, and usage data in a durable archive that satisfies applicable device-record retention requirements after the system is decommissioned.

disposal.name: Cancel connected services
disposal.statement: Cancel software subscriptions, calibration contracts, and support services linked to the retired dosing system.
disposal.description: Covers the administrative actions required to terminate all ongoing service relationships — software licenses, calibration service agreements, remote monitoring subscriptions, and technical support contracts — associated with a dosing system being decommissioned.
disposal.userGroup: Procurement staff, IT administrators, biomedical engineers, facilities managers

disposal.name: Decommission the dosing system
disposal.statement: Withdraw the precision dosing system from active service at end of its service life.
disposal.description: Formally remove the system from operational use, completing all required administrative and physical steps to retire the unit.

disposal.name: Decontaminate the dosing system
disposal.statement: Decontaminate the system to render it safe for handling, transport, and downstream disposal.
disposal.description: Apply validated decontamination procedures to all external and internal surfaces of the unit before it is moved or handed over for disposal.

disposal.name: Deregister the asset
disposal.statement: Remove the system from the device inventory, asset registry, and regulatory device listings.
disposal.description: Update all internal asset management systems and, where required, notify regulatory bodies to remove the unit from the active device register.

disposal.name: Discard biohazardous disposables
disposal.statement: Place contaminated dosing disposables into an approved biohazard waste stream.
disposal.description: This controls exposure from used reservoirs, tubing, cartridges, needles, or other medication-contact components.

disposal.name: Dispose of pharmaceutical waste
disposal.statement: Dispose of expired or unused medication associated with the system through regulated channels.
disposal.description: Matters because expired or unused pharmaceutical stock associated with the system requires regulated destruction.

disposal.name: Document disposal for compliance
disposal.statement: Document the final state and disposal method of the system for regulatory and audit records.
disposal.description: Create and retain records covering the system's condition at retirement, the disposal pathway taken, and any certificates of destruction or recycling as required by regulation.

disposal.name: Export dosing records
disposal.statement: Export stored dosing and event records from the system prior to disposal.
disposal.description: Extract all historical dosing logs and event data from the system's storage to an external medium or system for retention and audit purposes.

disposal.name: Notify stakeholders of system retirement
disposal.statement: Notify affected stakeholders that the dosing system is being taken out of service.
disposal.description: Communicate the planned or completed retirement of the unit to all relevant internal and external parties, including clinical staff, procurement, and service providers.

disposal.name: Obtain certificate of destruction or disposal
disposal.statement: Obtain a formal certificate of destruction or disposal from the authorised disposal vendor.
disposal.description: Secure written confirmation from the third-party recycler, scrapper, or disposal facility that the unit has been processed in accordance with applicable regulations, for use as compliance evidence.

disposal.name: Purge patient data
disposal.statement: Remove all stored patient and configuration data from the system's memory prior to disposal.
disposal.description: Erase protected personal and clinical data held in the device to comply with privacy regulations before the unit leaves the operator's custody.

disposal.name: Recycle recoverable components
disposal.statement: Segregate and route recoverable materials from the retired system to approved recycling streams.
disposal.description: Identify reusable or recyclable parts of the decommissioned unit and direct them to appropriate recycling or refurbishment channels in compliance with applicable regulations.

disposal.name: Remove hazardous components
disposal.statement: Remove batteries and other hazardous components from the system for separate regulated disposal.
disposal.description: Disassemble the unit to extract batteries, capacitors, or other hazardous materials that require separate handling under waste regulations.

disposal.name: Remove residual medication
disposal.statement: Remove residual pharmaceutical material from the dosing system's reservoirs and fluid pathways prior to decommissioning.
disposal.description: Covers the emptying and flushing of all pharmaceutical substance from reservoirs, tubing, cartridges, and fluid-contact pathways to prepare the system for safe handling, decontamination, and downstream disposal processing.
disposal.userGroup: Clinical staff, pharmacists, biomedical engineers, decommissioning technicians

disposal.name: Segregate disposable components
disposal.statement: Separate single-use and disposable system components into their designated waste streams.
disposal.description: Sort consumable and single-use parts of the system (e.g., tubing sets, filters, cartridges) into the correct regulated waste categories prior to collection.

disposal.name: Transfer ownership
disposal.statement: Transfer ownership of the functional system to a new operator or facility.
disposal.description: Matters when a still-functional system is transferred to another facility or resold on the secondary market.

disposal.name: Transition dosing operations to replacement system
disposal.statement: Transition dosing operations from the retired system to its designated replacement.
disposal.description: Plan and execute the operational handover so that dosing continuity is maintained while the old system is retired and the replacement becomes the active unit.

preparation.name: Assemble dosing system components
preparation.statement: Assemble the pump, reservoir, delivery, and ancillary components into a complete, operational dosing unit.
preparation.description: Covers mechanical assembly of all sub-components into the required operating configuration prior to installation.

preparation.name: Calibrate dosing mechanism
preparation.statement: Calibrate the dosing mechanism against a traceable measurement reference to verify delivered volumes meet specified tolerances.
preparation.description: Confirms metrological accuracy of the dosing system prior to use or after maintenance, using an appropriate reference standard.

preparation.name: Configure dosing parameters
preparation.statement: Configure dose limits, delivery rates, and operating protocols within the system settings.
preparation.description: Establishes the core dosing parameters governing how the system delivers pharmaceutical material during operation.

preparation.name: Connect fluid pathways
preparation.statement: Connect tubing, cartridges, and fluid lines between the dosing system and the intended delivery point.
preparation.description: Establishes the complete fluid circuit required for pharmaceutical material to flow from source to delivery endpoint.

preparation.name: Connect power supply
preparation.statement: Connect the dosing system to its power source and backup power provisions.
preparation.description: The system needs stable power and, where applicable, backup power for uninterrupted dosing.

preparation.name: Establish documentation and record-keeping baseline
preparation.statement: Establish the required installation, calibration, and configuration records for the dosing system in the applicable quality or compliance documentation system.
preparation.description: Creates the foundational documentation package — installation qualification records, calibration certificates, configuration logs — required before the system enters service in a regulated environment.

preparation.name: Establish operator accounts and access controls
preparation.statement: Provision operator accounts, roles, and access permissions for authorised dosing system users.
preparation.description: Covers creating user credentials, assigning role-based access levels, and configuring lockout or override permissions within the dosing system prior to initial operation.
preparation.userGroup: IT administrators, biomedical engineers, pharmacy informatics staff, department managers
preparation.frequency: Once at initial setup; repeated when user roles change

preparation.name: Install and mount unit
preparation.statement: Install and secure the dosing system in its designated operating location.
preparation.description: The unit must be physically mounted or positioned in its operating location for safe operation.

preparation.name: Integrate with clinical systems
preparation.statement: Integrate the dosing system with pharmacy, dispensing, and electronic health record systems.
preparation.description: Integration with pharmacy, EHR, or dispensing systems enables closed-loop medication workflows.

preparation.name: Learn system operation
preparation.statement: Learn to operate the dosing system through self-guided instruction, documentation, or on-device guidance.
preparation.description: Enables individual operators to acquire the knowledge and skills needed to set up, operate, and respond to the system independently.

preparation.name: Load approved drug library
preparation.statement: Import approved drug definitions, concentration profiles, formulary limits, and dosing rules into the dosing system.
preparation.description: Populates the system with the facility's approved pharmaceutical data set to enable safe dose-limit enforcement.

preparation.name: Perform installation qualification
preparation.statement: Execute a formal installation qualification protocol to verify that the dosing system is installed according to manufacturer specifications and site requirements.
preparation.description: Systematic, documented verification that all physical installation criteria — location, utilities, environmental conditions, ancillary connections — meet defined acceptance criteria before operational qualification proceeds.

preparation.name: Prime dosing pathway
preparation.statement: Prime the dosing system to purge air and establish continuous pharmaceutical fluid flow through the delivery pathway.
preparation.description: Ensures the fluid path is fully charged with liquid and free of air before dosing commences.

preparation.name: Register the dosing system
preparation.statement: Register the pharmaceutical precision dosing system with the manufacturer or applicable authority to activate warranty and support entitlements.
preparation.description: Complete product registration requirements with the manufacturer, regulatory body, or support portal following receipt.

preparation.name: Set safety controls and dose limits
preparation.statement: Set dose limits, alarm thresholds, lockout conditions, and override permissions within the dosing system.
preparation.description: Defines the safety envelope within which the system operates, preventing unsafe dosing events through configured constraints.

preparation.name: Train staff on use
preparation.statement: Train clinical staff to operate the dosing system according to established protocols.
preparation.description: Trainers must instruct staff on safe operation and protocol compliance before clinical use.

preparation.name: Unbox and inspect received system
preparation.statement: Unpack the precision dosing system and inspect all components for shipping damage, missing parts, and specification conformity.
preparation.description: Covers unpacking, visual inspection, and verification of delivered contents against order documentation.

preparation.name: Validate system setup readiness
preparation.statement: Validate the fully configured dosing system by executing required pre-use functional tests before commencing operation.
preparation.description: Confirms that assembly, connections, calibration, and configuration are correct and that the system is safe and ready for use.
