acquisition.name: Assess integration compatibility
acquisition.statement: Assess how a candidate dosing system integrates with existing production lines, control systems, and facility infrastructure.
acquisition.description: Evaluate communication protocols, physical interfaces, utility requirements, and software compatibility of the dosing system relative to the buyer's existing environment.

acquisition.name: Assess pharmaceutical compatibility
acquisition.statement: Assess each candidate system's compatibility with the intended substances, delivery formats, and process conditions.
acquisition.description: Evaluate whether the dosing system materials, contact surfaces, and operating parameters are compatible with the specific compounds or formulations it will handle.

acquisition.name: Complete purchase transaction
acquisition.statement: Complete the purchase transaction and contract terms for the dosing system.
acquisition.description: Matters at the point of commitment to finalize price, warranty, and service terms.

acquisition.name: Evaluate regulatory compliance fit
acquisition.statement: Evaluate whether a candidate dosing system meets applicable regulatory and validation requirements for the intended operating environment.
acquisition.description: Review system documentation, certifications, and validation readiness against the regulatory framework governing the buyer's facility or application.

acquisition.name: Evaluate service and support capabilities
acquisition.statement: Evaluate the service, maintenance support, and spare-parts availability offered by each candidate supplier.
acquisition.description: Assess post-sale support structures including response times, field service coverage, training offerings, and parts supply chain to inform supplier selection for a long-lifecycle capital asset.

acquisition.name: Evaluate total cost of ownership
acquisition.statement: Evaluate the total cost of ownership of candidate dosing systems over their expected operational life.
acquisition.description: Estimate and compare acquisition price, installation, consumables, maintenance, calibration, and end-of-life costs across candidate systems to support a financially sound purchasing decision.

acquisition.name: Obtain reference site visits or demonstrations
acquisition.statement: Obtain access to reference installations or live demonstrations of candidate dosing systems.
acquisition.description: Arrange visits to existing customer sites or request hands-on demonstrations to observe system performance, usability, and reliability under real or simulated operating conditions.

acquisition.name: Qualify the supplier
acquisition.statement: Qualify the supplier's quality systems and audit history before commitment.
acquisition.description: Matters in regulated environments where supplier qualification is a prerequisite for purchase.

acquisition.name: Request supplier quotations
acquisition.statement: Request price and delivery quotations from qualified dosing system suppliers.
acquisition.description: Matters when moving from evaluation toward budgeting and vendor selection.

acquisition.name: Research dosing system options
acquisition.statement: Research available pharmaceutical precision dosing systems and their capabilities.
acquisition.description: Identify and gather information on precision dosing systems on the market, including technical specifications and supplier landscape.

acquisition.name: Secure procurement approval
acquisition.statement: Secure internal approval to procure the selected dosing system.
acquisition.description: Navigate financial, legal, and governance approval workflows required to authorise the capital purchase.

acquisition.name: Track equipment shipment
acquisition.statement: Track the shipment and delivery status of the ordered dosing system.
acquisition.description: Matters between order and receipt to coordinate installation resources and downtime.

acquisition.name: Verify dosing performance evidence
acquisition.statement: Verify the dosing accuracy and performance evidence provided for each candidate system.
acquisition.description: Review test data, validation reports, and performance claims to confirm they substantiate the stated accuracy and reliability of candidate dosing systems.

maintenance.name: Back up system data
maintenance.statement: Back up the dosing system's configuration, calibration, dosing records, and maintenance data to a secure location.
maintenance.description: Covers the periodic export and secure storage of all system data — including dosing event records, configuration settings, calibration data, and maintenance logs — to protect against data loss and support audit and requalification activities.
maintenance.userGroup: Biomedical engineers, IT administrators, pharmacy informatics staff, quality assurance personnel
maintenance.frequency: Periodically per site data-management schedule; before any system change

maintenance.name: Document maintenance history
maintenance.statement: Document each maintenance action in the dosing system's service record.
maintenance.description: Records completed maintenance, calibration, and repair activities with date, performer, and findings to support regulatory traceability and equipment qualification.

maintenance.name: Inspect wetted parts and seals
maintenance.statement: Inspect the wetted parts and seals of the dosing system for wear or damage.
maintenance.description: Visually and tactilely examines fluid-contact components and sealing elements to detect degradation, cracking, or deformation that could compromise dosing integrity or sterility.

maintenance.name: Protect system during storage
maintenance.statement: Store and protect the dosing system under controlled environmental conditions during periods of non-use.
maintenance.description: Covers preparing the system for storage (e.g., cleaning, powering down, applying protective covers) and maintaining it in controlled conditions to prevent environmental degradation, contamination, or damage during idle periods.
maintenance.userGroup: Biomedical engineers, equipment custodians, facilities staff
maintenance.frequency: As required during periods of planned non-use

maintenance.name: Repair faulty components
maintenance.statement: Repair faulty components to restore the dosing system to operational specification.
maintenance.description: Diagnoses and repairs failed or out-of-spec components — mechanical, electrical, or software — to return the dosing system to its qualified operating state.

maintenance.name: Replace consumable components
maintenance.statement: Replace worn or depleted consumable components of the system.
maintenance.description: Consumable components such as seals, filters, tubing, and cartridges degrade with use and must be swapped to preserve performance.

maintenance.name: Replace filtration elements
maintenance.statement: Replace the filtration elements in the dosing system's fluid or air circuits.
maintenance.description: Removes and replaces inline filters, membrane filters, or air filters in the system's fluid-path or environmental control circuits to maintain flow performance and prevent contamination.

maintenance.name: Replace power components
maintenance.statement: Replace depleted or degraded power-storage components.
maintenance.description: Battery replacement preserves portable operation, clock retention, alarm availability, or safe shutdown capability.

maintenance.name: Requalify system performance
maintenance.statement: Requalify the dosing system against its validated performance criteria after maintenance or change.
maintenance.description: Executes a formal requalification protocol to confirm that the dosing system continues to meet its validated performance specifications following maintenance, repair, or component change.

maintenance.name: Schedule preventive service
maintenance.statement: Schedule preventive maintenance service for the system.
maintenance.description: Scheduled preventive service reduces unplanned downtime and keeps the system within qualified operating parameters.

maintenance.name: Service mechanical assemblies
maintenance.statement: Service the dosing system's mechanical drive assemblies.
maintenance.description: Specified servicing limits friction, binding, backlash, or wear that could reduce dosing consistency.

maintenance.name: Sterilize product-contact surfaces
maintenance.statement: Sterilize the product-contact surfaces of the dosing system.
maintenance.description: Applies an approved sterilization method to all surfaces that contact the pharmaceutical product, ensuring microbial control between production runs or after maintenance.

maintenance.name: Test safety functions
maintenance.statement: Test the dosing system's alarms, interlocks, and shutdown functions.
maintenance.description: Functional testing confirms that protective controls respond correctly to abnormal operating conditions.

maintenance.name: Update control software
maintenance.statement: Update the control software and firmware of the dosing system.
maintenance.description: Installs approved software and firmware releases on the dosing system's control platform to incorporate patches, regulatory compliance changes, or new functionality.

maintenance.name: Upgrade system components
maintenance.statement: Upgrade the modular components of the system.
maintenance.description: Upgrading modules or components extends capability and keeps the system current with evolving process requirements.

maintenance.name: Verify dosing accuracy
maintenance.statement: Verify the dosing system's delivered quantity against a traceable reference.
maintenance.description: Performs a check — without mechanical adjustment — to confirm that the dosing system delivers within its specified accuracy limits, generating a record for quality and regulatory purposes.

usage.name: Adjust mixing parameters
usage.statement: Adjust mixing parameters during the batch to achieve target consistency.
usage.description: Tuning speed, temperature, or timing during use accommodates ingredient behavior.

usage.name: Adjust process setpoints
usage.statement: Adjust an active process setpoint — including temperature, flow, timing, and dosing parameters — through the batching system controls.
usage.description: Combines controlling batch temperature (#56) with adjusting process setpoints (#74). Temperature control is a specific instance of setpoint adjustment; #74 is the correct Level-1 framing that subsumes #56.

usage.name: Coordinate multi-operator use
usage.statement: Coordinate batching system use across multiple operators during production.
usage.description: Manage task handoffs, access, and sequencing when more than one operator is working with the batching system simultaneously or across shifts.

usage.name: Diagnose process deviations
usage.statement: Diagnose a process deviation from the system's operational data.
usage.description: This helps isolate abnormal ingredient delivery, vessel conditions, control responses, or equipment behavior during use.

usage.name: Discharge finished batch
usage.statement: Discharge the finished batch from the system to downstream processes.
usage.description: Operate the system's discharge or transfer function to move the completed liquid batch to the next process step, storage, or filling line.

usage.name: Draw in-process sample
usage.statement: Draw an in-process sample from the system's designated sampling point.
usage.description: This supports intermediate evaluation of the liquid before the batch cycle is completed.

usage.name: Execute addition sequence
usage.statement: Execute the batching system's programmed ingredient-addition sequence.
usage.description: Combines operating the agitation function (#55) with executing the ingredient-addition sequence (#73). Agitation is an embedded function within the addition sequence execution; both are sub-activities of running a batch cycle, with #73 being the correct Level-1 framing.

usage.name: Handle input variations
usage.statement: Handle variations in incoming ingredient properties during batching.
usage.description: Accommodating changes in ingredient viscosity, temperature, or concentration keeps batches on-target.

usage.name: Load ingredient components
usage.statement: Load ingredient components into the batching system for a new batch.
usage.description: Physically or automatically load raw liquid and/or dry ingredients into the system's intake for a new production batch.

usage.name: Monitor batch progress
usage.statement: Monitor batch progress and system status during operation.
usage.description: Observe live system indicators, alarms, and process data to track batch status and detect anomalies throughout the batching cycle.

usage.name: Operate system in manual mode
usage.statement: Operate the batching system in manual mode to complete a batch step.
usage.description: Bypass automated sequencing and control individual system functions (pumps, valves, agitators) manually to complete a batch step during trials, failures, or non-standard conditions.

usage.name: Pause batch cycle
usage.statement: Pause the active batch cycle through the system controls.
usage.description: This limits further processing when an input, equipment, safety, or quality issue requires intervention.

usage.name: Record batch data
usage.statement: Record batch data for each production run performed on the system.
usage.description: Capture and store process data, timestamps, and batch identifiers generated during a production run for traceability and reporting.

usage.name: Recover interrupted batch
usage.statement: Resume an interrupted batch from an approved system state.
usage.description: This enables controlled continuation after a stoppage without restarting the entire run.

usage.name: Rinse between batches
usage.statement: Rinse the batching system's wetted surfaces between consecutive batch runs.
usage.description: Flush or rinse product-contact surfaces, lines, and vessels between batches of the same or different formulations to prevent cross-contamination or carry-over.

usage.name: Scale batch size
usage.statement: Scale the batch size to match the required production volume.
usage.description: Adjust the system's batch quantity and associated parameters to produce a larger or smaller volume than the default recipe specifies.

usage.name: Start batching cycle
usage.statement: Start the batching cycle to begin preparing the liquid batch.
usage.description: Initiate the automated or manual batching cycle sequence after recipe and quantity parameters are confirmed.

usage.name: Switch between recipes
usage.statement: Switch the batching system between different batch recipes during production.
usage.description: Combines setting batch recipe parameters before a run (#62) with switching between recipes during production (#67). Setting parameters before a run is a sub-step performed when switching between recipes; both address the same usage-phase recipe-selection activity.
usage.frequency: Per recipe change event during production.

usage.name: Transfer batch to intermediate vessel
usage.statement: Transfer the prepared batch to an intermediate holding or surge vessel.
usage.description: Operate the system's transfer function to move a completed batch into a buffer tank or intermediate vessel when direct downstream processing is not immediately available.

usage.name: Troubleshoot batching faults
usage.statement: Troubleshoot batching faults that occur during a production run.
usage.description: Identify and resolve faults or alarms that interrupt or degrade the batching process while the system is in operation.

usage.name: Verify batch accuracy
usage.statement: Verify that the batching system delivers the correct quantity and composition of each batch within specified tolerances.
usage.description: Periodic end-to-end accuracy verification of batch outputs against target recipes to confirm that cumulative instrument drift or mechanical wear has not degraded dosing performance.

usage.name: Verify batch endpoint
usage.statement: Verify the batch endpoint from the batching system's process readings.
usage.description: Confirm that the batch has reached its defined endpoint criteria using process readings available on the system before authorising discharge.

usage.name: Verify batch quality
usage.statement: Verify batch quality against specification during preparation.
usage.description: Sampling or in-line measurement during use confirms the batch meets specification.

disposal.name: Archive lifecycle records
disposal.statement: Archive required lifecycle, maintenance, and disposal compliance records for the retired system.
disposal.description: Combines documenting disposal activities for compliance records (#114) with archiving required lifecycle records (#103). Both describe the same record-archiving obligation at end of life.
disposal.userGroup: Quality assurance, compliance officers, records management.

disposal.name: Conduct end-of-life safety review
disposal.statement: Conduct a safety review of the system before initiating decommissioning activities.
disposal.description: Assess residual hazards — pressure, chemical, electrical, mechanical — present in the system to establish safe decommissioning procedures and protective measures.

disposal.name: Decommission the system
disposal.statement: Decommission the liquid batching and preparation system at end of service life.
disposal.description: Execute all steps required to permanently take the system out of service.

disposal.name: Decontaminate wetted components
disposal.statement: Decontaminate tanks, lines, and mixing components before disposal.
disposal.description: Apply appropriate decontamination procedures to all wetted surfaces and internals to meet safety and regulatory requirements prior to disposal.

disposal.name: Disconnect process interfaces
disposal.statement: Disconnect the system from upstream feeds, downstream lines, and utilities.
disposal.description: Safely isolate the system from all connected process streams and utility supplies before removal.

disposal.name: Dispose of hazardous residues
disposal.statement: Dispose of hazardous and regulated chemical residues retained by the batching system according to applicable regulations.
disposal.description: Combines disposing of hazardous chemical residues (#100) with disposing of regulated residues retained by the system (#119). Both describe the same regulatory disposal obligation for chemical residues.
disposal.userGroup: Environmental health and safety personnel, waste management contractors.

disposal.name: Document final system condition
disposal.statement: Record the system's physical and operational condition at retirement.
disposal.description: Capture the final state of all major components and subsystems to support asset records, warranty claims, or future reference.

disposal.name: Export batch records
disposal.statement: Export historical batch data and recipe records from the control system prior to decommissioning.
disposal.description: Extract all stored batch histories and production records from the system's control platform to ensure data is preserved after the system is retired.

disposal.name: Migrate control configuration
disposal.statement: Transfer the retiring system's required control configuration to its replacement.
disposal.description: Extract and port control logic, parameters, and automation settings from the retiring system to ensure continuity of operations on the replacement platform.

disposal.name: Notify affected stakeholders
disposal.statement: Notify operations, quality, and safety stakeholders of the system removal.
disposal.description: Operations, quality, and safety teams need notice before the system goes offline.

disposal.name: Obtain regulatory disposal approvals
disposal.statement: Obtain required regulatory approvals and permits before disposing of the system.
disposal.description: Identify and secure all permits, notifications, or regulatory clearances required by applicable environmental and safety authorities prior to decommissioning activities.

disposal.name: Prepare for extended storage
disposal.statement: Drain, flush, and protect the batching system for an extended period of inactivity.
disposal.description: Execution of lay-up procedures including draining fluid paths, purging residues, applying protective measures, and securing the system against deterioration during prolonged non-use.

disposal.name: Recycle system materials
disposal.statement: Route recoverable system materials into appropriate recycling streams.
disposal.description: Separate and route metals, piping, electronics, and other recyclable materials from the decommissioned system into applicable recycling channels.

disposal.name: Remove stored proprietary data
disposal.statement: Remove stored proprietary recipes, formulations, and configuration data from the system controller.
disposal.description: Purge all confidential process data and intellectual property from onboard storage before the system is transferred, resold, or scrapped.

disposal.name: Remove system identification markings
disposal.statement: Remove or obliterate identification markings, asset tags, and labels from the system before transfer or disposal.
disposal.description: Strip asset tags, serial-number plates, proprietary identification, and facility-specific markings from the system to protect organisational information and comply with asset management policies.

disposal.name: Return eligible equipment
disposal.statement: Return eligible equipment through its designated return channel.
disposal.description: This fulfills warranty, recall, lease, rental, exchange, or supplier take-back requirements.

disposal.name: Salvage reusable components
disposal.statement: Recover reusable pumps, valves, sensors, and vessels from the system.
disposal.description: Identify and extract components that retain service value for reuse in other systems or as spare parts.

disposal.name: Terminate linked services
disposal.statement: Terminate services, maintenance contracts, calibration agreements, and consumable supply arrangements linked to the retired batching system.
disposal.description: Combines cancelling specific service/calibration/consumable agreements (#101) with terminating all services linked to the retired system (#117). Both describe the same end-of-life service termination activity.

disposal.name: Transfer system ownership
disposal.statement: Transfer or resell the system to a new owner.
disposal.description: Execute the administrative and physical steps required to transfer legal ownership of the system to another party.

disposal.name: Transition batching operations
disposal.statement: Transition batching operations to the replacement system.
disposal.description: Coordinate the handover of production workload, process parameters, and operational responsibilities from the retiring system to its replacement.

acquisition.name: Arrange delivery logistics
acquisition.statement: Arrange transport, rigging, and site-delivery scheduling for the batching system.
acquisition.description: Coordinate with freight and rigging contractors to schedule transport of the system from the supplier or warehouse to the installation site, accounting for equipment size and weight.

acquisition.name: Assess process and material compatibility
acquisition.statement: Assess each candidate system's wetted materials, process conditions, and fluid chemistry suitability against the intended application.
acquisition.description: Combines wetted-material compatibility verification (#2) with process-condition and fluid-chemistry compatibility assessment (#21), which are inseparable aspects of the same compatibility evaluation activity.
acquisition.userGroup: Process engineers, materials engineers, procurement leads.
acquisition.frequency: Once per acquisition event, per candidate system evaluated.

acquisition.name: Compare vendor proposals
acquisition.statement: Compare competing suppliers' specifications, pricing, terms, and technical documentation for the batching system.
acquisition.description: Requesting technical documentation (#19) is a step performed within vendor proposal comparison (#4). The output — a comparative assessment of suppliers — is identical. Retaining #4's framing, enriched to include documentation.
acquisition.userGroup: Procurement leads, process engineers, project managers.

acquisition.name: Complete purchase transaction
acquisition.statement: Complete the purchase contract, payment, and warranty terms for the system.
acquisition.description: The buyer completes contract terms, payment, and warranty arrangements to finalize the purchase.

acquisition.name: Confirm lead time and availability
acquisition.statement: Confirm the supplier's manufacturing lead time and component availability for the specified system configuration.
acquisition.description: Obtain a firm lead-time commitment and confirm that long-lead components (e.g., specialised instrumentation, custom vessels) are available to meet the project schedule.

acquisition.name: Define system requirements
acquisition.statement: Define the functional, capacity, and process requirements that the liquid batching and preparation system must satisfy.
acquisition.description: Research of available system types (#15) is a sub-activity performed while defining requirements (#18). The output of both is the same: a defined requirements baseline. Retaining #18's more precise framing.
acquisition.userGroup: Process engineers, project managers, procurement leads.

acquisition.name: Evaluate capacity and specification fit
acquisition.statement: Evaluate whether candidate systems' capacity, throughput, and technical specifications match defined production requirements.
acquisition.description: Combines evaluation of batch capacity and throughput (#1) with comparison of technical specifications against requirements (#20), which are performed in the same decision activity and overlap in scope.
acquisition.userGroup: Process engineers, procurement leads, project managers.

acquisition.name: Evaluate regulatory compliance evidence
acquisition.statement: Evaluate compliance documentation, certifications, and regulatory evidence supplied by candidate system suppliers.
acquisition.description: Combines confirming the system meets certification standards (#3) with evaluating the compliance documentation and evidence supplied (#23). Both jobs occur in the same evaluation step and address the same compliance gate.
acquisition.userGroup: Procurement leads, quality engineers, regulatory affairs personnel.
acquisition.frequency: Once per acquisition event, per candidate system evaluated.

acquisition.name: Evaluate rental options
acquisition.statement: Evaluate rental or lease arrangements for temporary or trial batching capacity.
acquisition.description: Some batching needs are temporary or trial, making rental or lease options relevant.

acquisition.name: Inspect received equipment
acquisition.statement: Inspect and verify the delivered system against the order for completeness, transit damage, missing components, and contamination.
acquisition.description: Combines [Acquisition] #17 (inspect delivered system for completeness and damage) with [Preparation] #41 (inspect for transit damage, missing components, contamination). Both describe the same receive-and-inspect activity; it belongs in Acquisition as it occurs at point of delivery before preparation begins.
acquisition.userGroup: Receiving personnel, project managers, commissioning engineers.

acquisition.name: Place system order
acquisition.statement: Place the purchase order for the approved liquid batching and preparation system.
acquisition.description: Issue the formal purchase order to the selected supplier, confirming configuration, price, delivery schedule, and contractual terms.

acquisition.name: Qualify prospective suppliers
acquisition.statement: Qualify prospective suppliers of liquid batching and preparation systems against organisational procurement criteria.
acquisition.description: Assess supplier financial stability, quality management systems, manufacturing capability, references, and compliance with procurement standards before approving them for competitive evaluation.

acquisition.name: Register the system
acquisition.statement: Register the delivered system with the manufacturer for warranty activation and support entitlement.
acquisition.description: Submit serial numbers, installation site, and owner information to the manufacturer to activate warranty coverage and establish eligibility for technical support services.

acquisition.name: Request supplier quotation
acquisition.statement: Request a quotation for the selected liquid batching and preparation system configuration.
acquisition.description: This obtains pricing, scope, options, exclusions, lead time, payment terms, warranty coverage, and delivery conditions for procurement review.

acquisition.name: Review acquisition scope
acquisition.statement: Review the quoted acquisition scope for the liquid batching and preparation system.
acquisition.description: This identifies omissions, substitutions, assumptions, interfaces, optional components, documentation deliverables, and commercial limitations before commitment.

acquisition.name: Secure purchase approval
acquisition.statement: Obtain budget authorization and complete the procurement approval process for the system.
acquisition.description: Large capital equipment purchases require formal budgeting, approval, and procurement processes.

acquisition.name: Seek peer references
acquisition.statement: Seek references from existing users of candidate liquid batching and preparation systems.
acquisition.description: Contact reference customers provided by shortlisted suppliers, or peer networks, to gather first-hand operational experience with the system before committing to purchase.

acquisition.name: Specify system configuration
acquisition.statement: Specify the required components, options, and configuration for the batching system order.
acquisition.description: Custom-engineered systems require specification of options, materials, and configuration before ordering.

acquisition.name: Track system shipment
acquisition.statement: Track the shipment of the liquid batching and preparation system.
acquisition.description: This allows receiving personnel to prepare for arrival while responding to delays, split shipments, route changes, or special handling events.

acquisition.name: Verify site compatibility
acquisition.statement: Verify each candidate system's dimensions, utility requirements, and connectivity against site constraints and installation conditions.
acquisition.description: Combines assessing footprint, utility, and connectivity requirements against the site (#5) with verifying dimensions, utilities, and connectivity against site constraints (#22). Identical evaluation activity stated twice.
acquisition.userGroup: Facilities engineers, process engineers, project managers.
acquisition.frequency: Once per acquisition event, per candidate site/system combination.

acquisition.name: Witness system demonstration
acquisition.statement: Witness a demonstration of a candidate liquid batching and preparation system.
acquisition.description: This provides direct evidence of system operation, control behavior, batching performance, accessibility, and operator interaction before purchase.

preparation.name: Assemble system modules
preparation.statement: Assemble the supplied tanks, pumps, mixers, dispensers, sensors, and support modules into the complete batching system.
preparation.description: Mechanically assemble all major and ancillary system components — including structural supports, vessels, rotating equipment, instrumentation, and dispensing hardware — into the integrated system configuration.

preparation.name: Clean product-contact surfaces
preparation.statement: Clean the tanks, lines, pumps, mixers, valves, and dispensing points before introducing batch materials.
preparation.description: Pre-use cleaning removes residues or contaminants that could compromise the first subsequent batch.

preparation.name: Commission system with validation batch
preparation.statement: Commission the assembled system by running a validation batch to verify correct operation before production use.
preparation.description: Execute a controlled test or validation batch through the fully assembled and configured system to confirm that all components, controls, interlocks, and measurements perform to specification.

preparation.name: Configure batch recipes
preparation.statement: Configure the system-specific quantities, sequences, mixing times, temperatures, and dosing rates for each batch recipe in the control system.
preparation.description: Combines configuring batch parameters (#30 — recipes, fill volumes, mixing sequences) with configuring batch recipes (#36 — quantities, sequences, mixing times, temperatures, dosing rates). Both describe the same preparation activity at equivalent specificity.
preparation.userGroup: Process engineers, system operators, batch technicians.
preparation.frequency: Once at commissioning; repeated when new recipes are added.

preparation.name: Connect fluid piping
preparation.statement: Connect the inlet, outlet, and recirculation piping to the batching system.
preparation.description: Fluid pathways must be joined so ingredients can be transferred through the system.

preparation.name: Connect power and utility supplies
preparation.statement: Connect the batching system to its required electrical, pneumatic, and other utility supply sources.
preparation.description: Terminate and verify all electrical, pneumatic, and ancillary utility connections needed to energise the system, following applicable codes and specifications.

preparation.name: Connect process piping
preparation.statement: Connect the system to the required material inlet, transfer, recirculation, and discharge piping.
preparation.description: Install and connect all process fluid piping runs — including inlet feeds, inter-vessel transfer lines, recirculation loops, and discharge headers — between the batching system and plant infrastructure.

preparation.name: Document system configuration
preparation.statement: Document the as-installed configuration, calibration records, and parameter settings of the batching system.
preparation.description: Capture and record the final installed configuration — including pipe routing, instrument calibration certificates, control parameter settings, recipe data, and access permission structures — as the baseline reference for future maintenance, audits, and regulatory review.

preparation.name: Establish user access
preparation.statement: Establish user accounts and access permissions for the control system.
preparation.description: Access levels and user credentials control who can edit recipes and operate the system.

preparation.name: Import recipe data
preparation.statement: Import existing recipe formulations and product data into the batching system.
preparation.description: Recipe libraries, formulations, and historical data may need to be loaded from existing systems.

preparation.name: Map ingredient sources
preparation.statement: Map each ingredient source to its corresponding tank, line, pump, valve, or dosing channel.
preparation.description: Accurate source mapping prevents control commands from routing or dispensing the wrong liquid.

preparation.name: Position and mount equipment
preparation.statement: Position and mount the liquid batching system components at the designated installation location.
preparation.description: Place and secure tanks, pumps, metering hardware, and support structures in their specified positions, accounting for floor loading, access, and process flow.

preparation.name: Prime fluid circuits
preparation.statement: Prime the required pumps, dosing lines, valves, and transfer circuits with the designated liquid.
preparation.description: Priming removes trapped air and prepares fluid-handling components for consistent delivery.

preparation.name: Prime ingredient feeds
preparation.statement: Prime and charge the ingredient feed lines and supply tanks.
preparation.description: Raw ingredient supply tanks and feed lines must be primed and charged before the first batch.

preparation.name: Set operating limits
preparation.statement: Set the system limits for capacity, pressure, temperature, flow, timing, and dosing tolerance.
preparation.description: Operating limits constrain the equipment to ranges appropriate for its configuration and handled liquids.

preparation.name: Test system integrity
preparation.statement: Test assembled vessels, seals, fittings, hoses, and piping for leaks or pressure loss.
preparation.description: Integrity testing detects connection or containment defects before process liquids are handled.

preparation.name: Train system operators
preparation.statement: Train operators to prepare, start, monitor, stop, clean, and reset the batching system.
preparation.description: Deliver structured training to process operators covering system startup, batch execution, monitoring, shutdown, cleaning, and fault response procedures.

preparation.name: Verify safety interlocks and shutdown functions
preparation.statement: Verify the configured safety interlocks, alarms, emergency stop functions, and containment sensors before system startup.
preparation.description: Combines setting up safety interlocks (#32) with verifying them before startup (#50). Verification necessarily follows and encompasses setup; at a Level-1 job granularity these are one activity.
preparation.userGroup: Safety engineers, commissioning engineers, system operators.
preparation.frequency: Once at commissioning; repeated after maintenance affecting safety systems.

maintenance.name: Back up control and recipe data
maintenance.statement: Back up batch recipes, calibration records, controller settings, user configurations, and event logs from the batching system's control platform.
maintenance.description: Periodic export and secure storage of all critical process data and system configurations to enable recovery from data loss, hardware failure, or software corruption.

maintenance.name: Calibrate measurement instruments
maintenance.statement: Calibrate the flow meters, load cells, level sensors, temperature probes, and dosing devices to their reference standards to maintain measurement accuracy.
maintenance.description: Combines initial calibration at commissioning (#44, Preparation) with periodic recalibration during service life (#80, Maintenance). Periodic recalibration is the recurring Maintenance job; initial calibration at commissioning is a sub-step of Preparation commissioning covered by #46. Homing the canonical job in Maintenance.
maintenance.userGroup: Instrumentation technicians, maintenance engineers, quality personnel.
maintenance.frequency: At scheduled calibration intervals (typically annually or per quality plan).

maintenance.name: Document maintenance history
maintenance.statement: Document inspections, calibration results, replacements, repairs, updates, and service dates for the batching system.
maintenance.description: Ongoing recording of all maintenance activities, calibration certificates, and service events to support compliance, audit trails, and equipment lifecycle management.

maintenance.name: Inspect electrical and control connections
maintenance.statement: Inspect electrical wiring, terminal connections, conduit, and control panel components of the batching system for integrity and safety.
maintenance.description: Periodic physical inspection of all electrical infrastructure associated with the batching system to detect loose connections, insulation degradation, corrosion, and potential failure points before they cause downtime or safety incidents.

maintenance.name: Inspect for wear and leaks
maintenance.statement: Inspect the batching system's tanks, piping, pumps, valves, mixers, sensors, and fittings for leaks, corrosion, deterioration, and component wear.
maintenance.description: Combines #81 (inspect for leaks, corrosion, wear) with #95 (inspect tanks, piping, pumps, valves, mixers, sensors, fittings for deterioration, wear, damage). Both describe the same periodic inspection job; #95 adds component specificity to #81.

maintenance.name: Monitor system health
maintenance.statement: Monitor diagnostic alerts, component runtime, process trends, and equipment condition indicators of the batching system.
maintenance.description: Combines #83 (monitor operational health and status) with #97 (monitor diagnostic alerts, component runtime, process trends, condition indicators). #97 adds specificity to #83; both describe the same continuous monitoring job.

maintenance.name: Repair faults and failures
maintenance.statement: Diagnose and repair faults, breakdowns, and failed components in the system.
maintenance.description: Faults, breakdowns, and failed components must be diagnosed and repaired to restore operation.

maintenance.name: Replace filtration elements
maintenance.statement: Replace filters, cartridges, and other consumable filtration elements in the fluid path of the batching system.
maintenance.description: Swap-out of clogged, expired, or damaged filter media and cartridges to sustain flow rate, particulate control, and fluid quality.

maintenance.name: Replace seals and gaskets
maintenance.statement: Replace worn seals, gaskets, O-rings, diaphragms, and packing on pumps, valves, and connections within the batching system.
maintenance.description: Combines #79 (replace seals, gaskets, O-rings on pumps, valves, connections) with #96 (replace worn seals, gaskets, diaphragms, packing). Both describe the same sealing-component replacement job with overlapping component scope.

maintenance.name: Run clean-in-place cycle
maintenance.statement: Run the clean-in-place or sanitization cycle through the piping and vessels of the batching system.
maintenance.description: Execution of an in-situ cleaning or sanitization sequence through the system's internal fluid pathways to remove microbial and chemical residue without disassembly.

maintenance.name: Schedule preventive maintenance
maintenance.statement: Schedule and plan preventive maintenance activities for the batching system using service intervals, operating hours, condition data, and component life limits.
maintenance.description: Systematic planning and calendaring of all scheduled maintenance tasks to minimize unplanned downtime and extend equipment service life.

maintenance.name: Service pumps and agitators
maintenance.statement: Perform routine service, lubrication, and mechanical inspection on pumps, agitators, and mechanical drives.
maintenance.description: Combines lubrication of bearings, gearboxes, shafts, and drives (#85) with routine service and lubrication of pumps and agitators (#82). Lubrication is a sub-activity within pump/agitator servicing; they are the same Maintenance job.
maintenance.frequency: At scheduled service intervals per manufacturer recommendations.

maintenance.name: Test safety interlocks
maintenance.statement: Test emergency stops, guards, alarms, limit switches, and shutdown interlocks.
maintenance.description: Functional testing confirms that protective controls respond correctly when unsafe equipment states occur.

maintenance.name: Update control software
maintenance.statement: Update the control system software and firmware for the batching equipment.
maintenance.description: Application of software patches, firmware revisions, and driver updates to the batching system's control platform to maintain security, compliance, and functionality.

usage.name: Align and engage docking interface
usage.statement: Align the mating interfaces of the containment isolator docking system and engage the docking connection between the container and the isolator port.
usage.description: Combines aligning mating interfaces prior to engagement (67) with engaging the docking connection (59). Alignment is an immediate precursor to engagement and the two form one operational action at Level-1.

usage.name: Disengage docking connection
usage.statement: Disengage the docking connection while retaining the containment seal on each side.
usage.description: Undocking the container while retaining the split-barrier seal keeps both sides contained.

usage.name: Document transfer operation
usage.statement: Document the details of each docked transfer operation for traceability.
usage.description: Record transfer event data including time, materials, and operator details to satisfy traceability requirements.

usage.name: Equalize pressure across docked transfer interface
usage.statement: Equalize pressure across the docked transfer interface prior to barrier opening.
usage.description: Balance pressure differentials at the docking interface to prevent contamination or material dispersion when the barrier is opened.

usage.name: Handle high-volume docking cycles
usage.statement: Handle repeated high-volume docking cycles during intensive operation.
usage.description: Sustain system performance and containment integrity across a high number of consecutive docking cycles.

usage.name: Manage transfer rate through docking interface
usage.statement: Manage the rate at which material passes through the docking interface during transfer.
usage.description: Control or adjust transfer speed or flow to maintain containment integrity and meet process requirements.

usage.name: Manipulate contents via gloves
usage.statement: Manipulate the docked contents through the isolator glove ports.
usage.description: Handling items inside the isolator through glove ports is required for hands-on tasks during transfer.

usage.name: Monitor containment pressure
usage.statement: Monitor the containment pressure differential during the docked operation.
usage.description: Watching pressure differentials during use ensures the containment boundary is not breached.

usage.name: Operate docking system controls
usage.statement: Operate the docking system controls to sequence each stage of the transfer cycle.
usage.description: Use the control interface to initiate, advance, pause, or halt the docking cycle stages in the correct operational sequence.

usage.name: Transfer material through docking interface
usage.statement: Transfer material through the docked interface between the two enclosures.
usage.description: Move material from one enclosure to the other once the docking connection is established and barriers are open.

usage.name: Verify barrier position
usage.statement: Verify the indicated position of each docking barrier during operation.
usage.description: Confirm that each containment barrier is in its correct indicated position (open or closed) at each stage of the docking cycle.

usage.name: Verify seal integrity
usage.statement: Verify the integrity of the seal formed at the docking connection before opening the transfer barrier.
usage.description: Confirm that the docking seal meets containment requirements prior to proceeding with barrier opening.

acquisition.name: Arrange specialized delivery
acquisition.statement: Arrange delivery suitable for the docking system's dimensions, weight, handling limits, and destination access.
acquisition.description: This reduces the risk of transport damage, unsafe handling, or failed delivery for specialized equipment.

acquisition.name: Assess installation constraints
acquisition.statement: Assess the candidate docking system against the intended installation site constraints.
acquisition.description: Evaluate physical, structural, utility, and access constraints at the installation site to confirm the candidate system can be accommodated.

acquisition.name: Compare supplier proposals and references
acquisition.statement: Compare formal supplier proposals for the containment isolator docking system and seek references from existing users to support supplier selection.
acquisition.description: Combines comparing commercial proposals across candidate suppliers (11) with seeking user references and recommendations (3). Both jobs occur at the same decision point in supplier selection.

acquisition.name: Confirm regulatory compliance
acquisition.statement: Confirm the docking system meets applicable regulatory and industry standards.
acquisition.description: Regulated environments require documented compliance with GMP, ISO, or pharmacopeia standards prior to procurement.

acquisition.name: Define system requirements
acquisition.statement: Define the required specifications for the containment isolator docking system prior to procurement.
acquisition.description: Establish the technical, regulatory, and operational requirements that the docking system must satisfy, forming the basis for supplier solicitation.

acquisition.name: Evaluate and compare docking system options
acquisition.statement: Evaluate available containment isolator docking system technologies, configurations, and technical documentation against containment performance, isolator compatibility, and facility exposure limits.
acquisition.description: Covers researching available options (18), reviewing technical documentation (10), comparing containment performance (20), verifying isolator/transfer-port compatibility (19), and comparing systems across containment performance, port size, and specifications (1). Consolidated into one pre-selection evaluation job.
acquisition.userGroup: Process engineer, procurement specialist, EHS/containment engineer

acquisition.name: Identify eligible suppliers
acquisition.statement: Identify suppliers capable of providing the specified containment isolator docking system.
acquisition.description: This creates a viable supplier pool for a specialized item requiring relevant technical capability.

acquisition.name: Navigate procurement process
acquisition.statement: Navigate the internal procurement and approval process for the docking system.
acquisition.description: Enterprise procurement of capital equipment must move through defined approval and purchasing workflows.

acquisition.name: Obtain qualification documentation
acquisition.statement: Obtain the certification, qualification, and regulatory documentation supplied with the docking system.
acquisition.description: Collect factory test records, material certificates, declarations of conformity, and other qualification documents delivered with or shortly after the system.

acquisition.name: Place purchase order and complete transaction
acquisition.statement: Place the approved purchase order for the selected docking system configuration and complete the associated purchase transaction.
acquisition.description: Combines placing the formal purchase order (13) with completing the purchase transaction (6). These are sequential sub-steps of the same commercial closing event and are indistinct at Level-1.

acquisition.name: Qualify selected supplier
acquisition.statement: Qualify the selected docking system supplier under the applicable procurement controls.
acquisition.description: This verifies that the supplier can consistently meet technical, quality, documentation, delivery, and support obligations.

acquisition.name: Register acquired system
acquisition.statement: Register the acquired docking system with the supplier when registration is required.
acquisition.description: This establishes the equipment record needed for warranty coverage, technical support, service notifications, or controlled document access.

acquisition.name: Track system shipment
acquisition.statement: Track the docking system shipment through delivery completion.
acquisition.description: This enables coordination of receiving personnel, unloading equipment, controlled access, and schedule changes.

acquisition.name: Unbox, inspect, and receive docking system
acquisition.statement: Unbox and inspect the containment isolator docking system components for completeness and transit damage, and verify receipt against the purchase order and shipping documentation.
acquisition.description: Combines unboxing and inspecting components on arrival (32) with receiving and verifying the order against purchase and shipping documents (21). These occur at the same physical receiving event.

acquisition.name: Witness factory acceptance testing
acquisition.statement: Witness factory acceptance testing for the purchased docking system.
acquisition.description: This provides pre-shipment evidence that the manufactured system matches the approved configuration and defined acceptance criteria.

maintenance.name: Back up docking system configuration data
maintenance.statement: Back up the configuration and calibration data stored in the docking system controller.
maintenance.description: Export and securely store configuration parameters, calibration records, and operational settings from the docking system controller to enable recovery after a hardware failure or software update.

maintenance.name: Calibrate monitoring sensors
maintenance.statement: Calibrate the pressure and seal-monitoring sensors on the containment isolator docking system.
maintenance.description: Restores accurate readings on pressure, alignment, and seal-force indicators used to confirm a valid dock.

maintenance.name: Document maintenance history
maintenance.statement: Document the maintenance and service history of the containment isolator docking system.
maintenance.description: Records inspections, seal replacements, and service events to support compliance and traceability requirements.

maintenance.name: Lubricate moving mechanisms
maintenance.statement: Lubricate the moving clamp and collar mechanisms of the containment isolator docking system.
maintenance.description: Keeps the moving latch, clamp, and rotating collar mechanisms operating smoothly to ensure reliable sealing.

maintenance.name: Protect idle ports
maintenance.statement: Cover and protect the docking ports when the containment isolator docking system is not in use.
maintenance.description: Protects idle ports from contamination and mechanical damage while the docking system is not in use.

maintenance.name: Realign docking guides
maintenance.statement: Adjust and realign the docking alignment guides on the containment isolator docking system.
maintenance.description: Confirms the alignment guides hold the two halves in correct registration to ensure repeatable safe docking.

maintenance.name: Repair damaged components
maintenance.statement: Repair or replace damaged docking components on the containment isolator docking system.
maintenance.description: Removes and replaces worn or damaged docking components to return the system to service.

maintenance.name: Replace port seals
maintenance.statement: Replace the seals and gaskets on the docking ports of the containment isolator docking system.
maintenance.description: Remove worn or degraded seals and gaskets from docking ports and install compatible replacements to restore leak-tight containment at the docking interface.

maintenance.name: Schedule preventive maintenance
maintenance.statement: Schedule preventive maintenance for each docking system.
maintenance.description: Plan and schedule recurring preventive maintenance activities for each containment isolator docking system unit to ensure continued performance and compliance.

maintenance.name: Test dock seal integrity
maintenance.statement: Test the seal integrity of the docked connection on the containment isolator docking system.
maintenance.description: Confirms the mating flanges and locking mechanism achieve and hold a leak-tight connection when docked.

maintenance.name: Update control software
maintenance.statement: Update the control software and firmware of the containment isolator docking system.
maintenance.description: Keeps embedded control software and firmware current to maintain compatibility and correct known issues.

maintenance.name: Validate communication interface with connected isolator
maintenance.statement: Validate the communication interface between the docking system and the connected isolator unit.
maintenance.description: Test the electrical and data communication paths between the docking system controller and the mated isolator to confirm signals, interlocks, and status data are transmitted correctly.

maintenance.name: Verify interlock function
maintenance.statement: Verify the operation of the safety interlock and locking mechanism on the containment isolator docking system.
maintenance.description: Verifies the interlock and locking mechanism engages and disengages correctly to prevent unsafe undocking.

maintenance.name: Verify safety interlock override controls
maintenance.statement: Verify that manual override controls for docking safety interlocks function correctly and return to normal operation.
maintenance.description: Test manual override mechanisms on docking safety interlocks to confirm they engage and disengage correctly and that the system returns to its default safety state after override release.

preparation.name: Calibrate docking sensors against approved references
preparation.statement: Calibrate docking system sensors against approved reference standards prior to operation.
preparation.description: The calibration technician verifies and adjusts docking sensors — such as position, pressure, or seal-engagement sensors — against traceable reference standards to ensure accurate system feedback and safe operation.

preparation.name: Decontaminate product-contact surfaces before first use
preparation.statement: Decontaminate the docking system's product-contact and transfer-path surfaces before initial operation.
preparation.description: The operator or technician applies an approved decontamination procedure to all surfaces that will contact product or be exposed during transfer operations, ensuring the system is free from contamination prior to first use.
preparation.frequency: One-time (pre-first-use); periodic thereafter per maintenance schedule

preparation.name: Install interface seals at docking interface
preparation.statement: Install approved seals at the docking interface prior to system commissioning.
preparation.description: The technician fits and seats approved seal components at all docking interface points, confirming correct installation before the system is leak-tested and commissioned.

preparation.name: Record commissioning baseline configuration
preparation.statement: Record the approved commissioning baseline configuration of the docking system for reference and regulatory documentation.
preparation.description: The validation specialist documents all verified settings, test results, and as-built configuration data at the point of commissioning approval, establishing a baseline record for ongoing compliance, change control, and audit purposes.

disposal.name: Cancel associated service contracts
disposal.statement: Cancel service, calibration, and maintenance contracts associated with the decommissioned docking system.
disposal.description: Formally terminate all active third-party service agreements, calibration schedules, and maintenance contracts tied to the retired docking system to avoid continued charges and obligations.

disposal.name: Decommission the docking system
disposal.statement: Decommission the containment isolator docking system at end of service life.
disposal.description: Execute formal end-of-service decommissioning procedures for the docking system, including all required sign-offs and regulatory steps.

disposal.name: Discard consumable seals
disposal.statement: Discard the docking system's spent single-use seals and rings.
disposal.description: Single-use alpha rings, bags, or elastomer seals must be discarded according to their material class.

disposal.name: Dismantle the docking system
disposal.statement: Dismantle the containment isolator docking system into removable sub-assemblies.
disposal.description: Systematically disassemble the docking system following approved procedures to reduce it to transportable components for waste segregation, recycling, or disposal.

disposal.name: Dispose of hazardous components
disposal.statement: Dispose of the docking system's hazardous and contaminated components through an authorized waste stream.
disposal.description: Route removed hazardous components — including contaminated materials and regulated waste — through applicable authorised disposal channels.

disposal.name: Document and archive decommissioning records
disposal.statement: Document the docking system's final condition at decommissioning including decontamination status and disassembly configuration, and archive all decommissioning, decontamination, and disposal documentation.
disposal.description: Combines documenting the final state at decommissioning (115) with archiving decommissioning and disposal records (114). Documentation and archiving are performed as part of the same records-closure activity.
disposal.frequency: Once at end of service life

disposal.name: Notify stakeholders of retirement
disposal.statement: Notify relevant stakeholders that the docking system has been retired.
disposal.description: Manufacturers, safety officers, and quality teams need notification when a containment asset is retired.

disposal.name: Recycle recoverable materials
disposal.statement: Recycle the docking system's recoverable metal and polymer components.
disposal.description: Identify and route recoverable materials — metals, polymers, and other recyclable parts — from the decommissioned docking system to appropriate recycling channels.

disposal.name: Remove from asset register
disposal.statement: Remove the retired docking system from the asset and maintenance registry.
disposal.description: Retired units must be struck from asset registers, calibration schedules, and maintenance systems.

disposal.name: Remove residual process materials
disposal.statement: Remove residual process materials from the docking system prior to decontamination.
disposal.description: Clear all residual powders, liquids, or other process materials retained within the docking system's cavities, ports, and transfer zones before decontamination steps begin.

disposal.name: Return under warranty
disposal.statement: Return the docking system to the manufacturer under warranty or recall.
disposal.description: Units with defects covered under warranty or subject to recall may be returned to the manufacturer.

disposal.name: Transfer ownership of the docking system
disposal.statement: Transfer ownership of the docking system and its associated records to a new owner or facility.
disposal.description: Execute the formal ownership transfer of the docking system, including documentation of asset history and regulatory status, to the receiving party.

disposal.name: Transition to replacement system
disposal.statement: Transition the containment line from the decommissioned docking system to its replacement unit.
disposal.description: Plan and execute the operational handover from the retired docking system to the replacement, ensuring continuity of containment processes.

disposal.name: Verify disposal clearance
disposal.statement: Verify the docking system's release status against applicable disposal criteria.
disposal.description: This confirms whether the equipment may be handled as reusable equipment, recyclable material, controlled waste, or hazardous waste.

usage.description: Combines aligning mating interfaces prior to engagement (67) with engaging the docking connection (59). Alignment is an immediate precursor to engagement and the two form one operational action at Level-1.
