disposal.statement: Transition the containment line from the decommissioned docking system to its replacement unit.
disposal.description: Plan and execute the operational handover from the retired docking system to the replacement, ensuring continuity of containment processes.

disposal.name: Verify disposal clearance
disposal.statement: Verify the docking system's release status against applicable disposal criteria.
disposal.description: This confirms whether the equipment may be handled as reusable equipment, recyclable material, controlled waste, or hazardous waste.

preparation.name: Align docking mechanism
preparation.statement: Align and adjust the port's docking and locking mechanism to the mating component.
preparation.description: Aligning the docking mechanism ensures the mating port or container connects reliably without breach.

preparation.name: Assemble port components
preparation.statement: Assemble the transfer port components in the required configuration prior to installation.
preparation.description: Some transfer port designs are supplied in sub-assemblies that must be combined before the unit is installed at the process boundary.

preparation.name: Clean and sanitize port contact surfaces
preparation.statement: Clean and sanitize the port's transfer-contact surfaces to the required readiness condition prior to first use.
preparation.description: Removing particulates and biological burden from contact surfaces ensures the port is ready for contained transfer without cross-contamination.

preparation.name: Conduct interface compatibility trial
preparation.statement: Conduct a representative trial of the candidate port with mating components before final selection.
preparation.description: Test or process engineer performs a physical fit and function trial of the candidate port against actual mating equipment to validate interface compatibility prior to committing to a purchase.

preparation.name: Connect utilities and power
preparation.statement: Connect the port to required power, pressure, or control utilities.
preparation.description: Where powered or automated ports exist, connecting utilities is needed before operation.

preparation.name: Install seals and gaskets
preparation.statement: Install the sealing rings, gaskets, and interlock components on the port.
preparation.description: Correct gasket and sealing element placement is essential to maintain containment integrity.

preparation.name: Mount port to enclosure
preparation.statement: Mount the transfer port into the opening of the containment enclosure.
preparation.description: Physically fitting the port into the isolator or enclosure wall is required before it can function.

acquisition.name: Approve Port Supplier
acquisition.statement: Approve the proposed port supplier through the applicable procurement process.
acquisition.description: This verifies that the supplier satisfies required commercial, quality, traceability, support, and continuity controls.

acquisition.name: Arrange controlled delivery conditions
acquisition.statement: Arrange delivery under the specified handling and transport conditions for the port.
acquisition.description: Logistics coordinator specifies and arranges transport conditions (e.g., orientation, temperature, vibration limits) required to protect the port during shipment.

acquisition.name: Assess decontamination process compatibility
acquisition.statement: Assess the port's compatibility with the specified cleaning, sterilization, or decontamination process.
acquisition.description: Validation or process engineer determines whether the port materials, seals, and construction can withstand the cleaning or decontamination regime that will be applied in the intended environment.

acquisition.name: Confirm regulatory and certification compliance
acquisition.statement: Confirm the port meets applicable regulatory, cleanroom, and material-certification standards for the intended use.
acquisition.description: Quality or compliance specialist verifies that candidate ports carry the necessary regulatory approvals and certifications before approving them for procurement.

acquisition.name: Establish supply agreement
acquisition.statement: Establish a supply or framework agreement to ensure ongoing availability of matched port components.
acquisition.description: For phased or ongoing production, buyers negotiate framework agreements to secure consistent supply of matched port halves.

acquisition.name: Evaluate and compare candidate port solutions
acquisition.statement: Evaluate candidate contained material transfer port solutions against selection criteria, containment performance requirements, and supplier references.
acquisition.description: Covers comparing supplier offerings on features, materials, and validation documentation; seeking reference installations; evaluating stated containment performance and operator exposure ratings; and comparing eligible port configurations against approved selection criteria. Collapses four overlapping pre-selection evaluation jobs into one canonical entry.
acquisition.userGroup: Procurement engineers, process engineers, EHS/containment specialists
acquisition.frequency: Once per new port selection event or when re-qualifying suppliers

acquisition.name: Obtain supplier quotation
acquisition.statement: Obtain a quotation for the selected port configuration.
acquisition.description: Procurement specialist requests formal pricing, lead time, and commercial terms from the chosen supplier for the specified port configuration.

acquisition.name: Place port purchase order
acquisition.statement: Place an order for the approved contained material transfer port configuration.
acquisition.description: Purchaser or procurement specialist issues a formal purchase order for the selected and approved port configuration to the chosen supplier.

acquisition.name: Research transfer port technologies
acquisition.statement: Research the available contained material transfer port technologies and their containment mechanisms.
acquisition.description: Buyer or specifier investigates the range of transfer port types, operating principles, and containment approaches available on the market before defining requirements.

acquisition.name: Review Qualification Evidence
acquisition.statement: Review the supplier-provided qualification evidence for the contained material transfer port.
acquisition.description: This establishes whether test reports, certificates, drawings, declarations, and validation support satisfy governance requirements.

acquisition.name: Verify equipment compatibility
acquisition.statement: Verify that the transfer port is compatible with the existing enclosure, vessel, and mating port interfaces.
acquisition.description: Ports must physically and functionally mate with existing isolators, glove boxes, drums, or process vessels, so compatibility drives every purchase.

acquisition.name: Verify received port traceability documentation
acquisition.statement: Verify the received port's traceability package against the purchase order requirements.
acquisition.description: Quality specialist checks that the traceability documents accompanying the delivered port (e.g., certificates of conformance, batch records) match what was specified in the purchase order.

disposal.name: Archive lifecycle records
disposal.statement: Archive the qualification, validation, and decommissioning records for the retired contained material transfer port.
disposal.description: Consolidate and store all lifecycle documentation — including qualification, validation, and decommissioning records — in a retrievable archive.

disposal.name: Cancel assigned services
disposal.statement: Cancel service coverage assigned to the retired contained material transfer port.
disposal.description: This prevents continued charges or scheduled support after the port leaves service.

disposal.name: Decommission the transfer port
disposal.statement: Decommission the contained material transfer port from the process system.
disposal.description: Formally remove the transfer port from active service within the process system, completing all required decommissioning steps.

disposal.name: Decontaminate the transfer port
disposal.statement: Decontaminate the contained material transfer port surfaces after removal from service.
disposal.description: Apply approved decontamination procedures to all internal and external surfaces of the transfer port to render it safe for handling, disposal, or transfer.

disposal.name: Dispose of regulated waste components
disposal.statement: Dispose of contaminated contained material transfer port components through the applicable regulated waste stream.
disposal.description: Route components that cannot be decontaminated or recycled through the correct regulated waste disposal channel in compliance with applicable requirements.

disposal.name: Document final condition
disposal.statement: Document the final condition and disposition of the contained material transfer port.
disposal.description: Record the physical state, any defects, contamination status, and final disposition decision for the transfer port at end of life.

disposal.name: Notify affected stakeholders of removal
disposal.statement: Notify affected stakeholders of the contained material transfer port's removal from service.
disposal.description: Communicate the retirement and removal of the transfer port to all parties whose processes, safety, or compliance are affected.

disposal.name: Purge residual material
disposal.statement: Purge residual contained material from the transfer port before disposal.
disposal.description: Matters before removal because residual contained material inside the port could pose contamination or exposure risk.

disposal.name: Recycle recoverable components
disposal.statement: Separate and recycle the recoverable components of the transfer port.
disposal.description: Matters when port components are made of recoverable materials such as stainless steel or elastomers.

disposal.name: Recycle uncontaminated materials
disposal.statement: Route uncontaminated contained material transfer port materials into an approved recycling stream.
disposal.description: Identify and divert clean, recyclable materials from the retired transfer port into the appropriate recycling channel, diverting them from landfill.

disposal.name: Return defective port
disposal.statement: Return the defective transfer port to the supplier under warranty or recall.
disposal.description: Matters when a defective port is returned under warranty or a manufacturer recall.

disposal.name: Seal the vacated interface
disposal.statement: Seal the enclosure interface left by the removed contained material transfer port.
disposal.description: Fit an approved blanking plate, cap, or seal to the opening left in the enclosure after the transfer port has been removed, maintaining containment integrity.

disposal.name: Segregate contaminated components
disposal.statement: Segregate contaminated contained material transfer port components from clean components prior to disposal.
disposal.description: Physically separate components that have contacted process materials from uncontaminated components to enable compliant disposal routing.

disposal.name: Transfer port ownership
disposal.statement: Transfer ownership of the serviceable contained material transfer port to another user or site.
disposal.description: Execute the legal and administrative steps required to transfer ownership of a retired or surplus transfer port to a new recipient.

disposal.name: Transition process to replacement port
disposal.statement: Transition the process from the retired contained material transfer port to its replacement.
disposal.description: Manage the changeover of process operations from the decommissioned transfer port to its successor, ensuring continuity and compliance.

disposal.name: Verify decontamination status
disposal.statement: Verify the decontamination status of the contained material transfer port before release from service.
disposal.description: Confirm through inspection or testing that the transfer port meets the required decontamination standard before it is released for disposal, transfer, or recycling.

maintenance.name: Back up port configuration data
maintenance.statement: Back up configuration and calibration data stored in electronically controlled transfer port units.
maintenance.description: Export and securely store parameter sets, calibration records, and interlock configuration data from ports with embedded electronic controls, enabling recovery after component replacement or firmware failure.

maintenance.name: Calibrate interlock and alignment mechanisms
maintenance.statement: Calibrate and verify the port's interlock and alignment mechanisms to confirm they operate within specification.
maintenance.description: Perform dimensional and functional calibration of locking, alignment, and engagement mechanisms to ensure correct mating and interlock operation during transfers.

maintenance.name: Calibrate port instrumentation
maintenance.statement: Calibrate instrumentation integrated into the transfer port.
maintenance.description: This maintains the accuracy of equipped pressure, position, or status-monitoring devices.

maintenance.name: Calibrate transfer port monitoring devices
maintenance.statement: Calibrate the transfer port's monitoring devices against applicable reference standards before first use.
maintenance.description: Calibrating sensors and gauges integrated into the port ensures measurement accuracy and compliance with process and regulatory requirements.

maintenance.name: Decontaminate transfer port contact surfaces
maintenance.statement: Decontaminate the port's contact surfaces to neutralise hazardous or biological residue following material transfer.
maintenance.description: Apply a validated decontamination procedure to all product-contact surfaces of the port to eliminate residual hazardous, toxic, or biological material and restore safe handling status.

maintenance.name: Document maintenance history
maintenance.statement: Record all cleaning, inspection, and repair activities in the port's maintenance log.
maintenance.description: Regulated and validated processes require an auditable record of every maintenance action on containment equipment.

maintenance.name: Inspect coupling mechanisms
maintenance.statement: Inspect the port's coupling mechanism for wear that could affect engagement.
maintenance.description: This detects deterioration in the components that connect mating port sections.

maintenance.name: Lubricate moving components
maintenance.statement: Apply approved lubricant to the port's moving latch and rotation components.
maintenance.description: Moving latch and rotation surfaces need lubrication to operate smoothly without shedding particles.

maintenance.name: Monitor port operational status
maintenance.statement: Monitor the port's seal, interlock, and pressure status for signs of degradation or impending failure.
maintenance.description: Continuously observe available indicators of port condition — including seal state, interlock engagement, and differential pressure — to detect deterioration before a containment event occurs.

maintenance.name: Protect idle port openings
maintenance.statement: Protect exposed port openings while the transfer port is not in use.
maintenance.description: Fit dust caps, blanking plates, or protective covers to open port interfaces when the port is not engaged in a transfer, preventing ingress of contamination and damage to sealing surfaces.

maintenance.name: Repair damaged port mechanisms
maintenance.statement: Repair or replace damaged latches, rings, or actuators to restore specified port function.
maintenance.description: Diagnose and remedy mechanical failures in port components such as latches, actuators, or coupling rings that prevent the port from achieving its specified engagement or containment function.

maintenance.name: Replace consumable transfer components
maintenance.statement: Replace disposable liners, sleeves, or single-use transfer components between uses.
maintenance.description: Swap out single-use or limited-life consumable elements within the port assembly to maintain specified hygiene and containment performance between transfers.

maintenance.name: Replenish port consumable stock
maintenance.statement: Replenish the inventory of port seals, liners, and single-use transfer consumables to maintain operational readiness.
maintenance.description: Track and reorder consumable port components — including seals, gaskets, and single-use liners — before stock falls below the level needed to sustain scheduled maintenance and transfer operations.

maintenance.name: Schedule preventive maintenance
maintenance.statement: Schedule and perform preventive maintenance on the transfer port assembly.
maintenance.description: Scheduled service prevents unplanned failures that would breach containment during operation.

maintenance.name: Store port properly
maintenance.statement: Store idle port components in protective covers to preserve sealing surfaces.
maintenance.description: Proper storage protects sealing surfaces from contamination and damage when the port is idle or in reserve.

maintenance.name: Tighten mounting hardware
maintenance.statement: Tighten port mounting hardware to its specified condition.
maintenance.description: This prevents movement that could compromise alignment or sealing performance.

maintenance.name: Update control firmware
maintenance.statement: Update the firmware of an electronically controlled transfer port.
maintenance.description: This maintains supported functionality in ports equipped with electronic controls.

maintenance.name: Verify closure operation
maintenance.statement: Verify that the port's closure mechanism completes its specified motion.
maintenance.description: This confirms that the port can return to its contained state after use.

maintenance.name: Verify safety interlocks
maintenance.statement: Verify the operation of each safety interlock fitted to the port.
maintenance.description: This ensures equipped ports prevent unintended opening or incorrect transfer sequences.

usage.name: Clear obstruction from port passage
usage.statement: Clear a blockage or obstruction from the port passage to restore unobstructed material flow during operation.
usage.description: Removing material clogs, bridging, or foreign objects from inside the port passage while maintaining containment, to allow transfer to continue.

usage.name: Close transfer passage
usage.statement: Close the port passage after material transfer is complete.
usage.description: Actuating the internal door, valve, or disc of the port assembly to fully close the transfer pathway before undocking, restoring containment integrity on both sides.

usage.name: Coordinate transfer between operators
usage.statement: Coordinate the transfer with an operator on the opposite side of the port.
usage.description: Two-sided transfers require synchronized action to open, pass, and close the port correctly.

usage.name: Detach transfer container from port
usage.statement: Detach and remove the transfer container from the port interface after undocking is complete.
usage.description: Safely releasing the bag, drum, liner, or transfer vessel from the port face once the halves have been separated, ensuring the closed container is properly handled and containment is maintained.

usage.name: Dock transfer port halves
usage.statement: Align the alpha and beta port interfaces and dock the two mating halves of the contained transfer port to establish a sealed connection.
usage.description: Combines interface alignment (#60) and docking to achieve a sealed connection (#59). Alignment is a sub-step of the docking action and the two entries describe a single continuous operation. The merged job retains the mechanical detail of both.
usage.userGroup: Process operators, material handlers, production technicians

usage.name: Document transfer event
usage.statement: Record the details of each transfer performed through the port.
usage.description: Documenting transfers during use supports traceability and compliance in regulated environments.

usage.name: Equalize pressure across port
usage.statement: Equalize the pressure differential across the connected port before opening the transfer passage.
usage.description: Balancing the pressure on both sides of the port to prevent pressure-driven material ejection, contamination ingress, or seal stress when the passage is opened.

usage.name: Handle liquids through port
usage.statement: Pass liquid containers or fluids through the transfer port while maintaining the seal.
usage.description: Liquid transfers demand controlled passage to avoid spills that compromise containment.

usage.name: Handle solids through port
usage.statement: Pass powders or solid components through the transfer port without loss of containment.
usage.description: Solids require handling that keeps dust and particulates contained during passage through the port.

usage.name: Interrupt Material Transfer
usage.statement: Stop material movement through the port during an active transfer.
usage.description: This is required when operating conditions become abnormal or the target quantity is reached.

usage.name: Isolate Compromised Port
usage.statement: Isolate the port after detecting a containment failure.
usage.description: This limits continued material release when the transfer boundary becomes compromised.

usage.name: Monitor containment integrity
usage.statement: Monitor the containment boundary of the port during material transfer for signs of breach or leakage.
usage.description: Continuously observing seals, interfaces, and surrounding surfaces for visual, audible, or instrument-indicated signs of containment failure during an active transfer.

usage.name: Monitor transfer progress
usage.statement: Monitor the movement of material through the port during an active transfer.
usage.description: Observing the flow, volume, and condition of material passing through the open port to detect blockages, incomplete transfer, or deviations from expected transfer behaviour.

usage.name: Move material through port
usage.statement: Move material through the open transfer port from one contained environment to another.
usage.description: Physically transferring powder, liquid, components, or other materials through the open port passage while maintaining containment on both sides.

usage.name: Open transfer passage
usage.statement: Open the port passage to allow material to move through the sealed transfer port.
usage.description: Actuating the door, valve, or disc mechanism within the docked port assembly to create an unobstructed pathway for material transfer between the two connected environments.

usage.name: Operate port via remote interface
usage.statement: Operate the transfer port through a remote control interface when direct manual access is not available.
usage.description: Using remote actuation, control panels, or automated systems to dock, open, close, and undock the port from a location separate from the port itself.

usage.name: Regulate material flow rate
usage.statement: Regulate the rate of material flow through the port passage during an active transfer.
usage.description: Adjusting the port opening position or associated flow controls to modulate how quickly material passes through, to avoid spillage, overloading downstream processes, or loss of containment.

usage.name: Select compatible container size
usage.statement: Select or confirm the container or component size that is compatible with the port opening before initiating transfer.
usage.description: Determining whether the item to be transferred fits within the port's dimensional constraints, and choosing appropriately sized containers or adapters where the port supports multiple sizes.

usage.name: Test installed port containment integrity
usage.statement: Test the installed transfer port for containment integrity and leak-tightness to the required specification before first use.
usage.description: Combines leak/integrity testing (#31) and containment integrity testing to specification (#44). Both describe the same pre-use pressure/leak test on the installed port. Qualification documentation is separately captured in the training and validation workflows.
usage.userGroup: Installation engineers, validation engineers, quality assurance
usage.frequency: Once after installation; repeated after seal replacement or any maintenance that breaks containment

usage.name: Transfer under isolator conditions
usage.statement: Operate the transfer port while maintaining the pressure and atmosphere of a connected isolator.
usage.description: Preserving isolator pressure differentials during transfer is critical for sterile or hazardous containment work.

usage.name: Verify port connection status
usage.statement: Verify that the port connection has achieved the correct locked and sealed status before proceeding with transfer.
usage.description: Checking available indicators, interlocks, or visual cues on the transfer port to confirm the connection is fully engaged and safe to open for material passage.

preparation.name: Align docking mechanism
preparation.statement: Align and adjust the port's docking and locking mechanism to the mating component.
preparation.description: Aligning the docking mechanism ensures the mating port or container connects reliably without breach.

preparation.name: Assemble port components
preparation.statement: Assemble the transfer port components in the required configuration prior to installation.
preparation.description: Some transfer port designs are supplied in sub-assemblies that must be combined before the unit is installed at the process boundary.

preparation.name: Clean and sanitize port contact surfaces
preparation.statement: Clean and sanitize the port's transfer-contact surfaces to the required readiness condition prior to first use.
preparation.description: Removing particulates and biological burden from contact surfaces ensures the port is ready for contained transfer without cross-contamination.

preparation.name: Conduct interface compatibility trial
preparation.statement: Conduct a representative trial of the candidate port with mating components before final selection.
preparation.description: Test or process engineer performs a physical fit and function trial of the candidate port against actual mating equipment to validate interface compatibility prior to committing to a purchase.

preparation.name: Connect utilities and power
preparation.statement: Connect the port to required power, pressure, or control utilities.
preparation.description: Where powered or automated ports exist, connecting utilities is needed before operation.

preparation.name: Install seals and gaskets
preparation.statement: Install the sealing rings, gaskets, and interlock components on the port.
preparation.description: Correct gasket and sealing element placement is essential to maintain containment integrity.

preparation.name: Mount port to enclosure
preparation.statement: Mount the transfer port into the opening of the containment enclosure.
preparation.description: Physically fitting the port into the isolator or enclosure wall is required before it can function.

acquisition.description: This verifies that the supplier satisfies required commercial, quality, traceability, support, and continuity controls.

disposal.description: Consolidate and store all lifecycle documentation — including qualification, validation, and decommissioning records — in a retrievable archive.

disposal.description: Confirm through inspection or testing that the transfer port meets the required decontamination standard before it is released for disposal, transfer, or recycling.

maintenance.name: Replace consumable transfer components
maintenance.statement: Replace disposable liners, sleeves, or single-use transfer components between uses.
maintenance.description: Swap out single-use or limited-life consumable elements within the port assembly to maintain specified hygiene and containment performance between transfers.

maintenance.name: Replenish port consumable stock
maintenance.statement: Replenish the inventory of port seals, liners, and single-use transfer consumables to maintain operational readiness.
maintenance.description: Track and reorder consumable port components — including seals, gaskets, and single-use liners — before stock falls below the level needed to sustain scheduled maintenance and transfer operations.

maintenance.name: Schedule preventive maintenance
maintenance.statement: Schedule and perform preventive maintenance on the transfer port assembly.
maintenance.description: Scheduled service prevents unplanned failures that would breach containment during operation.

maintenance.name: Store port properly
maintenance.statement: Store idle port components in protective covers to preserve sealing surfaces.
maintenance.description: Proper storage protects sealing surfaces from contamination and damage when the port is idle or in reserve.

maintenance.name: Tighten mounting hardware
maintenance.statement: Tighten port mounting hardware to its specified condition.
maintenance.description: This prevents movement that could compromise alignment or sealing performance.

maintenance.name: Update control firmware
maintenance.statement: Update the firmware of an electronically controlled transfer port.
maintenance.description: This maintains supported functionality in ports equipped with electronic controls.

maintenance.name: Verify closure operation
maintenance.statement: Verify that the port's closure mechanism completes its specified motion.
maintenance.description: This confirms that the port can return to its contained state after use.

maintenance.name: Verify safety interlocks
maintenance.statement: Verify the operation of each safety interlock fitted to the port.
maintenance.description: This ensures equipped ports prevent unintended opening or incorrect transfer sequences.

usage.name: Clear obstruction from port passage
usage.statement: Clear a blockage or obstruction from the port passage to restore unobstructed material flow during operation.
usage.description: Removing material clogs, bridging, or foreign objects from inside the port passage while maintaining containment, to allow transfer to continue.

usage.name: Close transfer passage
usage.statement: Close the port passage after material transfer is complete.
usage.description: Actuating the internal door, valve, or disc of the port assembly to fully close the transfer pathway before undocking, restoring containment integrity on both sides.

usage.name: Coordinate transfer between operators
usage.statement: Coordinate the transfer with an operator on the opposite side of the port.
usage.description: Two-sided transfers require synchronized action to open, pass, and close the port correctly.

usage.name: Detach transfer container from port
usage.statement: Detach and remove the transfer container from the port interface after undocking is complete.
usage.description: Safely releasing the bag, drum, liner, or transfer vessel from the port face once the halves have been separated, ensuring the closed container is properly handled and containment is maintained.

usage.name: Dock transfer port halves
usage.statement: Align the alpha and beta port interfaces and dock the two mating halves of the contained transfer port to establish a sealed connection.
usage.description: Combines interface alignment (#60) and docking to achieve a sealed connection (#59). Alignment is a sub-step of the docking action and the two entries describe a single continuous operation. The merged job retains the mechanical detail of both.
usage.userGroup: Process operators, material handlers, production technicians

usage.name: Document transfer event
usage.statement: Record the details of each transfer performed through the port.
usage.description: Documenting transfers during use supports traceability and compliance in regulated environments.

usage.name: Equalize pressure across port
usage.statement: Equalize the pressure differential across the connected port before opening the transfer passage.
usage.description: Balancing the pressure on both sides of the port to prevent pressure-driven material ejection, contamination ingress, or seal stress when the passage is opened.

usage.name: Handle liquids through port
usage.statement: Pass liquid containers or fluids through the transfer port while maintaining the seal.
usage.description: Liquid transfers demand controlled passage to avoid spills that compromise containment.

usage.name: Handle solids through port
usage.statement: Pass powders or solid components through the transfer port without loss of containment.
usage.description: Solids require handling that keeps dust and particulates contained during passage through the port.

usage.name: Interrupt Material Transfer
usage.statement: Stop material movement through the port during an active transfer.
usage.description: This is required when operating conditions become abnormal or the target quantity is reached.

usage.name: Isolate Compromised Port
usage.statement: Isolate the port after detecting a containment failure.
usage.description: This limits continued material release when the transfer boundary becomes compromised.

usage.name: Monitor containment integrity
usage.statement: Monitor the containment boundary of the port during material transfer for signs of breach or leakage.
usage.description: Continuously observing seals, interfaces, and surrounding surfaces for visual, audible, or instrument-indicated signs of containment failure during an active transfer.

usage.name: Monitor transfer progress
usage.statement: Monitor the movement of material through the port during an active transfer.
usage.description: Observing the flow, volume, and condition of material passing through the open port to detect blockages, incomplete transfer, or deviations from expected transfer behaviour.

usage.name: Move material through port
usage.statement: Move material through the open transfer port from one contained environment to another.
usage.description: Physically transferring powder, liquid, components, or other materials through the open port passage while maintaining containment on both sides.

usage.name: Open transfer passage
usage.statement: Open the port passage to allow material to move through the sealed transfer port.
usage.description: Actuating the door, valve, or disc mechanism within the docked port assembly to create an unobstructed pathway for material transfer between the two connected environments.

usage.name: Operate port via remote interface
usage.statement: Operate the transfer port through a remote control interface when direct manual access is not available.
usage.description: Using remote actuation, control panels, or automated systems to dock, open, close, and undock the port from a location separate from the port itself.

usage.name: Regulate material flow rate
usage.statement: Regulate the rate of material flow through the port passage during an active transfer.
usage.description: Adjusting the port opening position or associated flow controls to modulate how quickly material passes through, to avoid spillage, overloading downstream processes, or loss of containment.

usage.name: Select compatible container size
usage.statement: Select or confirm the container or component size that is compatible with the port opening before initiating transfer.
usage.description: Determining whether the item to be transferred fits within the port's dimensional constraints, and choosing appropriately sized containers or adapters where the port supports multiple sizes.

usage.name: Test installed port containment integrity
usage.statement: Test the installed transfer port for containment integrity and leak-tightness to the required specification before first use.
usage.description: Combines leak/integrity testing (#31) and containment integrity testing to specification (#44). Both describe the same pre-use pressure/leak test on the installed port. Qualification documentation is separately captured in the training and validation workflows.
usage.userGroup: Installation engineers, validation engineers, quality assurance
usage.frequency: Once after installation; repeated after seal replacement or any maintenance that breaks containment

usage.name: Transfer under isolator conditions
usage.statement: Operate the transfer port while maintaining the pressure and atmosphere of a connected isolator.
usage.description: Preserving isolator pressure differentials during transfer is critical for sterile or hazardous containment work.

usage.name: Verify port connection status
usage.statement: Verify that the port connection has achieved the correct locked and sealed status before proceeding with transfer.
usage.description: Checking available indicators, interlocks, or visual cues on the transfer port to confirm the connection is fully engaged and safe to open for material passage.
